  and fix. Out of scope: the full runs (Segment 7) and the regime verdict (Segment 8).

- **Segment 7 — Main experiment full runs (primary model).** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). The locked
  pre-registered grid was executed on Opus 4.5; ALL binding validity guards PASS on the full-run data;
  the result set is clean, reproducible, and analysis-ready. The §5 regime verdict was correctly deferred
  to Segment 8.**
  - **Outcome:** executed `results/preregistration.md` §4/§10 verbatim via `run_segment7.sh` →
    `results/seg7_*.jsonl` (16 files, **274k Opus calls**, marginal spend **$445.66** after Segment-5/6
    cache reuse; `--assert-cached` reproduces at $0; cumulative ≈ $1215 of $5k). Both levels
    (`sumprod_t3_d2` primary, `add_n15_d6` secondary); k∈{1,2,4,8}; reveal-after over the per-k n-grid +
    reveal-before at the operating point (each with n=0); position-balanced (`sweep_positions`); the
    fixed-item robustness arm. **ALL BINDING GUARDS (#1–3) PASS at every (level,k) operating cell** + the
    k=1 reference (`results/seg7_guards.md`): reveal-before positive control significantly positive
    everywhere; positions off-ceiling; genuine no-CoT (operand-based `genuine_violation` detector, per-size)
    ≤0.4% / reasoning 0% / truncation <0.2%. No VOID cell; the headline cell is valid. Data integrity
    triple-checked (data inspection + an `empirical-ml-data-inspector` pass + 2 `research-reviewer` passes;
    first-token-digit 100%, model answers the asked question, 0 scoring inconsistencies, 0
    robust-flips-to-wrong). Analysis-ready **descriptive** per-cell sanity summary in
    `results/main_run_summary.md` (+`main_run_cells.csv`/`main_run_contrast.csv`): per-(level,k,n)
    early-pool reveal-after `B_k` + Q1-only + aggregate + reveal-before + the contrast + n=0 per-position
    acc + the fresh/reused split, all cluster-bootstrap CIs (seed 0).
  - **The pilot-anticipated picture is CONFIRMED on the full run (descriptive; the §5 verdict is Segment 8):**
    headline cell **sumprod k=8 @ n=200 → reveal-after early-pool `B_k` = +2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7]** (excludes 0,
    the divided ref +13.7, and the parallel ref +35.5; Q1-only +1.2 [-1.1,+3.5]; aggregate −6.3 = the
    recency-erasure mixture, NOT the science), **directedness contrast +16.2pp [+14.3,+18.3]** with a sig.+
    reveal-before control +18.7 → the **NEAR-NULL / WEAK-POSITIVE PARTIAL + DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** picture
    ("directed ≫ undirected; undirected ≪ divided"). **d6 (secondary):** the directedness contrast is
    strongly positive at every k (k=8 **+31.9** [+30.0,+33.8], matching Segment 5) → cross-family
    generalization of the directedness finding; d6's *regime* `B_k` is muddy/negative (k=8 early-pool −16.8,
    a carry-disruption × recency-erasure mixture) exactly as pre-registered (only d6's CONTRAST is
    confirmatory). sumprod `B₁(n)` peaks +35.5 @150–200 tok then declines.
  - **Carry-forward for Segment 8** (honest limitations, all in `seg7_guards.md`/`main_run_summary.md`):
    (1) the **fresh-only firewall is weak** (~±3pp at k=8: only ~350 never-seen instances) — and the
    full-run **fresh-only `B_k` = +0.9 [-1.1,+2.9] sits BELOW the full +2.5 and its CI includes 0** (the
    pilot's fresh>full anomaly REVERSED) → the NULL-vs-PARTIAL call is firewall-/Q1-sensitive (both far
    below divided regardless). (2) **k=4 early-pool reveal-after is net-negative (−4.0), Q2-driven**
    (Q1-only +0.1) → report Q1-only + a position-sensitive flag; do NOT force a monotone-in-k trend.
    (3) **d6 k=8 reveal-before Q4 ceilings (95% @ n>0)** → the d6 positive control + contrast are
    headroom-limited → report d6 on the **logit** scale. (4) Guard #4: sumprod show-work ~0.6–0.9pp higher
    under reveal-before at k=4,8 (robust-scorer-handled) → report the contrast with/without those rows.

- **Segment 8 — Main analysis + regime determination.** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). The pre-registered
  §5 verdict is delivered.**
  - **Outcome:** applied `results/preregistration.md` §5 VERBATIM to the Segment-7 data (analysis-only;
    no new data) via `seg8_lib.py`/`analyze_seg8_verdict.py` → `results/seg8_verdict.md`,
    `seg8_regime_cells.csv`, `seg8_alpha_collapse.md`; 6 publication figures (`plots/seg8_*.png`), all
    inspected + GPT-vision-checked. **HEADLINE (§5.8, sumprod k=8 n=200):** the undirected reveal-after
    early-pool `B_k` is **NEAR-NULL** — **NULL** on the cleaner Q1-only (+1.2 [−1.1,+3.6]) and held-out
    fresh-only (+0.9 [−1.1,+2.9]) estimands, only statistically **PARTIAL (sub-divided)** on the pooled
    early-average (+2.5 [+1.2,+3.7]) — i.e. **~7% of B₁(n)** and **≪ the divided reference +13.7 (~10σ
    below, SE_R-inclusive)**; carries qualifiers _position-sensitive_ + _not robust to held-out_. The
    **robust, falsifiable result is the co-primary directedness contrast = DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE +16.2pp
    [+14.3,+18.3]** (sig.+ reveal-before control +18.7) → combined **"partial directed": directed ≫
    undirected, undirected ≪ divided** (the model uses filler when DIRECTED but, at the most-shared k=8,
    banks ~no UNDIRECTED computation). The **Bonferroni 98.3% confirmatory family of 3 all hold** (sumprod
    k=8 regime; sumprod k=8 contrast; **d6 k=8 contrast — paired Haldane-logit +1.50 [+1.39,+1.60]**, the
    cross-family generalization, since d6's additive +31.9 is headroom-/recency-inflated). **α NOT
    estimated** (positivity gate fails → ill-defined near the null, the pre-registered deviation);
    collapse plots show NO collapse on either *n* or *n/k* axis → neither parallel nor divided.
    **Retention gradient:** the less-shared cells retain MORE (k=2 early `B_k` +7.8 = 23% of B₁, robust;
    k=8 = 7%); per-k is NON-monotone (k=4 early-pool −4.0 is a Q2 recency-confound; Q1-only +0.1). The
    **+2.5 mechanism is UNRESOLVED within analysis scope** (fixed-item corr(B_k,B₁)≈0 is largely
    uninformative/attenuated; the k-gradient is at most weak/ambiguous on the artifact) — the
    attention-redistribution confound remains LIVE-but-disfavoured; the robust conclusions do NOT depend
    on it (a Segment-11 mechanism deep-dive).
  - **Interpretation (honest, NOT overclaimed):** on the **sharing axis the data ARGUE AGAINST** "the
    model does k questions' worth of parallel latent thinking" (parallel is *disfavored*, not merely
    unproven); the **directedness reading is suggestive, not proven** (doesn't-bank vs banks-but-can't-
    retrieve are not separated); the directedness contrast cancels attention-redistribution **only under a
    reveal-timing-invariance assumption**; parallel is **unreachable in-prompt** (even DIRECTED ~+15–20pp
    ≪ the lone +35.5 — context interference); the divided reference is interference-naive; the clean
    regime read rests primarily on **one family (sumprod)**. Validated by 5+ review rounds (LLM sanity +
    2× `research-reviewer` + `empirical-ml-data-inspector` + overall `research-reviewer` + focused
    re-review), all fixes applied, **verdict UNCHANGED**. See the "Segment 8" write-up section +
    `results/seg8_verdict.md`.

  *(Original planned scope, for the record:)* Apply the locked pre-registered
  `results/preregistration.md` §5 decision rules to the Segment-7 data (analysis-only; no new data).
  **PRIMARY (per the pre-registration):** the
  **3-way parallel/divided/null resolution via the position-resolved early-position reveal-after `B_k`**
  + the **reveal-before/after directedness contrast** (on additive AND logit scales), with the validity
  guards (reveal-before control significantly positive; positions off-ceiling). **SECONDARY (only where
  B_k is meaningfully positive):** the α-exponent fit (`n_eff=n/k^α`) + the boost-vs-*n* / boost-vs-(*n/k*)
  collapse plots (α is ill-defined at the null). Deliver the verdict (likely the **`B_k≈0` undirected
  null** — the model uses filler when directed but does not bank undirected computation; report the
  fraction-of-boost-retained where defined) with uncertainty, distinguishing a *genuine* null from a
  no-headroom artifact via the off-ceiling level + the positive control + position-resolution. Inspect
  plots (GPT-vision for complex ones; Gemini 3.1 Pro unavailable). State the §1 interpretation caution
  (a regime verdict is robust, but the stronger "k questions' worth of parallel hidden computation"
  claim is only suggestively supported), and the operational-vs-capability scope of the null
  (told-vs-not-told = Segment 10).

- **Segment 9 — Open-model cross-model generalization (DeepSeek-V3-0324).** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0).
  VERDICT: PARTIALLY REPLICATES.** On DeepSeek-V3-0324 (provider-pinned good cluster, `parser=open`,
  reasoning OFF, genuine no-CoT re-verified in the k-structure across 168k outputs), at its off-ceiling
  mid-band level `add_n4_d10`, position-resolved (early-pool + **Q1-only**, additive+logit, comparison-CI
  with a genuine SE_R off the DeepSeek k=1 curve): the **directedness contrast reproduces (EMERGES at
  k≥4**: ≈0 at k=2, sig.+ Q1 +2.6/+1.9 at k=4/8, sig.+ reveal-before positive control at every k) and the
  **undirected boost DECLINES with k and is sig. below divided at k=4,8** (cleanest at FIXED total filler:
  Q1 undirected +8.2→+4.5→+1.7 for k=2→4→8). **Two load-bearing caveats:** (1) what replicates is the
  *structure*, **NOT the near-null magnitude** — DeepSeek's k=8 Q1 undirected is +3.4pp (~39% of B₁, sig.
  POSITIVE), weaker than Opus's true ~7% null, and the contrast is ~8–9× smaller; (2) **cross-MODEL is
  confounded with cross-LEVEL** (Opus on gradual `sumprod`, DeepSeek on fast-saturating `add_n4_d10`;
  among DeepSeek addition levels only `add_n4_d10` boosts, and a gradual non-addition DeepSeek level was
  not calibrated — out of the focused-generalization scope), so the cross-model *differences* (k=2≈0,
  smaller magnitude) can't be cleanly attributed to the MODEL. The **questions-as-filler / recency-erasure**
  phenomenology and the **retention-declines-with-sharing** gradient reproduce. Provider-confound re-checked
  on the seg9 cells; full review battery (LLM + 2× research-reviewer + data-inspector) with fixes, verdict
  unchanged. (Original scoped-down framing below.)

  Test whether the
  Opus headline — **undirected per-question filler boost near-null at high k (≪ any divided reference)
  while the directedness contrast is positive** — **replicates on a second, open model.** **Scoped as a
  focused generalization check, NOT a full Opus-grade pre-registered power gate** (DeepSeek's boost is
  modest ~+5pp → its own divided-vs-null separation is likely weak; the headline is Opus regardless).
  Mirror the Opus Segment-8 methodology on the model-agnostic harness (provider-pinned good cluster,
  `parser=open`, reasoning OFF, genuine-no-CoT re-verified in the k-structure): pick/confirm a DeepSeek
  **off-ceiling, mid-band-across-k** level (candidate `add_n4_d10`; verify per-position off-ceiling — the
  pre-saturation/ceiling lesson is model-specific; adopt a more gradual/harder DeepSeek level if its early
  positions ceiling), run **reveal-after AND reveal-before each with n=0**, k∈{1,2,4,8}, position-balanced
  + **position-resolved** (early pool), plus a **light DeepSeek in-structure `B₁(n)` at k=1** for the
  divided/parallel references. **PRIMARY cross-model statistic = the directedness contrast** (already
  positive in the pilot: ~+8/+11pp at k=8/k=4, but ≈0 at k=2 — a documented nuance) **+ the undirected
  near-null / `B_k ≪ divided` check**, on additive AND logit, cluster-bootstrap CIs. **Graceful
  degradation:** if DeepSeek's modest boost can't establish a clean divided reference / power a clean 3-way
  regime read, still report the directedness contrast + the undirected near-null and document the power
  limitation (a 2nd strong *closed* model is a documented fallback, likely unnecessary). Build on the
  existing DeepSeek k-question runs (`kq_ds_*`, `kq_pilot_ds_*`, `kq_fixed_ds_*`); use the DEFAULT (told)
  framing to match the headline (told-vs-not-told = Segment 10). **Out of scope:** any Opus re-runs; the
  Opus robustness ablations; the mechanism deep-dives; the final synthesis.

- **Segment 10 — Robustness of the (evolved) regime verdict.** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). The headline
  EVOLVED — the undirected near-null is TOLD-framing-specific; the framing-ROBUST core is "no parallel
  latent thinking."** On **Opus 4.5** at `sumprod_t3_d2` (mirroring the Segment-8 methodology;
  position-resolved early-pool + Q1-only; additive+logit; comparison-SE/SE_R; the reveal-before positive
  control; genuine no-CoT re-verified on 290k outputs), tested three variations.
  - **V1 told-vs-not-told framing (the major finding): the headline PATTERN is FRAMING-DEPENDENT.** Under
    **not-told** the undirected reveal-after boost is **much larger and concentrates on the model's
    DEFAULT first-listed Q1** (k=8 Q1 boost **+1.2 told → +26.3 not-told**, dose-responsive/n-scaling,
    ≈ the directed reveal-before Q1 boost, EXCEEDS the divided ref) → the Segment-8 "does not bank
    UNDIRECTED computation" interpretation is **REFUTED under not-told** (it banks ~1 question's worth,
    on Q1). A **`disclose` arm** (told minus the encouragement clause) pins the driver: `disclose ≈ told
    ≪ not-told`, so the **random-target DISCLOSURE** ("one of these, chosen at random, will be asked"),
    NOT the encouragement, suppresses the Q1-default banking. The **directedness contrast SURVIVES**
    (weakened) on the primary early-pool read (an earlier "contrast vanishes" draft was reviewed and
    RETRACTED); the confound-minimal `reveal_before − disclose` contrast is **large/framing-robust
    (~+24pp)**. The deeper **"no PARALLEL latent thinking across k" SURVIVES / is REINFORCED** (under
    NEITHER framing does the model bank >~1 question's worth).
  - **V2 alternative k∈{3,6,16} (told): HOLD.** Undirected `B_k ≪ divided` at EVERY k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}
    (largest at the least-shared k=2; NOT a clean monotone decline — overlapping CIs); contrast positive
    at every k. Not a k∈{2,4,8} artifact.
  - **V3 counting-vs-dots (told): HOLD / strengthens.** Dots undirected `B_k` is negative (≪ the higher
    dots divided ref); contrast positive/larger. Not counting-specific. (An **unexplained dots×k
    interaction** — dots ≈ counting at k=1 but disruptive in the k-structure — flagged for Segment 11.)
  - **Process exemplary:** a beyond-scope `disclose` experiment + a full review battery (LLM + 2×
    research-reviewer + data-inspector + focused re-review) with all fixes applied, walking back two
    overclaims ("contrast vanishes"; "monotone decline"); the V1 BREAK verdict and the anti-parallel
    conclusion unchanged through review. Marginal ≈ $470 (cumulative ≈ $1758).
  - **⚑ The evolved, framing-ROBUST headline (carry into Segments 11–12):** the model does **NOT** share
    the filler benefit across questions in parallel — it commits a roughly **single-question** compute
    budget to **ONE** question, so the per-question undirected boost is **≪ the divided prediction** and
    there is **no parallel latent thinking**. WHERE that budget lands depends on directedness/framing:
    **directed** → the named question (large, framing-robust); **undirected + disclosure ("told")** →
    suppressed (near-null); **undirected, no disclosure ("not-told")** → its default first-listed Q1

(~1 question's worth). The framing-conditional refinements + the directedness contrast are

- **Segment 11 — Mechanism & confound investigation (reframed around the evolved headline).**
  **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0).** Exemplary, honest, fully-reviewed mechanism work (locked

    a ~flat baseline (the stronger lever, cleanest on sumprod, Opus-leaning — d6 baseline-confounded,
    DeepSeek flat); banks-vs-retrieves refutes diffuse all-questions banking. **Calibrated claim:**
    genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, **filler-amount-dependent** (NOT "filler-time" —
    localization not established) computation committed to ONE question; **NOT parallel.** Graded attention
    not formally excluded; evidence base Opus-leaning. No overclaiming.
  - **TIER 2:** the **default target is POSITIONAL** (primacy/first-listed, not content-based — fixed-item
    reorder); the **context-interference cap is mostly STRUCTURE** (imposed at the first distractor,
    ~saturating; partly questions-as-filler headroom pre-consumption; led with logit).
  - **TIER 3 (light):** dots×k is a framing-INDEPENDENT uniform penalty (a weaker k-structure substrate,

    to the Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit saturation puzzle) documented as an OPEN PUZZLE for the synthesis.
  - **Process:** 5 review rounds (LLM sanity + `empirical-ml-data-inspector` + 2× `research-reviewer` +
    a completion review) with **all fixes applied AND propagated to the verdict file** (`seg11_verdict.md`,
    which Segment 12 lifts from); several overclaims caught & fixed (filler-time→filler-amount-dependent;

  *(Original asked scope, for the record.)* Understand *why* and **harden the interpretation** for the
  synthesis. **TIER 1 (essential):** (a)
  **confirm the Segment-10 V1 framing / Q1-default-banking / disclosure-suppression finding GENERALIZES**
  — it rests on one family (Opus sumprod); run told-vs-not-told(-vs-disclose) on **Opus `add_n15_d6`**

## 6. How this plan may adapt

This decomposition is a starting point; phase planners should adjust based on what actually happens.
**Update after Segment 5:** Segments 0–5 are DONE. The new Segment 5 (inserted after Segment 4)
**resolved the in-structure picture and locked the confound-aware design.** Its results sharpen the
framing for the rest of the project: (1) the Segment-4 "in-structure curves are narrower" worry was a
**full-reveal artifact** — with the adopted minimal reveal the in-structure boost ≈ single-turn (a small
offset that cancels in the gate Δ / contrast); (2) the **pre-saturation confound** is real and decisive
for level choice — fast-saturating addition (d8/d10/d12) **ceilings** at high k, so the gradual
**`sumprod_t3_d2` is the ONLY off-ceiling headline level** (with `add_n15_d6` as a carry-fragile
secondary); (3) the **in-structure 3-way gate is GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2**; (4) the **likely
headline is the `B_k≈0` undirected null**, carried by a large, cross-level, cross-model
**reveal-before/after directedness contrast** (the primary interpretive tool). **⚑ The headline
statistic has evolved:** because `B_k≈0` makes the pre-specified **α-fit ill-defined**, the **PRIMARY**
analysis is now the **3-way (parallel/divided/null) resolution + the directedness contrast, read
position-resolved** (the position-balanced aggregate is a recency-erasure mixture); the **α-fit/collapse
plots are SECONDARY** (only where B_k is meaningfully positive). Segment 6 must **pre-register this
explicitly as a deviation** and **size on position-resolved variance**. This is a clean,
plan-anticipated, *informative* outcome — a genuine, powered null (vindicated by the off-ceiling level
+ a significantly-positive reveal-before control + position-resolution) is a real result, **not** a
failure.

**Update after Segment 8 (DONE — the headline is now confirmed and clean):** the pre-registered §5 verdict
is delivered: **at the most-shared k=8 the undirected per-question filler boost is NEAR-NULL (~7% of B₁,
≪ divided at ~10σ; NULL on the cleaner Q1-only/held-out estimands), while the directedness contrast is
robustly POSITIVE — combined "partial directed".** Parallel is *disfavored* on the sharing axis; the
directedness reading is *suggestive* (not a proven mechanism). This is the clean, plan-anticipated,
*informative* end-state. **Streamlining decision now actioned:** (a) **Segment 9 (cross-model) is kept but
SCOPED DOWN** to a focused DeepSeek generalization of the directedness-positive + undirected-near-null
picture (NOT a full Opus-grade gate — its modest boost can't power that; graceful degradation expected and
acceptable). (b) **Segments 10 (robustness) and 11 (mechanism) remain separate for now, but the
phase planner after Segment 9 should actively reconsider MERGING them** into one focused
"robustness + mechanism + measuring-what-you-want" segment if Segment 9 leaves few open threads (the
robust headline does not depend on resolving the +2.5 mechanism or the cross-model arm). The genuinely-
novel **side-findings to keep prominent in the final write-up regardless**: the
**questions-act-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure** phenomenology, the **context-interference
cap** (~+15–20pp ≪ the lone +35.5), the **retention gradient** (23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8), and the **d6
per-position carry anomaly**. The told-vs-not-told ablation (Segment 10) is interpretively central — it
tests whether the null is framing-dependent (the null is currently "operational under the told framing").

**Update after Segments 6–7 (both DONE):** the experiment is **locked + pre-registered**
(`results/preregistration.md`) and the **full run is executed** (Opus 4.5, 274k calls, all binding
validity guards PASS on the full data). The pilot-anticipated headline is **CONFIRMED descriptively**
(sumprod k=8 reveal-after early `B_k` = +2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7]; directedness contrast +16.2pp; d6 contrast
+31.9pp) — the **NEAR-NULL / WEAK-POSITIVE PARTIAL + DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** picture ("directed ≫
undirected; undirected ≪ divided"). Segment 8 (NEXT) applies the §5 decision rules to deliver the formal
verdict. **Strategic note for the run loop — a post-Segment-8 decision (do NOT act on it yet):** because
the headline is now essentially known and clean, once Segment 8 confirms it the planner should consider
whether the remaining segments can be **streamlined or merged** — e.g. the **cross-model arm (9)** may
degrade gracefully to a qualitative "directedness contrast reproduces on DeepSeek" point (the modest
~+5pp DeepSeek boost likely can't power its own gate), and **robustness (10)** + **mechanism (11)** could
be combined into one focused "robustness + mechanism" segment if Segment 8 leaves few open threads. Keep
the genuinely-novel **side-findings** prominent in the eventual write-up regardless: the
**questions-act-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure** phenomenology, the **context-interference
cap** (~+12–18pp ≪ the lone +37), and the **d6 per-position carry anomaly**. For now the plan stands as
numbered.

**Update after Segment 9 (DONE — cross-model arm complete):** the headline **PARTIALLY REPLICATES** on
DeepSeek-V3-0324 — the *directedness-positive + undirected-declines-with-k + ≪divided-at-k8 structure*
generalizes (with the questions-as-filler/recency-erasure phenomenology and the retention gradient), but
**(a) the absolute near-null magnitude does NOT** (DeepSeek banks more undirected compute, ~39% retention
at k=8 vs Opus ~7%; contrast ~8–9× smaller) and **(b) cross-MODEL is confounded with cross-LEVEL** (Opus
sumprod vs DeepSeek add_n4_d10; a gradual boosting DeepSeek level was not calibrated — largely intrinsic,
since DeepSeek's boost is level-specific). This is an honest, scoped, *informative* cross-model result;
the headline stands on Opus regardless. **On the 10/11 merge I flagged earlier:** I **kept Segments 10
(robustness) and 11 (mechanism) separate** — they are genuinely distinct, and Segment 11's
**attention-redistribution disambiguation** (a live confound on the regime axis, flagged in Segment 8 as
needing NEW experiments) is important enough that bundling 6+ sub-investigations into one phase risks
shallow treatment. **The post-Segment-10 planner should reconsider** whether the *lighter* mechanism
items (the d6 carry anomaly; characterizing the context-interference cap) can fold into the final
synthesis (12), keeping a focused Segment 11 mainly for the attention-redistribution disambiguation.
Segment 10's **told-vs-not-told ablation is interpretively central** (it tests whether the null is
framing-dependent). Side-findings to keep prominent in the final write-up (now cross-model-confirmed):
the **questions-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure** phenomenology, the **context-interference
cap**, and the **retention-declines-with-sharing** gradient.

**Update after Segment 10 (DONE — the headline EVOLVED; mechanism is next):** the told-vs-not-told
ablation (V1) was the major result and **reframed the headline.** The Segment-8 "undirected near-null" is
**TOLD-framing-specific**: under **not-told** the model banks ~one question's worth of filler compute,
**concentrated on its DEFAULT first-listed Q1** (+26.3 at k=8, dose-responsive, exceeds divided), and the
**random-target DISCLOSURE** (not the encouragement — pinned by a `disclose` arm) is what suppresses that
under told. The directedness contrast **survives** (weakened; confound-minimal `reveal_before−disclose`
~+24). alt-k (V2) and dots (V3) **HOLD** (`B_k ≪ divided` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}; counting≈dots). **⚑
The framing-ROBUST core headline is now "NO PARALLEL latent thinking":** the model commits a roughly
single-question compute budget to ONE question (directed target, or default Q1 absent disclosure), so the
per-question undirected boost is `≪ divided` and never spread across k. **Decision — KEPT Segment 11
(mechanism) separate and REFRAMED it** rather than merging into 12: the V1 finding *expanded* the
mechanism landscape (it must be confirmed cross-family/cross-model before the synthesis leans on it, and
the genuine-computation-vs-attention crux still needs new experiments), so a focused mechanism segment is
warranted. Segment 11 priorities: **Tier 1** = confirm the V1 framing/Q1-default finding generalizes
(Opus d6 + DeepSeek) + strengthen/bound the genuine-hidden-computation interpretation (directed
dose-response, compute-selectivity, banks-vs-retrieves); **Tier 2** = the default-target ("why Q1")
reorder + the context-interference-cap-vs-distractor-count characterization; **Tier 3 (light)** = the
dots×k interaction + the d6 carry anomaly (probe or defer to 12). The attention-redistribution concern is
now *partly addressed* by V1's dose-response (genuine, n-scaling compute), so Tier 1(b) solidifies it
rather than starting from scratch. The **side-findings to keep prominent in the synthesis** are unchanged
(questions-as-filler/recency-erasure; the context-interference cap; the retention gradient; dots×k; the
d6 anomaly), plus the **framing-conditional Q1-default-banking** finding itself.

**Update after Segment 11 (DONE — mechanism complete; the synthesis is the LAST segment):** the
mechanism work landed cleanly. **The Segment-10 framing reframe REPLICATES in DIRECTION** cross-family
(Opus d6) AND cross-model (DeepSeek) — not-told concentrates a positive Q1 boost that the random-target
disclosure suppresses — though the **magnitude attenuates even on logit** (DeepSeek ~10× weaker, a
partial leg; d6 rests on Q1+logit). **The genuine-hidden-computation reading is STRENGTHENED but
honestly BOUNDED:** the directed dose-response (necessary-not-sufficient — disfavours a *pure-step*
attention account) + compute-selectivity (the stronger lever, cleanest on sumprod, **Opus-leaning**) +
banks-vs-retrieves support a **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, *filler-amount-dependent***
(NOT "filler-time" — localization not shown) computation committed to ONE question; a *graded* attention
account is **not formally excluded**, so the synthesis must say "strengthened, not proven" and must NOT
claim parallel latent thinking (disfavoured). The **default target is positional** (primacy, not
content); the **context-interference cap is structure-imposed** (the first distractor). dots×k is a
framing-independent uniform penalty; the d6 carry anomaly is an open puzzle. Five review rounds with all
fixes applied + propagated to `seg11_verdict.md`. **No merge needed now** — Segment 12 is the natural
last segment. **The central Segment-12 challenge** is writing the EVOLVED headline as a **clean
convergent result** (not a sequence of reversals): lead with the framing-robust "no parallel latent
thinking / single-question budget to ONE position" core, then the framing-conditional WHERE-it-lands and
the mechanism, and **reconcile the pre-registered §5 verdict (correct within its told scope) with the
Segment-10 reframe (exploratory scope-widening) transparently.** Note the unified `write_up.md` currently
has **stale Segment-0-era trailing "Headline results / Limitations / Open questions" sections** that the
synthesis must remove/rewrite. The genuine-computation **decisive future tests** (a length-matched
random-filler control + activation/logit-lens probing) are out of scope — flag as future work.

**Update after Segment 12 (DONE — ✅ PROJECT COMPLETE):** the final synthesis landed cleanly and met/
exceeded every rubric criterion. `write_up.md` now opens with a self-contained **"⭑ Final synthesis
(READ THIS)"** that a fresh reader can read top-down; the **§5-verdict-vs-Segment-10-reframe
reconciliation** is transparent (a *sharpening*, not a reversal — LLM-sanity-confirmed it reads as
neither goalpost-moving nor a buried reversal); the stale Segment-0-era trailing sections are folded
into a scoped appendix; the interpretation is honestly bounded (parallel **disfavoured**; genuine
computation **strengthened-not-proven**; no overclaiming). A key review fix **scoped the `≪ divided`
magnitude claim to the *told* framing** and reframed the framing-independent core as "no parallel + no
divided-pool *spreading* — banks ≤~1 question on ONE question" (resolving a headline-internal tension).
`--assert-cached` reproduces all grids at **$0** (durable log); a numbers-match check PASSES; the full
review battery ran with all fixes applied; the locked science is byte-identical (untouched). **The
project is COMPLETE** — the core question is answered (no parallel latent thinking; a single-question
budget committed to ONE position; framing determines where it lands), and the remaining limitations are
inherent / out-of-infrastructure-scope (the **decisive genuine-computation test — activation/logit-lens
probing — needs model internals; this sandbox has no GPU/vllm**, Opus/DeepSeek are API-only; a behavioral
random-filler control would not resolve the *graded*-attention ambiguity) or high-risk/low-value (a
gradual boosting DeepSeek level may not exist; the directedness/structure findings already generalize),
all documented as future work — **none headline-blocking, so no further segment is warranted.**

Current numbering: 6 power/lock-in/pre-reg (DONE), 7 main-exp full runs (DONE), 8 main analysis (DONE),
9 open-model cross-model generalization (DONE — partially replicates),
10 robustness (DONE — headline framing-dependent; anti-parallel core robust),
11 mechanism (DONE — reframe generalizes in direction; genuine computation strengthened-not-proven),
**12 final synthesis + reproducibility + review (DONE — ✅ PROJECT COMPLETE)**. Other risks unchanged: the open-model cross-model arm (Segment 9) may be under-powered (modest/narrow DeepSeek
boost → fall back to a 2nd strong closed model or report DeepSeek as a "boost reproduces" point;
headline is Opus regardless); analyze on **both additive and logit** scales (the baseline shifts a lot

# Filler Tokens — Parallel Latent Thinking? (final write-up)

> Multi-segment project, **COMPLETE (Segments 0–12).** This file is the canonical, self-contained
> final document. Read the **"Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section just below to learn the answer
> top-down; the per-segment sections further down are the detailed record/appendix. From Segment 9 on
> the project also keeps **phase-specific** write-ups (`writeups/write_up_segment_{8,9,10,11,12}_phase_0.md`)
> alongside this unified summary — the convention evolved over the project (Segments 0–8 were unified-only).

## Research question (overall project)
When a recent LLM answers a math problem with **no chain-of-thought** (the answer is the first
token it emits), it gets one forward pass of compute at the answer position. Padding the prompt
with semantically-empty **filler tokens** (e.g. `1 2 3 ... 300`) before the answer reportedly
raises no-CoT math accuracy ([Redwood 2026](https://blog.redwoodresearch.org/p/recent-llms-can-use-filler-tokens);
[Pfau et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.15758)). **The core question:** if the model is given
*k* difficulty-matched questions that **share the same total *n* filler**, then one is revealed by
index **after** the filler and only that single answer is read, does each question keep the full
single-question boost `B₁(n)` (**parallel latent thinking**, `B_k≈B₁(n)`), does the boost shrink as
questions share a fixed pool (**divided pool**, `B_k≈B₁(n/k)`), or does the model **not bank
computation it can't direct** (**null**, `B_k≈0`)? The pre-specified headline statistic was a sharing
exponent **α** (`n_eff = n/k^α`; α=0 parallel, α=1 divided); the co-primary disambiguator is the
**reveal-before vs reveal-after directedness contrast**. See `OVERALL_PLAN.md` for the full plan.

## ⭑ Final synthesis (READ THIS)

> This section stands on its own — a fresh reader can read it top-down and learn the project's final
> answer without wading through the per-segment chronology below.

### TL;DR (plain language)
Padding a no-chain-of-thought prompt with meaningless **filler tokens** before the answer boosts a recent
LLM's arithmetic accuracy — extra positions to do hidden computation. We asked whether that hidden compute
can be **shared across *k* questions at once**. **It cannot.** The model banks **at most ~one question's
worth** of filler computation, committed to a **single** question — it never spreads compute in parallel,
and never realizes a shared pool that grows with *k*. *Where* that single-question budget lands depends on
how the question is framed (a named target, or — absent a "one-at-random" disclosure — the first-listed
question). The effect is a real, dose-responsive, difficulty-sensitive computation, but the precise
mechanism is **not** pinned down.

### Abstract
On **Claude Opus 4.5** answering multi-digit arithmetic with **genuine no-CoT** (the answer is literally
the first token, enforced by assistant prefill, reasoning off — verified on >1M outputs), padding the
prompt with semantically-empty **filler** before the answer raises accuracy by a large per-question
boost — up to **`B₁ ≈ +35pp`** on our gradual mid-band level (`sumprod_t3_d2`). We asked whether that
hidden computation can be **spread across *k* difficulty-matched questions** sharing one filler pool,
when only one randomly-revealed question is read (**reveal-after**). The answer is **convergent: the
model does NOT do parallel latent thinking**, and the **anti-parallel core is framing-robust.** It
**behaves as if banking at most ~one question's worth** of filler computation, committed to a **single**
question — never a divided pool that *scales* with k. **Under the encouraging ("told") framing** that
single-question commitment is *suppressed*, so the per-question **undirected** boost stays
**`≪ the divided-pool prediction` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (counting **and** dots); **under
"not-told"** it instead *concentrates* ~one question's worth on its **first-listed Q1** (which can
*exceed* the divided per-question share — concentration on ONE question, the **opposite** of even
spreading, still not parallel). The structure **partially replicates on the open model DeepSeek-V3-0324**
(weaker; cross-model confounded with cross-level). **WHERE** that single-question budget lands depends on
**directedness + framing**: a **named** (reveal-before) target gets a large boost; an undirected model
**told** a random question will be asked **suppresses** the commitment (near-null); an undirected model
**not told** defaults to banking ~one question's worth on its **first-listed Q1**. The driver of the
suppression is the random-target **disclosure**, not the encouragement. The banked benefit is **genuine**
(i.e. a real task-relevant computational effect, *not* a format/length/reduced-give-up artifact),
**dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent** — **meaningfully strengthened (still
Opus-`sumprod`-leaning), but NOT a proven mechanism** (a *graded*-attention account is not formally
excluded; localization to the filler positions is not established — hence "as if banking", and
"filler-amount-dependent" not "filler-time").

### The final headline (in this shape)
1. **The robust, framing-INDEPENDENT core — no parallel latent thinking; no divided-pool spreading.**
   Across the whole project the model **never spreads compute across the *k* questions** — under
   **neither** framing does it bank **>~1 question's worth**, and it never realizes a divided pool that
   *scales* with k. **Under the pre-registered "told" framing** the undirected per-question boost is
   **`≪ the divided prediction` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (Segments 7/8 + the Segment-10 V2/V3
   robustness), on **counting AND dots** filler; **under "not-told"** the model instead *concentrates*
   ~one question's worth on its default Q1 (which can *exceed* the divided per-question share — that is
   concentration on ONE question, the **opposite** of even divided spreading, still not parallel). The
   qualitative *structure* (directedness + undirected-declines-with-sharing) **partially replicates on
   DeepSeek** (weaker magnitude; cross-model is confounded with cross-level — see caveats).
2. **The pre-registered Segment-8 result (the formal, frozen-rules verdict — honored, scoped).** Under
   the pre-registered **encouraging ("told") framing** on the locked grid, at the most-shared **k=8** the
   undirected reveal-after `B_k` is **NEAR-NULL**: **NULL** on the cleaner **Q1-only (+1.2 [−1.1,+3.6])**
   and **held-out fresh-only (+0.9 [−1.1,+2.9])** estimands, and only statistically **PARTIAL/sub-divided
   (+2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7])** on the pooled early-average (**~7% of B₁**; **≪ divided +13.7 at ~10σ**,
   SE_R-inclusive). The genuinely-falsifiable co-primary, the **directedness contrast**, is robustly
   **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE (+16.2pp [+14.3,+18.3]**, sig.+ positive control +18.7) → combined **"partial
   directed"**. A **retention gradient** (less-shared cells retain more: **23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8**; non-monotone
   — the robust claim is `B_k ≪ divided` at **every** k, not a clean monotone decline). **α is NOT
   estimated** (the positivity gate fails — α is ill-defined near the null; a pre-registered, documented
   deviation).
3. **WHERE the single-question budget lands depends on directedness + framing (Segment 10 V1, exploratory
   scope-widening — a major finding).** **Directed** (reveal-before, target named before the filler) → the
   named question gets a large, framing-robust boost (the confound-minimal `reveal_before − disclose`
   contrast is **~+24pp** at k=8 Q1). **Undirected + "told"** (the prompt **discloses** "one of these,
   chosen at random, will be asked") → the commitment is **suppressed** → the near-null Segment-8 result.
   **Undirected + "not-told"** (no disclosure) → the model commits ~one question's worth to its **default
   first-listed Q1** (k=8 Q1 boost **+26.3** [+23.0,+29.6], n-scaling; this is **~68% of the not-told lone
   boost ≈ one question's worth, NOT more** — it exceeds the *divided per-question share* only because it is
   *concentrated* on one question, not because the model banks >1 question). The driver is the random-target
   **disclosure**, not the encouragement (pinned by the `disclose` arm: **disclose ≈ told ≪ not-told**,
   k=8 Q1 +2.0 / +1.2 / +26.3). So "the model does not bank UNDIRECTED computation" is **refuted under
   not-told** — but the **anti-parallel core survives** (under neither framing does it bank >~1 question's
   worth).
4. **Mechanism (Segment 11, exploratory; cross-family + cross-model confirmed in DIRECTION).** The banked
   filler benefit is a **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent**
   computation committed to ONE question — the **named** target (target-following but **primacy-modulated**)
   or, absent the disclosure, the **positional default Q1** (positional, not content-based: the same item
   gives **+25.1** at Q1 vs **−18.0** at Q8). The in-prompt **directed** boost is **capped at ~half the
   lone boost** by the multi-question structure (most of the cap appears at the first distractor; partly
   questions-as-filler headroom pre-consumption). *("The lone boost" = the single-question B₁: ~+35pp
   position-averaged at the operating n, rising to ~+43pp on the hardest items/positions; the not-told
   lone ~+38pp is the same quantity under the not-told framing — these are the same effect measured under
   different item/position/framing conditions, not different findings.)*

### Answer to the original research question (parallel / divided / null)
- **Parallel (`B_k≈B₁(n)`) is DISFAVORED — not merely unproven.** At k=8 the undirected `B_k ≈ 7% of B₁`,
  far below the parallel ceiling (+35.5) **and** the divided prediction (+13.7).
- **Divided (`B_k≈B₁(n/k)`) is EXCLUDED** — at the pre-registered (told) headline cell by **~10σ**
  (SE_R-inclusive), and the undirected boost is `≪ divided` at every k **under the told framing**; the
  collapse plots show the `B_k` lines collapse onto the k=1 curve on **neither** the `n` nor the `n/k`
  axis ⇒ neither parallel nor divided. **Even-divided-pool *spreading* is excluded framing-independently**
  (under not-told the model concentrates ~1 question on Q1 rather than spreading 1/k to each — the
  opposite of an even divided split).
- **The realized regime under the pre-registered "told" framing is the third, NEAR-NULL outcome** — the
  model does not bank undirected computation it can't direct.
- **α is NOT estimated** — it is **ill-defined near the null** (no `n_eff` maps the positive B₁ curve onto
  ≈0). This is a **pre-registered, documented** result, not a failure; the headline statistic is the
  3-way regime resolution + the directedness contrast instead.
- **Fraction-of-boost-retained:** a **retention gradient 23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8** (the less-shared cells retain
  more), all ≪ the interference-naive divided prediction. (This uses each k's per-k operating point; the
  robust claim is `B_k ≪ divided` at **every** k, not a clean monotone decline — the fixed-total-filler
  view gives a steeper ~19%→3%.)
- **The genuinely-falsifiable result is the directedness contrast** (reveal-before − reveal-after): robustly
  **positive**, i.e. the model **uses filler when it knows the target** but banks ~none it can't direct.

### Key supporting findings + their figures
*(The story is carried by ~6 core figures — the **regime verdict**, the **directedness contrast**, the
**per-position recency-erasure**, the **collapse plots**, the **framing reframe**, and the
**dose-response + selectivity**; the rest are supporting. All are reused/lightly-polished from Segments
8/10/11, inspected, the complex ones GPT-vision-checked, captions de-overclaimed, axes = realized tokens
+ pp.)*
- **Regime verdict** — reveal-after `B_k` is near-zero/negative and ALWAYS `≪ divided` (neither parallel
  nor divided), with CIs vs the parallel/divided/null references, position-resolved:
  ![regime verdict](../plots/seg8_regime_verdict.png) ![B₁ references](../plots/seg8_b1_references.png)
- **Directedness contrast by k** (the falsifiable co-primary, + the sig.+ reveal-before positive control),
  both levels: ![contrast](../plots/seg8_contrast.png)
- **Per-position `B_k` / recency-erasure** — explicit filler erases the late-position recency advantage,
  which is *why* the analysis is position-resolved: ![per-position](../plots/seg8_position_bk.png)
- **Collapse plots** — neither the `n` nor the `n/k` axis collapses the lines (⇒ neither parallel nor
  divided; α ill-defined): ![collapse](../plots/seg8_collapse.png)
- **Retention gradient** — measured undirected retention 23%→7%, far below the divided prediction at every
  k: ![fraction retained](../plots/seg8_fraction_retained.png)
- **Framing reframe / Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression** (the Segment-10 V1 finding + the
  cross-family/cross-model replication): ![V1 framing](../plots/seg10_v1_framing.png)
  ![framing replication](../plots/seg11_framing.png)
- **Directed dose-response** (committed Q1 boost scales with filler amount, tracking lone B₁(n)) +
  **compute-selectivity** (directed boost rises with item difficulty at a flat baseline): the
  genuine-computation evidence: ![dose](../plots/seg11_dose.png) ![selectivity](../plots/seg11_selectivity.png)
- **Default target is positional + the context-interference cap is mostly structure:**
  ![default + cap](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png)

### Reconciling the pre-registered §5 verdict with the Segment-10 reframe (a sharpening, NOT a reversal)
The headline **sharpened** over the project; it is **not** a sequence of reversals. To be bulletproof
against both the "you moved the goalposts" and the "you buried a reversal" critiques:
- **The pre-registration was honored.** The Segment-8 §5 "near-null undirected / partial directed" verdict
  is the **formal, frozen-rules** result, computed by applying `results/preregistration.md` §5 **verbatim**
  to the Segment-7 data, with **all four validity guards passed** and the headline cell **pre-registered**
  (pilot-known, with forking-paths protection disclosed). It is **correct within the encouraging ("told")
  framing it pre-registered.**
- **Segment 10 (explicitly EXPLORATORY) widened the scope** to the **not-told** framing and found the
  undirected behavior differs: the model banks ~one question's worth on its default Q1. This **refutes the
  *interpretation*** "the model does not bank UNDIRECTED computation" **as a framing-general claim**, while
  leaving the pre-registered told-framing number untouched. The **disclose** arm pins the driver to the
  random-target **disclosure**, not the encouragement.
- **The anti-parallel core holds throughout.** Under **neither** framing does the model bank more than ~one
  question's worth, and the undirected `B_k ≪ divided` regime leg is **robust to k and filler-type** (V2 +
  V3 HOLD). So the evolution is: a frozen-rules near-null *under told* → a sharper, framing-conditional
  picture of *where the single-question budget lands* → a converged **anti-parallel** headline with a
  characterized mechanism. The pre-registered verdict is **not re-declared or altered** here; Segment 10/11
  are labeled exploratory scope-widening.

### Genuinely-novel side-findings (real contributions beyond the headline)
- **Questions-act-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure.** The n=0 baseline **RISES** with k (the
  distractor questions act as preceding-context filler); the position-balanced aggregate `B_k` is a
  **recency-erasure mixture** (explicit filler erases the late positions' recency advantage), which is
  **why** the entire analysis is **position-resolved** (early-pool + Q1-only), not aggregate.
- **Framing-conditional Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression** (Segment 10 V1): the model has a
  **positional default target** (Q1) it banks on when undirected and not told a random question is coming;
  **disclosing** the random target suppresses this. Replicates in DIRECTION cross-family (Opus d6) and
  cross-model (DeepSeek).
- **Context-interference cap** (~half the lone boost), **structure-imposed at the first distractor** (lone
  +43 → +24–30 with ≥1 distractor; ~saturating).
- **Retention gradient** 23%→7% across k.
- **Filler-boost selectivity** (Segment 0): the boost helps **deep multi-digit arithmetic**, not
  aggregate-many-easy-checks (null even at a matched ~48% baseline) — refines the Pfau "parallelizable
  computation" framing.
- **Documented open puzzles:** the **dots×k** interaction (dots ≈ counting at k=1 but a uniform ~6–7pp
  weaker filler in the k-structure) and the **d6 per-position carry anomaly** (tied to the Segment-2
  8-vs-6-digit instant-saturation puzzle).

### Honest interpretation caveats (load-bearing — do NOT overclaim)
- **The regime/directedness verdict is robust to mechanism**, but the stronger claim "the model does *k*
  questions' worth of latent thinking in parallel" is **disfavored** (not merely unproven).
- **Genuine computation is STRENGTHENED but NOT PROVEN.** A **pure-step** attention/recency-reallocation
  account is **disfavored** (the directed boost shows graded n-dependence and difficulty-selectivity at a
  flat baseline). But a **graded** attention/bandwidth account is **NOT formally excluded**;
  **localization is not established** (filler *enables* the benefit and it scales with filler *amount*, but
  the computation is not shown to occur **AT** the filler positions — hence **"filler-amount-dependent",
  NOT "filler-time"**); and the cleanest genuine-computation evidence is **Opus-`sumprod`-leaning** (on d6
  selectivity carries a baseline confound; on DeepSeek it is absent).
- **The directedness reading is suggestive, not proven** at the mechanism level (a positive contrast is
  consistent with banked directed compute OR with reveal-timing-induced attention/retrieval changes); we
  rely on it for the **regime/directedness** verdict, not for a mechanism claim.
- **The clean *absolute*-regime read rests on one Opus family** (`sumprod_t3_d2`); the
  directedness/structure/framing findings are cross-family + cross-model.

### Limitations & future work (honest — the result is informative, not a failure)
- **Genuine computation is strengthened, not proven.** The **decisive future tests** are a **length-matched
  RANDOM-filler content control** + **activation/logit-lens probing of the last filler position** (both out
  of this project's *behavioral* scope — the latter needs model internals). Out of scope here.
- **One clean Opus family for the absolute regime**; **cross-model is confounded with cross-level** (Opus
  `sumprod_t3_d2` vs DeepSeek `add_n4_d10`; no DeepSeek level matches sumprod's flat early band).
- **No clean reasoning-bound positive control** (a decontaminated post-cutoff competition-math set) was
  obtained — the boost is validated on arithmetic-precision tasks (the selectivity finding; AIME 2025 is
  contaminated/underpowered and demoted).
- **The d6 per-position carry anomaly / the Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit instant-saturation puzzle** remains an
  **open puzzle**; the **dots×k** interaction is characterized but not mechanistically resolved.
- **The headline cell was pilot-known** (Segment 7 added precision + frozen-rules forking-paths protection,
  not discovery — pre-registered transparently) and the **fresh-only firewall is weak** (~±3pp on 350
  never-seen instances; it confirms `B_k ≪ divided` but cannot crisply separate NULL from PARTIAL).

### How to reproduce
See the **"How to reproduce"** section near the end of this document for the full guide (canonical
datasets + loader, the locked pre-registration, the harness, the per-segment runners/analysis libs, where
each headline number/figure comes from, the model/provider config, and the `--assert-cached` audit). In
one line: the science lives in `results/preregistration.md` (LOCKED) → `results/seg7_*.jsonl` (the
Opus main run, 274k calls) → `results/seg8_verdict.md` (the §5 verdict) → `results/seg{9,10,11}_verdict.md`
(cross-model / robustness / mechanism); every cell re-streams from cache at **$0** via `--assert-cached`.

## ⚑ Final verdict (the project's answer — read the "Final synthesis" section above for the full story)
> **The project's answer (the strongest SUPPORTABLE statement):** the model does **NOT** do parallel
> latent thinking across the *k* shared questions. It commits a **genuine, dose-responsive,
> difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent single-question compute budget to ONE question** (the
> directed target, or — absent the random-target disclosure — its **positional default Q1**), so the
> per-question **undirected** boost is **`≪ the divided prediction` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}**. Under the
> pre-registered **encouraging ("told") framing** at the most-shared **k=8** the undirected reveal-after
> `B_k` is **NEAR-NULL** (NULL on Q1-only +1.2 and held-out fresh-only +0.9; statistically PARTIAL/sub-divided
> +2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7] on the pooled early-average; ~7% of B₁; **≪ divided +13.7 at ~10σ**), while the
> falsifiable co-primary **directedness contrast is DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE (+16.2pp**, sig.+ control +18.7)
> → **"partial directed."** A **retention gradient 23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8** (non-monotone — the robust claim is
> `B_k ≪ divided` at every k, not a clean monotone decline); **α is NOT estimated** (ill-defined near the
> null — a pre-registered deviation). The genuine-computation reading is **strengthened but NOT
> proven** (a *pure-step* attention account is disfavoured; a *graded* one is not excluded; localization is
> not established — hence "filler-amount-dependent", not "filler-time"). **Read the "Final synthesis (READ
> THIS)" section above** for the full story; the formal frozen-rules verdict is `results/seg8_verdict.md` +
> the "Segment 8" section; the framing reframe + mechanism are Segments 10/11.

**The design-phase power gate (historical, preserved in the Segment 2 + Segment 5 sections below).** The
make-or-break Segment-2 gate **passed (GO)**: the parallel (`B_k≈B₁(n)`) and divided (`B_k≈B₁(n/k)`)
reference predictions were shown **separably different for k∈{2,4,8}** on multiple canonical levels (the
wide no-degradation plateau is the enabler — operate total filler `n` on the plateau, keep `n/k` just above
the helping-threshold). Segment 5 re-confirmed the gate **in-structure** and identified **`sumprod_t3_d2` +
counting** as the only off-ceiling headline level (the others ceiling under k); Segment 6 locked + pre-registered
the grid. The full per-level Δ/σ tables, the level-choice reasoning, and the d6+dots design-lever analysis are
in the **"Segment 2"** and **"Segment 5"** detailed sections below.

**Two load-bearing caveats (carry forward):**
1. **The divided prediction `B₁(n/k)` is an ABSTRACTION.** It is read off the *single-question* curve
   as a model of "even split of a divided pool"; the real k-prompt gives the model all `n` positions
   at once and has its own (likely lower) n=0 baseline (context interference). The gate proves the two
   *reference* predictions are far enough apart to be resolved — it does **not** guarantee the measured
   `B_k` lands on either (it could even be the third “≈0” outcome). Helpfully, the shared n=0 baseline
   **cancels** in `Δ` (so Δ is a clean *curve-shape* separation); the main experiment still needs its
   per-k baselining + logit-scale checks (planned).
2. **The verdict was corrected twice during review:** an earlier draft (a) wrongly capped operating `n`
   at the saturation point (made it look like only d6 worked for k≤8) and (b) under-sampled mul/sumprod
   (m=500). Both fixed: operate `n` over the whole plateau; mul/sumprod re-measured at m=1000.

## Segment 1: canonical datasets + difficulty calibration (the dataset foundation)
Desiderata-first (`writeups/desiderata_procedural_arithmetic.md`, LLM-critiqued), a rigorous
baseline-vs-difficulty calibration (reusing cached Segment-0 baselines), locked mid-band levels saved
to `data/*.jsonl` with metadata + a deterministic loader, and a full quality-validation +
`dataset-reviewer` pass.

**Calibration (Opus 4.5 no-CoT, n=0).** 8-digit multi-operand addition is a **clean monotonic
difficulty knob** in #operands (n10=89% → n20=17%); 6-digit is **non-smooth** (a confirmed localized
dip at `add_n14_d6`=34% vs its n13/n15/n16 neighbors ~49% — a model quirk, not a magnitude artifact;
the shipped `add_n15_d6` is *not* in the anomalous spot). Multiplication difficulty depends on **both
operands** (≈ #partial-product rows × width), not the smaller one alone (hence the sawtooth in the
plot when ordered by smaller operand); sum-of-products by #terms. ![calibration](../plots/calibration.png)

**Locked canonical levels** — baseline is the **pooled n=1000** estimate (calibration items 0–499 +
disjoint held-out 3000–3499; the two halves are the selection-bias check); paired filler-gate boost
confirms responsiveness (all derived from the lock file at save time, not hardcoded):

| level (`data/<tag>.jsonl`) | role | no-CoT baseline (pooled) | calib / held-out | filler-gate boost |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n16_d8` | addition lower | 47.1% | 45.4 / 48.8 | +13.8pp |
| `add_n15_d8` | addition central — **PRIMARY** | 55.1% | 54.4 / 55.8 | +17.2pp |
| `add_n12_d8` | addition higher (k-headroom) | 68.2% | 68.2 / 68.2 | +20.8pp |
| `add_n15_d6` | addition max-boost alt | 52.0% | 49.2 / 54.8 | +30.4pp |
| `mul_a4_b4_hom` | 2nd family (homogenized mul) | 47.1% | 47.0 / 47.2 | +11.0pp |
| `sumprod_t3_d2` | 3rd family (all-+ sum-of-products) | 40.3% | 40.8 / 39.8 | +22.6pp |

The addition levels **span the mid-band** (47→52→55→68%); `add_n15_d8` is the designated primary;
`add_n12_d8` (68%) is the higher-baseline level kept because the n=0 baseline drops as k grows;
`add_n15_d6` (+30pp) is the max-boost option for the downstream power gate (its baseline is the
noisiest — calib 49 vs held-out 55, hence the pooled 52% is the best estimate). The three families
(addition / multiplication / sum-of-products) all boost → the effect isn't addition-specific. Each
pool has 5000 deterministic items with per-item difficulty features.

**Dataset-quality verdict (validated, `validate_canonical.py` — all PASS; figure
[dataset_quality.png](../plots/dataset_quality.png)).** Deterministic & prefix-stable; unique **exact
big-int** answers (independent recompute); no within-set duplicate questions and **canonical-form
(reorder-invariant) dedup**; non-degenerate operands (<2% digit-pattern artifacts); robust
first-integer scoring unit-tested AND verified on 24k+ real cached outputs (0 scoring inconsistencies;
0 space-separated-digit outputs at baseline; ≤0.2% non-integer at n=0). The calibrated baselines
re-confirm on the **disjoint held-out** sample (panel A) and the filler-boost **plateau magnitude** is
**robust to filler type** (counting ≈ dots at ~300 tok, panel B; but the small-n *rise shape* differs —
see Segment 2's dots check). Within-level difficulty is **distributionally matched** (i.i.d.
draws): *artifact*-feature gaps are tiny (≤6% for addition) and the only real artifact (mul
round-number answers) was removed; the remaining variation is **gradual, genuine** difficulty (carry
load up to ~24% across addition `max_carry`; operand/answer magnitude for mul/sum-of-products) — no
degenerate easy/hard cliff (panels C/D).

**Judgment-heavy dataset findings (with rationale):**
- **Multiplication needed homogenization.** Raw 4×4 has an easy subpopulation (trailing-zero / small
  products, **27% gap**, `analyze_mul_homogeneity.py`). Shipped `mul_a4_b4_hom` = 8-digit products,
  no trailing zeros (artifact gap→0%; residual smaller-operand gradient is gradual). Addition is
  naturally homogeneous (artifact gaps ≤6%) → it is the primary.
- **Signed expression-eval dropped; all-positive sum-of-products shipped instead.** A *signed*
  sum-of-products (`a*b ± c*d ± e*f`) boosts (+16pp) but is **difficulty-bimodal by sign** (negative
  answers ~7% vs positive ~32%) and floor-band (~25%). The **all-positive** variant `sumprod_t3_d2`
  (with a `|answer|≥1000` floor so all answers are ≥4-digit) removes the sign bimodality, lands cleanly
  mid-band (40%), is homogeneous, and boosts strongly (+22pp). *Caveat:* its answers are 4–5 digit
  (vs ~9–10 for addition, ~8 for mul) — a magnitude difference to keep in mind for any cross-family
  comparison (it's the only mid-band sum-of-products level; larger-answer variants sit at the floor).
  (No shipped family has negatives now; negative scoring is still unit-tested.)
- **Per-item proxies / `num_carries` caveat.** For many-operand addition `num_carry_columns` is
  ~saturated (collinear with answer width), so we also save **`max_carry`/`total_carry`** which
  genuinely vary; downstream feature-balancing should use those.
- **Selection-bias / loader guard.** The default `load_canonical(tag, n)` returns items `[0:n]` =
  the calibration sample; downstream "fresh" baselines should pass `offset>=500` (documented;
  metadata records the calibration/held-out item ranges).
- **Cross-family caveats (for any cross-family robustness comparison, NOT the within-family k-design):**
  the families differ in **answer magnitude** (addition ~9–10 digit, mul ~8, sum-of-products 4–5) and
  **prompt length** (addition long, sum-of-products short) — so equal no-CoT baselines don't mean
  equal surface difficulty, and a fixed filler budget loads different total context per family. Within
  a family these are constant. The `mul_a4_b4_hom` label means "4×4 *conditioned on* 8-digit non-round
  products" (a selected sub-distribution, not representative 4×4) — the round-answer **artifact** is
  removed; genuine operand-magnitude variation (the largest of the shipped levels, ~24% gradual) remains.
- **Note for downstream:** the filler boost here is a *gate* confirmed on the calibration sample
  (items [0,500)); the precise `B₁(n)` magnitude should be re-measured on **fresh** items (`offset>=500`)
  in the next segment. Under filler, mul/sum-of-products emit ~7% spaced-digit/"shows-working" outputs
  (first-integer parse handles them conservatively, biasing those boosts slightly **downward** — use
  `parse_answer_robust`); addition stays clean. `sumprod` uses standard ×-before-+ precedence (the
  ~40% accuracy confirms the model applies it; random 2-digit factors rarely create shortcuts).
- The three d8 addition levels share a seed so are **nested prefixes** (operands of the smaller-n
  level are a subset) — fine for within-level matching, but they are correlated across levels.
  (`add_n12_d8`'s identical 68.2% on both disjoint samples is a verified coincidence, not a bug.)

Full calibration/validation detail and the review-driven fixes: `progress_log.md` (Segment 1).

## Segment 2: the `B₁(n)` single-question boost curve + the power gate
The make-or-break segment. Measured the **paired** boost `B₁(n) = acc(n filler) − acc(0 filler)` on
Opus 4.5 over a **fine** small-n grid + a long tail (realized tok ≈ 0–1200), on **fresh** canonical
items (`offset=500`, disjoint from the Segment-1 calibration/held-out windows), with bootstrap CIs
over items (paired). 76k calls + densification of the gate-critical `add_n15_d6` rise. `n` is always
in **realized tokens** (Anthropic `input_tokens` delta; mapping ≈ 2·items+1, level-independent). The
robust parser scores mul/sumprod (their <0.5% spaced-digit-under-filler outputs). Runner
`run_b1curve.py`; analysis `analyze_b1curve.py`/`b1lib.py`; plots `plot_b1curve.py`.

**Rising-region characterization (realized tokens; addition m=1000, mul/sumprod m=1000):** `n_lo` =
filler-starts-helping threshold; `n_sat` = saturation onset; `n_top` = no-degradation upper bound.
| level | base | plateau boost | n_lo | n_sat | n_top | degrades? | small-n dip? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n15_d8` (old PRIMARY) | 55% | **+16.1pp** [13.3,19.0] | ≤3 | 7 | 1201 | no | no |
| `add_n15_d6` | 49% | **+30.3pp** [27.3,33.3] | 25 | 29 | 1201 | no | **yes, −8pp @ 5–11 tok** |
| `add_n12_d8` | 68% | **+22.1pp** [19.5,24.9] | ≤3 | 7 | 1201 | no | no |
| `mul_a4_b4_hom` | 51% | +11.0pp [8.4,13.8] | 31 | 41 | 501 | **YES** | yes, −3pp @ 5 tok |
| `sumprod_t3_d2` | 40% | +23.3pp [20.0,26.7] | 21 | 81 | 801 | **mild** | yes, −8pp @ 5 tok |

- **8-digit addition saturates almost instantly** (n_sat 7 tok): the boost jumps to near-plateau by
  3–7 realized tokens, **no degradation** to 1200 tok. **mul degrades** (peaks +12.8 @101 → +6.2
  @1201) and **sumprod degrades mildly** (peaks ~+27 @201 → +24 @1201). (`n_lo=≤3 tok` for d8/d12 is
  resolution-limited — 1 item is the smallest measurable filler.)
- **`add_n15_d6`** has a small-filler **dip** (−8pp at 5–11 tok — genuine high-digit **carry
  disruption**, verified by per-item flips + clean error inspection: new errors are off by exactly
  1,000,000), a flat ~0 region (15–23 tok), then a **sharp step** at 25→29 tok (+7 → +29, densely
  confirmed). Its rise is **narrow & steep**; what is *wide* is the flat **plateau** (29–33pp out to
  1200 tok). **`sumprod`/`add_n15_d10`** have much more **gradual** rises.
- **Small-n dip is REAL, not a filler-content artifact:** it **reproduces with dots filler** on d6
  (−10pp at 15–25 tok), confirming "small filler genuinely hurts" (carry disruption). Interestingly
  the **sharp step is partly counting-specific** — with **dots** (m=1000), d6 rises *gradually*
  (div-slope ~0.3 pp/tok at threshold, vs counting's 2.9) to a **wide flat +27pp plateau** (n_top=1000,
  no degradation; gated directly in `analyze_d6_dots_gate.py`: k=4 Δ=+21.0/7.9σ, k=8 Δ=+23.1/8.7σ,
  k=2 marginal +6.3/2.4σ). So d6's "steepness"/fragility is **filler-type-dependent**, and **dots gives
  d6 a graded (robust) divided reference *and* a large Δ** at k≥4 — filler type is a usable design
  lever (see the verdict box).
  ![d6 counting vs dots](../plots/d6_filler_compare.png) The mul/sumprod dip at ≤5 tok is partly a
  small-n shows-working format confound (≤3% of rows); the d6 dip is clean addition; **plateau**
  shows-working is ~0% (sumprod 0.02%), so plateau boosts/Δ are NOT under-measured.
![B1(n) curve](../plots/b1curve.png)

**Power-gate `Δ(n,k)=B₁(n)−B₁(n/k)`.** Operate `n` on the no-degradation plateau `[n_sat, n_top]`
with `n/k ≥ n_lo` (divided prediction graded, not degenerate), and maximize Δ. **Every wide-plateau
level is feasible for k∈{2,4,8}** (the plateau is so wide that `n_lo·k ≪ n_top`). What differs is the
**Δ magnitude** (≈ plateau − B₁(n_lo)) and the **divided-prediction sensitivity** (= local slope of B₁
at `n/k`; HIGH ⇒ the divided reference is sensitive to where the measured B₁ curve sits). Best
operating point per level (k=8 shown; full k∈{2,4,8} in `results/b1curve_summary.md`):
| level | k=8 Δ (n*) | Δ_fix (n=8·n_lo) | div-slope pp/tok | σ (Δ_max/SE_cmp) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n15_d6` | +22.9 (n=201) | +23.5 | 2.9 (steep/sensitive) | 8.8 |
| `sumprod_t3_d2` | +18.3 (n=169) | +18.3 | 0.69 (gradual/robust) | 6.7 |
| `add_n12_d8` | +13.7 (n=25) | +14.0 | 2.1 | 6.1 |
| `add_n15_d8` | +9.5 (n=25) | +9.5 | 1.6 | 3.6 |
| `mul_a4_b4_hom` | +5.0 | (k=2 Δ_fix +0.3) | — | 2.3 (NO-GO) |

The separability comes from **high plateau vs low threshold value**, NOT a graded wide rise.
`add_n15_d8`'s Δ is smallest because its `n_lo≤3 tok` already sits mid-rise (B₁(3)=+7, ~half its +16
plateau). The **`Δ_fix`** column (Δ at the principled n=k·n_lo rule, not selected to maximize Δ)
de-biases the operating-point winner's curse — it confirms the addition/sumprod Δ (Δ_fix≈Δ_max) and
**exposes mul as NO-GO** (its max Δ=+6.4 collapses to Δ_fix=+0.3 at k=2 — a noise wiggle). `sumprod`'s
**gradual rise** (slope 0.69) keeps its divided prediction robust (Δ stable to ±5 curve-tok) and gives
**more informative (k,n) cells for the eventual α-fit**; `add_n15_d6`'s steep step gives the largest Δ
but a more curve-sensitive divided reference (mitigated: with dots its rise is gradual; and we measured
the curve densely). ![power gate](../plots/b1curve_gate.png)

**Power statement.** The main experiment judges measured `B_k` against TWO estimated references
(`B₁(n)`, `B₁(n/k)`), so the comparison SE ≈ √3·`sqrt(2p(1−p)/m)` ≈ 2.6pp at m=2000 (not 1.5pp).
Then d6 (8.8σ), sumprod (6.7σ), d12 (6.1σ) are **comfortably resolvable**; d8 (~3.6σ) is marginal-GO;
mul (~2.3σ, + degradation, Δ_fix≈0) is the lone **NO-GO**.

**VERDICT: GO** (see the boxed verdict near the top). The narrow-rising-region risk flagged by
Segments 0–1 turns out **not to block the experiment**: although 8-digit addition saturates in ~7
tokens, the plateau is wide, so you operate `n` on the plateau and put `n/k` at the threshold.
**Recommend running ≥2 levels**, prioritising a **graded-rise** level (`sumprod`, or `add_n15_d6`
with dots) as the headline α-fit primary (graded ⇒ a well-constrained α and a clean parallel/divided/
null 3-way call), with `add_n15_d6`(counting)/`add_n12_d8` as max-signal confirmatory levels — see the
boxed verdict's recommendation. Final operating-point lock-in + per-cell power analysis are a later
segment.

**Open mechanistic puzzle (flagged for the later mechanism segment):** *why* does 8-digit addition
saturate almost instantly (n_sat=7 tok) with **no** small-n dip, while the *easier* 6-digit `add_n15_d6`
(and mul/sumprod) **dip then step up** much later (n_sat 29–81 tok)? Harder-per-operand problems
benefiting from filler *faster* and without a dip is counter-intuitive; the dip is a genuine
high-digit carry disruption (verified) but the digit-width dependence of n_sat/the dip is not
explained. Not load-bearing for the gate (we measured all curves directly), but the recommended
levels' shapes hinge on it, so it is named here rather than left implicit.

**Harder-difficulty probe (diagnostic):** making addition harder does **not** reliably widen the
rising region — `add_n20_d8`/`add_n20_d10` fall to the **floor** (base 10–16%) where the boost
**vanishes** (no headroom); only the still-mid-band `add_n15_d10` boosts (+15–20pp, rise to ~81 tok
then mild degradation). So the wide plateau is tied to **boost magnitude / operand-digit structure**,
not raw difficulty. This informs the (now-skipped) conditional Segment 3. ![probe](../plots/harder_probe.png)

**Consolidated replication (fresh items, ~300 tok, bootstrap CI, m=1000):** d8 +16.1, d6 +30.5,
d12 +22.4, mul +10.6, sumprod +24.5 pp — reconfirms the headline effect on the *finalized* datasets
and *fresh* items (close to the Segment-1 calibration-sample gate boosts).
![replication](../plots/b1curve_replication.png)

Full per-cell tables, the gate CSV, and data-inspection notes: `results/b1curve_summary.md`,
`results/b1curve_{points,gate}.csv`, `results/inspect_b1curve.md`; chronology in `progress_log.md`
(Segment 2).

## Segment 3: open-model selection + filler-boost replication (cross-model robustness + cheap pilot platform)

> ⚑ **Headline correction during review (read this):** the FIRST-pass open-model boost (+11pp, unpinned
> OpenRouter routing) was **~half a provider-quantization artifact**. OpenRouter routes each call across
> providers serving DeepSeek-V3-0324 at different quantizations: a **high-accuracy cluster**
> (DeepInfra/ModelRun/SiliconFlow ≈ 54–55%) and a **low-accuracy cluster** (AtlasCloud/Novita/GMICloud
> ≈ 22–29%, aggressive quants that wreck multi-digit arithmetic). Critically the provider mix was
> **size-dependent** — the bad cluster served ~45% of the **n=0 baseline** calls but only ~20% of the
> filler cells — which **depressed the baseline and inflated the boost**. Restricting routing to the
> homogeneous good cluster (`provider={"only":["deepinfra","modelrun","siliconflow"]}`) removes the
> confound (and collapses truncation 3%→0.01%, since the bad providers also produced the pathological
> outputs): the clean boost is **+5.7pp**, not +11pp, and **counting ≈ dots** (the "dots>counting" of the
> first pass was also an artifact). The clean numbers below are the headline; **provider pinning is
> mandatory for the Segment-8 open-model main experiment.**

Goal (per `OVERALL_PLAN.md` §5 Segment 3): pick a recent **open-weights** model on OpenRouter that shows
the filler boost with **genuine no-CoT** (reasoning OFF; prefill working), to (1) carry cheap iteration for
the upcoming k-question design pilot and (2) provide a later cross-model robustness check. Infra:
`llm_openrouter.py` (cached OpenRouter wrapper mirroring `llm.py`) + a **model-agnostic harness**
(`harness.answer_problem(..., caller=...)`) so the SAME message-building/parsing/scoring drives Opus AND
the open model. `n` is always in **this model's realized tokens** (re-measured from `prompt_tokens`, NOT
the Anthropic mapping).

**Selected: DeepSeek-V3-0324** (`deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`). Rationale — among 9 surveyed models it
has the **cleanest enforceable genuine no-CoT** AND the **only clean reproducible boost**, and is fast /
cheap / reliable (the best "cheap iteration platform"). The survey is **config-fair**: every viable
