content.text:
candidate was also tested at DeepSeek's winning config (`add_n4_d10` 4× 10-digit addition + dots), where
GLM-4.6 is null (−3.0, pinned), Qwen3-235B null/negative (−1.2), and **Kimi-K2-0905 collapses** (≈−55pp) —
so DeepSeek's boost is genuinely model-specific, not a config artifact. (Caveat: `add_n4_d10` is DeepSeek's
mid-band but puts GLM/Qwen/Kimi at 64–79% baselines — less headroom; the fair survey justifies *selecting*
DeepSeek over them at a common config, not that they could never boost at their own mid-bands.)
![open-model selection survey](../plots/openrouter_selection.png)

**Genuine no-CoT verified (the make-or-break check; `results/inspect_openrouter_deepseek_add_n4_d10.md`,
clean good-cluster data).** Reasoning disabled via OpenRouter `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":False}}`;
prefill (final assistant message `"<filler> The answer is"`) is **honored** (verified by a neutral
continue-this-text probe AND by the model continuing the seeded text with the integer). On 23k add_n4_d10
outputs: **0% populated reasoning/reasoning_content field**, **0.026% letters-before-the-first-digit**
(0.0% at the n=0 baseline), median output **6 tokens**, **0.01% truncation**, scoring 100% consistent. The
rare pre-answer text is a dots-filler "the answer is hidden in the dots… just kidding!" joke that still
parses to the right integer. First-integer parse is the correct conservative no-CoT scorer; the open-model
analysis uses a **separator-tolerant** variant (`or_analysis.parse_answer_open`) that handles the rare
comma/space/narrow-space/period thousands-separator outputs DeepSeek emits under filler (Opus's `parse_answer`
is untouched). (NB the inflated first-pass data had ~3% truncation, which was a LOW-quant-provider artifact,
not real CoT — it vanishes on the good cluster.)

**Mid-band difficulty FOR the open model (reuse the generator, not a full Segment-1 pass).** The Opus
canonical levels (8-digit, 12–16 operands) are at the **FLOOR** for the weaker open model — DeepSeek scores
**0%** on `add_n15_d8` (fresh items, `results/orboost_deepseek_canonfloor.jsonl`). A calibration sweep
(`run_or_calibrate.py`, addition `#operands × #digits` on fresh `offset≥500` items, good cluster) found a
clean monotonic difficulty knob and a mid-band; the chosen primary is **`add_n4_d10`** (4× 10-digit
addition, good-cluster base **52%**), a generator level (`OR_SEED=7777`, distinct from the canonical seed).
The boost is **operating-point-specific**: clearly positive only at `add_n4_d10`, and ~null at
`add_n4_d12` (43%), `add_n5_d10` (35%), `add_n5_d8` (63%) — so the "bigger answer → bigger boost"
intuition does **not** hold cleanly (d12 has bigger answers but is null), and `add_n4_d10` was partly
selected for showing the boost (a winner's-curse caveat; mitigated by the logit-robustness, error
structure, and config-fair survey below).

**Realized-token mapping (re-measured from DeepSeek's `prompt_tokens`).** DOTS ≈ **1.0 token/item**; COUNTING
≈ **2.0 tokens/item** (the two filler types differ, and dots differs from Anthropic's ≈2/item — so the mapping
MUST be re-measured per model, which the runner does per-row).

**Replicated paired boost + rising-region/dynamic-range check** (`run_openrouter.py`, paired per item, n=1000/
cell, bootstrap 95% CI over items, prefill, reasoning OFF, **good provider cluster**; plateau-averaged from
100 realized tok; full per-level table `results/or_deepseek_clean_summary.md`, robustness
`results/or_deepseek_robustness.txt`):

| level | base | filler | raw boost (pp) [CI] | logit boost [CI] |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **`add_n4_d10`** (PRIMARY) | 52.1% | **counting** | **+5.2 [+2.3, +8.0]** | **+0.21 [+0.09, +0.32]** |
| **`add_n4_d10`** | 52.1% | **dots** | **+5.7 [+3.1, +8.3]** | **+0.23 [+0.12, +0.34]** |
| `add_n4_d12` | 43.3% | counting / dots | +0.2 / −1.8 (null) | +0.01 / −0.07 |
| `add_n5_d10` | 34.6% | counting / dots | −1.6 / +1.5 (null) | −0.07 / +0.07 |
| `add_n5_d8` | 62.9% | counting / dots | +0.9 / +2.2 (null) | +0.04 / +0.10 |

At `add_n4_d10` both filler types give a comparable **+5.2–5.7pp** boost that survives on the **logit scale**
(CI excludes 0; plateau = cells with ≥100 realized tokens, token-matched across filler types). The rising
region is a **small-n dip** (−~5pp at 5–11 realized tok — the same carry-disruption dip Opus showed on
`add_n15_d6`) then a rise to a **plateau by ~100–200 tok** (no degradation out to ~600 tok) — a usable
dynamic range. **Counting ≈ dots** here (the first-pass "dots>counting" was a provider artifact). `add_n4_d12`
is null even though its answers are bigger. The boost is **identical under the standard `parse_answer` and
the separator-tolerant `parse_answer_open`** (+5.16/+5.70 both ways) — not a parsing artifact.
![DeepSeek-V3-0324 filler boost](../plots/openrouter_boost_deepseek.png)

**Not a residual provider-mix artifact (the airtight check = a single-provider run).** Routing is per-cell
within the good cluster, so the cluster boost could in principle be a residual within-cluster mix-shift
(the three providers' *baseline* accuracies do differ — ModelRun 48%, DeepInfra 51%, SiliconFlow 57% — a
~9pp spread; their *pooled* 54–56% looks homogeneous only because it is boost-contaminated). Two checks
rule out inflation: (i) a **properly-powered SINGLE-provider run (DeepInfra-pinned, n=1500, NO mix at all)
reproduces the boost: dots +5.0pp [+2.6, +7.3], counting +3.9pp [+1.0, +6.7]** — both CIs exclude 0 and
match the cluster (`results/orboost_singleDeepInfra_deepseek_*_pwr.jsonl`); (ii) weighting the per-size
provider mix by each provider's **baseline-only** accuracy, the mix-implied baseline accuracy **decreases**
with filler size (52.1%→49.9% dots) — i.e. the plateau cells are served by *lower*-baseline providers, so
the size-dependent mix works **against** the boost (conservative), it cannot manufacture it. Net: the
honest magnitude is **~+4–6pp** (cluster +5.2–5.7, single-provider +3.9–5.0), and the *existence* of a
genuine effect is solid.

**The boost is genuine arithmetic computation (not reduced give-up or a format/length artifact).** Error
structure at `add_n4_d10` (`analyze_or_robust.py`): of baseline-WRONG items, **81% are carry/precision
near-misses**, 19% far, and **0% give-ups** (no tiny/None answers); filler at the plateau fixes near-misses
at **~39%** but far-misses at only **~17%** (near−far gap +15–29pp, bootstrap 95% CI excludes 0). The
strongest arguments that this is real arithmetic and not a generic length/format bump are the **0% give-ups**
(there is nothing to "un-give-up"; filler isn't just making the model answer rather than abstain), the
**level-specificity** (null at the SAME providers on `add_n4_d12`/`add_n5_d10`/`add_n5_d8` — a generic
length effect would help everywhere), and the **dip-then-rise shape** (a length artifact wouldn't dip at
small n then recover). The near−far gap is *consistent with* carry-precision repair but is weaker evidence
on its own (a generic accuracy gain also flips near-misses more easily). Mirrors the Opus error-structure
result.

**What TRANSFERS to Opus vs what is model-specific (`OVERALL_PLAN.md` §3.7).**
- **Transfers (design/scoring/format):** the prompt format (assistant prefill + `"The answer is"` lead-in +
  first-integer scoring; `parse_answer`/`parse_answer_robust`/`parse_answer_open`), the model-agnostic
  harness, and the upcoming **k-question reveal-by-index design** (this open model is its cheap iteration
  platform). (NB: "dots-as-a-lever" is an **Opus-specific curve-shape** finding — on DeepSeek counting ≈ dots
  in both magnitude and rise shape, so dots is **not** a lever here.)
- **Model-specific (does NOT transfer):** the **operating point / mid-band difficulty** (DeepSeek = 4×10-digit
  @ 52%, vs Opus 15×8-digit @ 55%), the **n-grid**, the **realized-token mapping** (dots 1/item, counting
  2/item), and the **boost magnitude** (DeepSeek ~+6pp vs Opus +16–30pp — much smaller, expected for a weaker
  open model). Placement = **prefill** (verified honored; no prompt-only fallback needed). **NEW transfer
  lesson:** for any OpenRouter open model, **pin/allowlist a homogeneous-quantization provider cluster** —
  unpinned multi-provider routing can manufacture a spurious size-dependent accuracy gap.

**Survey (9 models; why the others were not chosen).** Selected = DeepSeek-V3-0324. *Viable (valid no-CoT)
but no usable boost — measured at the SAME fair config (`add_n4_d10` + dots), provider-controlled:*
`glm-4.6` (−3.0pp, null — **pinned to DeepInfra** because its pool also had a low-quant provider, AtlasCloud
34.6%), `qwen3-235b-a22b-2507` (−1.2pp, null/neg — its providers are quant-homogeneous 69–76%, so unpinned
is clean; genuine clean no-CoT, so the non-positive is real, confirming the harness works), `kimi-k2-0905`
(**−55pp — filler COLLAPSES it** at d10; its providers are homogeneous ~40% so the collapse is clean, not an
empties artifact; also SLOW + ~10% empty/0-token responses on its pool). *Disqualified (genuine no-CoT not
enforceable / unavailable):*
`deepseek-v3.2` (prefill **unreliable** across OpenRouter providers + **CoT leakage** 2.8% text-before-answer
+ one 7073-token essay), `gpt-oss-120b` (reasoning **mandatory**, cannot be disabled), `llama-3.3-70b`
(echoes the working → first integer is an operand, breaks first-integer no-CoT), `qwen3-max` (negative +
slow), `kimi-k2` base (50 RPM cap + provider 400s). (`qwen3-max`/`grok-4-fast` are closed-API, not
open-weights — included only as availability probes.) Per-candidate inspection evidence in
`results/inspect_openrouter_*.md` (+ `inspect_openrouter_disqualified_extra.md` for gpt-oss/llama).

**Caveats / limitations (carry forward).** (1) The boost is **modest (~+6pp)** and much smaller than Opus,
and **narrow** (clear only at `add_n4_d10`; null at the other 3 levels tried) — fine for a cross-model check
and design pilot (the boost reproduces with genuine no-CoT and has a usable rising region), but the later
open-model main experiment (Segment 8) must set its own operating point, **pin a provider**, and may have a
tighter (or absent) parallel/divided separation than Opus. (2) `add_n4_d10` was partly selected for showing
the boost (winner's curse); the logit-robustness, the carry-near-miss error structure, and the config-fair
survey (others null/collapse at the same config) argue it is genuine, but Segment 8 should pre-register the
level on fully fresh items. (3) Dots filler occasionally makes DeepSeek joke about "the answer hidden in the
dots" before the integer (≤0.03% on the clean cluster; still parses) — a benign dots-content artifact, but a
heads-up that **dots may behave differently inside a multi-question (k>1) prompt** (validate in the Segment-4
pilot). (4) The k-question prompt format was **NOT** built here (next segment); no Opus-grade power-gate/α-fit
on the open model (reserved for Segment 8). Full chronology + per-candidate data inspection: `progress_log.md`
(Segment 3); summaries `results/or_deepseek_clean_summary.md`, `results/or_deepseek_robustness.txt`.

## Segment 4: the k-question design + pilot (the experimental apparatus, de-risked)

Built the **k-question reveal-by-index harness** (`kharness.py`, `run_kquestion.py`, `kanalysis.py`)
that the main experiment (Segment 6) will run at scale, and **piloted it cheaply** (DeepSeek-heavy +
Opus confirm) to prove it behaves as the science requires. Model-agnostic (SAME builder/parser/scoring
drives Opus and DeepSeek). All numbers + data inspection: `results/kquestion_summary.md`,
`results/inspect_kquestion_deepseek.md`.

**The validated prompt format (multi-turn chat; n=0 keeps the same 4 turns with an EMPTY filler turn
— both APIs accept it, so structure is identical across filler sizes → clean paired boost):**
1. **user** — encouraging framing (“Here are k problems Q1…Qk; in a moment I’ll ask you exactly ONE
   at random — be ready for any; do not answer yet”) + the k labelled questions. (reveal-**before**
   names the target index here, before the filler; framing knob: told [default] / not_told.)
2. **assistant** — the FILLER (counting/dots), BEFORE the reveal so reveal-after is genuinely
   undirected (empty string at n=0).
3. **user** — the REVEAL by INDEX only; never restates the question text. **ADOPTED default = a MINIMAL
   reveal** (`“Now answer Q3:”`, with the “no work…” instruction moved up to turn 1) — a verbose reveal
   turn sits between filler and answer and SUPPRESSES the boost (see Apparatus FIX below);
   `--reveal-style {minimal,full}` is a knob.
4. **assistant** prefill — `"The answer to Q3 is"` (no trailing whitespace) → integer is the first
   content token. The k items are i.i.d. fresh draws (`offset>=500`, deterministic per (level,k,inst)
   so conditions/sizes are paired). We read ONE randomly-revealed answer per prompt.

**Mechanics — all validated (data-inspection: `results/inspect_kquestion_deepseek.md`):**
- **(2e) Genuine no-CoT holds in the k-structure.** Across **~56k** k-prompt outputs (all runs incl.
  minimal-reveal; stronger detector in `analyze_nocot_kquestion.py` that also catches DIGIT-INITIAL
  shows-working — restating the expression then computing, which the naive letters-before-digit metric
  missed): **genuine no-CoT violations = 11 (0.020%)** (the model showed working so the first integer is
  an OPERAND; all scored wrong, conservative) — **6 of these are the `sumprod`+**dots`** show-work quirk
  at k>1 (counting-filler runs are ~0.005%; addition levels 0). A broader ~0.05% are benign “answer-first + chatty epilogue” (DeepSeek emits the
  integer as the first token, then a `(Note: …)` — no-CoT preserved, parsed correctly). 0% populated
  reasoning field, ~0% truncation, median output 6–8 tokens. (Opus levels are completely clean: 0
  violations across ~10k rows.) *Caveat:* the “genuine” classifier keys on the first integer being an
  operand, so the true worst-case CoT rate is bounded by the **broad-flag rate (≤0.13%)**, not 0.009%.
- **(2b) The model answers the ASKED question.** Among prompts where the emitted integer matches
  *some* question’s answer, **100.0%** match the **asked** one (offset_hist = {0: all}); **0%** ever
  match a non-asked (distractor) question; it is NOT just always answering Q1/last. (Both models, all k.)
- **(2c) Index→question recall is trivial.** Recall is essentially perfect (the model never answers the
  wrong question). The reveal-by-restatement diagnostic is +9–12pp higher at n=0, but that gap is a
  **recency/salience** effect (restating the operands adjacent to the answer; the gain is largest for
  the primacy-disadvantaged Q1, +20–28pp, and small for already-good positions) — **not** a recall
  failure (which is exactly why the real design uses index-only).
- **(2d) Scoring works.** Across all result files (n≈56k): **0** disagreements between the
  conservative (`parse_answer`/`parse_answer_open`) and robust scorers; the rare DeepSeek balk
  (“not computable…”, 0.01%) is correctly marked wrong; DeepSeek’s benign `:\n<int>` / `: **<int>**`
  separator formatting parses correctly; **no** “answer-in-the-dots” pathology in the k-prompt.

**⚑ Headline finding 1 — the n=0 baseline RISES with k (the plan assumed it DROPS), driven by the
serial-position effect averaged over the random reveal.** n=0 accuracy **rises** with k: DeepSeek
54→62→70→72%; Opus add_n12_d8 84→87→92→96%; add_n15_d8 61→72→86→90%. **What this is (carefully):** the
k-trend is essentially the **position effect (finding 5) averaged over the uniform revealed position** —
e.g. Opus add_n12_d8 k=8 = (Q1 69% + 7×~100%)/8 ≈ 96% reconstructs the reported number. Two distinct
effects combine: (a) positions ≥2 are much better than position 1 — the jump is from **one preceding
question** then it *saturates*, mirroring the fast-saturating B₁(n) curve → **consistent with
preceding-questions-acting-as-filler**; (b) position 1 actually **DEGRADES** with k on Opus (k=1 has no
distractors at 84%, but k=8 Q1=69%) — the most distant question is hurt by following distractors /
distance. The random-reveal average rises because the upper/ceiling positions dominate the mixture.
Supporting the filler interpretation (not proving it): the per-k trend follows the B₁(n) shape —
**dip-then-rise** for carry-fragile `add_n15_d6` (60→41→51→53%, mirroring its small-n dip), monotonic for
fast addition, ~flat for gradual `sumprod` (42→42→38→34%, whose ~10–40-tok distractors sit below its
n_sat=81). It is **not a difficulty or routing artifact**: revealed-item difficulty is identical across
k (uniform pool draw), it reproduces on Opus (direct API, no routing), and on DeepSeek it holds
**within each pinned provider** (SiliconFlow 59→67→72→79%; stable mix). **Consequence for lock-in:** no
k lands near the FLOOR (good); but fast addition **CEILINGS** at high k (add_n12_d8 k=8=96%, positions
2–8 at ~100% with tight CIs — additive-accuracy comparison INVALID there). `sumprod_t3_d2` stays
**graded mid-band** across k AND across positions (k=8 positions span 18–56%, none ceilinged) — the
desirable profile. ![n=0 baseline vs k](../plots/kq_baseline_vs_k.png)

**⚑ Headline finding 2 — a VERBOSE reveal turn SUPPRESSES the boost (the diagnostic that led to the
apparatus fix below).** With the original FULL reveal turn, the multi-turn k=1 boost was suppressed
level-dependently (and d6+dots collapsed to ≈0). On the SAME 300 items, single-turn (Seg-2 layout,
filler adjacent to the answer) vs multi-turn k=1 with the **FULL reveal** (filler separated from the
answer by the verbose reveal turn) — this is what motivated the **minimal reveal** (Apparatus FIX,
next paragraph, which RESTORES all of these):
| level (filler) | single-turn n0→boost | multi-turn FULL-reveal n0→boost | verdict (full reveal) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n12_d8` (cnt sz100) | 72% +20pp | 80% **+13pp** | asymptote PRESERVED, baseline pre-inflated |
| `add_n15_d8` (cnt sz100) | 53% +15pp | 64% **+4pp** | asymptote preserved, boost pre-consumed |
| `add_n15_d6` (cnt sz200) | 48% +32pp | 55% **+14pp** | asymptote SUPPRESSED (carry-fragile) |
| `add_n15_d6` (**dots** sz400) | 48% +25pp | 55% **+3pp** | dots boost ~GONE — **loud flag** |
| `sumprod_t3_d2` (cnt sz100) | 35% +30pp | 42% **+27pp** | boost RETAINED — gradual rise |

All differences are paired-bootstrap significant except sumprod at sz100 (Δ(multi−single), matched to
the table rows: d12-sz100 −7.7pp [−13.2,−2.4]; d8-sz100 −11.2 [−18.0,−4.3]; d6-cnt-sz200 −17.9
[−25.4,−10.4]; **d6-dots-sz400 −21.4 [−28.6,−14.3]**; **sumprod-sz100 −3.9 [−11.3,+3.9] = NOT sig
(preserved at the ~200-tok peak)**; sumprod-sz200 −14.4 [−21.8,−6.7]; full table in
`results/kquestion_summary.md`). Two mechanisms: (a) the **structural scaffolding** (framing + reveal turn, separating
question from answer by ~40 tok) acts as filler that **pre-inflates the n=0 baseline +6–12pp**, and on
fast-saturating levels (d8/d12, n_sat=7 tok) pushes it to/near the plateau → little measurable explicit
boost left; (b) on **carry-fragile d6** the intervening reveal turn **suppresses the asymptote**
(adjacency partly recovers it: a diagnostic with filler adjacent to the answer gives +20pp vs +14pp
separated; d12 is identical separated-vs-adjacent). A **gradual-rise** level (`sumprod`, n_sat=81 tok)
escapes BOTH problems at the peak: the scaffolding doesn’t saturate it (so explicit filler still buys
+27pp) and it is not carry-fragile.

**The IN-STRUCTURE B₁(n) curve is what the lock-in actually has.** Mapping multi-turn k=1 B₁(n) finely
with the (pre-adoption) FULL reveal (`kq_opus_instructure_*`): sumprod peaked +27.7pp at ~200 tok then
declined. **[CORRECTED by Segment 5 — see the Segment-5 section: this was a FULL-reveal measurement.
With the ADOPTED MINIMAL reveal (m=1000), sumprod peaks +37 @150-200 tok; on MATCHED items the
in-structure boost is NOT narrower than single-turn (only ~+5pp higher, baseline not inflated); the
real shape feature is sumprod's peak-then-DECLINE. add_n15_d10 is DISQUALIFIED (ceilings under k).]**
![in-structure B1(n)](../plots/kq_instructure_b1.png)

**⚑ Apparatus FIX (task-2a “STOP and rework” gate — we DID rework it).** The structure suppression is
NOT intrinsic — it is largely caused by the **verbose reveal turn** (~15 tok of “Do not show any
work…” boilerplate sitting between the filler and the answer). Replacing it with a **MINIMAL reveal
turn** (`“Now answer Q3:”`, with the no-work instruction moved up to turn 1 — still reveal-by-index, no
restatement) **recovers the boost to ~single-turn levels on EVERY level and filler type**, on the SAME
items: sumprod +27→**+36**, add_n12_d8 +13→**+22**, add_n15_d8 +4→**+15**, add_n15_d6 cnt +14→**+28**,
and crucially **add_n15_d6 dots +3→**+24 (so d6+dots is REVIVED). It also de-inflates the n=0 baseline
back to ~single-turn (sumprod 42→33%, d8 64→51%, d12 80→72%), restoring measurable headroom. **We
adopted the minimal reveal as the apparatus default** (`run_kquestion.py --reveal-style minimal`;
verified it preserves genuine no-CoT [0–1 violations/300] and answers-asked [frac_asked|some=1.000]).
The gate’s intent is met by FIXING the structure, not by documenting a limitation.
![reveal-style recovery](../plots/kq_reveal_recovery.png) **Lock-in implication:** re-measure B₁(n)
IN-structure with the minimal reveal (it ≈ the single-turn boost at k=1), and prefer a level that stays
mid-band across k while keeping positions off the ceiling (sumprod is best; d6+dots is viable again,
NOT dead). (Model-dependent: on **DeepSeek** even the FULL-reveal interaction is the OPPOSITE — the multi-turn structure *raises*
the asymptote, single-turn +6.7pp→58% vs multi-turn k=1 **+12pp→66%** on identical items
(`scripts/ds_structcheck.py`) — because DeepSeek’s small single-turn boost leaves room the richer chat
format unlocks. So the boost SURVIVES on both models; the *direction* of the structure effect depends
on how much of the boost the single-turn layout already extracts.)
![single vs multi-turn boost](../plots/kq_structcompare.png)

**(5) Question-position effect — strong PRIMACY disadvantage (clean controlled sweeps, CIs).** At fixed
k, n=0 accuracy depends on revealed position: Q1 is much worse; the lift saturates after ~1 preceding
question. DeepSeek k=8 sweep (n=250/pos): Q1=59% vs Q2–Q8 70–79%. Opus add_n12_d8 k=8 sweep
(n=150/pos): **Q1=69%[61,77] vs Q2–Q8 99–100%** (extreme/ceilinged). Opus `sumprod_t3_d2` k=8:
**graded 18–56%** (Q1=18%[12,24] primacy + Q6–Q8 49–56% recency) — NO position at the ceiling, the
desirable profile for the headline level. Because B_k averages over a random revealed position, the
lock-in must **balance positions** (or model position explicitly). ![position](../plots/kq_position.png)

**(4) reveal-after vs reveal-before — sane, distinguishable, and the positive control works on the
recommended level (adopted minimal-reveal apparatus).** reveal-before (directed) is consistently ≥
reveal-after (undirected). **On `sumprod_t3_d2` (the recommended headline level), minimal reveal**:
reveal-before k=4 boost **+16.5 to +21pp** vs reveal-after k=4 **−3 to −6pp** — a clean, large directed
positive control where the undirected one is flat/negative; at k=8 before (+1.5 to +7pp) is still >
after (−9 to −14pp) but weaker (less headroom once distractors pre-saturate). Also confirmed on the
full-reveal apparatus and other levels (Opus `add_n15_d6` k=4 before +13 vs after +5; DeepSeek k=4
before +4 vs after −4). So the positive control is validated **on the level we actually recommend**.
Both conditions are correctly built (reveal-before states the index in turn 1, reveal-after only in
turn 3). ![reveal-after vs before](../plots/kq_revealbefore.png)

**(7) Pilot `B_k(n)` (sanity + variance — NOT the result; adopted minimal-reveal apparatus).** The
pipeline produces sensible, non-degenerate boosts: reveal-after **k=1 has a large boost** (Opus sumprod
**+36pp**; DeepSeek +6pp), while reveal-after **k>1 collapses to ≈0/negative** (sumprod k=4/8
−6/−9pp; DeepSeek similar). **Why k>1 is ≈0/negative is partly a measurement confound, not purely the
science** (carry-forward for Seg 5/7): a *position-resolved* B_k at k=8 (sumprod) shows filler **helps
the primacy position ~0/+1pp** (Q1, no preceding-question filler) but **strongly HURTS the late
positions** (Q6–Q8 −16 to −28pp — already pre-saturated by preceding-question filler, so more tokens
over-saturate/interfere). So the aggregate reveal-after k>1≈0 mixes an undirectedness penalty AND
per-position pre-saturation — the **reveal-before control disambiguates** (sumprod k=4 before **+16.5**
vs after −3), and the lock-in must **balance position** and prefer a level whose per-k baseline does NOT
pre-saturate (sumprod). **Variance for Segment-5 sizing:** per-instance paired-boost SE ≈ **3.3pp at
n=250**, ≈ **2.0pp at n=600** (random-reveal; conservatively includes position-mixing variance);
resolving a between-regime Δ to a ~2pp comparison-SE needs ~1500–2000 paired instances/cell (rough; per‑cell SE varies a lot with baseline). ![pilot B_k](../plots/kq_pilot_bk.png)

**What this hands the lock-in (Segment 5) — concrete:** (1) **Re-measure the FULL in-structure B₁(n)
curve** (NOT the Seg-2 single-turn curves — they over-state the boost and the plateau width); the
in-structure window is tighter (minimal-reveal sumprod B₁ peaks +36 @~200t, sustains ~+30 to 400t).
(2) Prefer a **gradual-rise level that stays mid-band across k AND keeps positions off the ceiling**:
`sumprod_t3_d2` is the leading candidate (minimal-reveal k=1 +36pp, flat 34% across k, graded positions
18–56%, reveal-before positive control validated); **`add_n15_d6`** (counting +28 / dots +25, stays
mid-band 50–56% across k — but carry-fragile/dip) and **`add_n15_d10`** (flat ~+15) are alternatives —
all should get a full minimal-reveal in-structure curve before locking. (3) **`d6+dots` is VIABLE
again** with the minimal reveal (the “dots dies” result was a verbose-reveal artifact; dots recovers to
+24–25pp); sumprod is still preferred (flatter cross-k baseline, graded positions), but dots is NOT
disqualified. **Dots filler caveat (mild):** on the multiplication-heavy `sumprod` at k>1, dots
occasionally (~1%, vs ~0.03% for counting) makes the model restate-and-compute the products (show-work,
conservatively scored wrong) — so **prefer COUNTING filler on sumprod** (or monitor the no-CoT rate);
dots is clean on the addition levels. (At k>1 reveal-after the dots boost is ≈0/neg just like counting
— d6/sumprod k=4 dots −6 to −14pp — i.e. the undirected k>1 collapse is filler-type-independent.)
(4) Pick per-question difficulty hard enough that distractor-as-filler doesn’t ceiling k=8 (fast
addition d8/d12 ceiling; sumprod/d6 stay mid-band).
(5) **Balance revealed positions** (strong primacy disadvantage; pick a level whose positions stay off
the ceiling — sumprod 18–56% does, d12 does not). (6) Do the analysis on the **logit scale** as well as
additive (per OVERALL_PLAN §2; the n=0 baseline shifts a lot with k and can near the ceiling). (7)
*Consider a revealed-item-fixed (paired-across-k) design* — draw ONE revealed item, vary the
surrounding distractors and balance its position — to control revealed-item difficulty exactly across k
and cut the per-cell sample requirement (current draws are distribution-matched, not item-paired across
k). The apparatus itself is validated and ready; what remains is operating-point selection (Segment 5).

## Segment 5: in-structure B₁(n) curve + pre-saturation confound + design lock-in
The pre-experiment segment. Re-measured the **in-structure** B₁(n) curve (multi-turn, **minimal-reveal**,
k=1, Opus 4.5, fresh items, **m=1000**), characterized the **pre-saturation/position confound**,
re-confirmed the gate **in-structure** (3-way), and **finalized + validated the confound-aware design**.
Consolidated verdict + design: `results/segment5_verdict.md`.

### The in-structure B₁(n) curve (the foundational re-measurement)
Fine token grid {0,24,50,76,100,150,200,300,400,600,800} (counting 2 tok/item, dots 1 tok/item).
| level (filler) | base | n_lo | peak boost | shape | off-ceiling at k=8? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **`sumprod_t3_d2` (counting)** | 36% | ~24 | **+37.2pp @150-200 tok** | gradual rise, PEAKS then DECLINES to +28 @800 | **YES (21-52%)** |
| `add_n15_d6` (counting) | 52% | ~24 | +28.9 @150 | rise to ~100 then flat | yes (25-73%), carry-fragile |
| `add_n15_d6` (dots) | 52% | ~25 | +28.9 @600 | gradual rise, plateau ~+26 | yes |
| `add_n15_d10` (counting) | 38% | ~24 | +22.8 @150 | FAST rise (~+22 by 50 tok), mild decline | **NO — ceilings** |
| `add_n12_d8` (counting) | 68% | ~24 | +25.0 @100 | fast rise, flat; acc≈92% | **NO — ceilings** |

**The Seg-4 "in-structure is NARROWER" claim was a FULL-reveal artifact — CORRECTED.** With the adopted
minimal reveal the in-structure curve is NOT narrower; the real shape difference is that sumprod
**PEAKS-then-DECLINES** (operate near the ~150-200 tok peak), whereas the addition levels keep a wider
flat plateau. **On MATCHED items** (`structcompare_opus.jsonl`, the clean comparison) the minimal-reveal
in-structure boost is only **~+5pp** above single-turn (+36 vs +31 @200 tok), NOT the +11pp the
cross-SAMPLE curves suggest, and the **n=0 baseline is identical** (35.0% vs 35.3% — minimal reveal does
NOT inflate the baseline; only the FULL reveal did, 41.7%). This modest structural offset is COMMON to
k=1 and k>1 cells → it **CANCELS in the gate Δ** and in the contrast. **Small-filler dip:** sumprod dips
−16pp at ~6 tok then rises (true **n_lo ≈ 12 tok**; the operating n=200 keeps n/8=25 tok safely above it).
![in-structure B1(n)](../plots/instructure_b1.png) ![narrowing](../plots/instructure_narrowing.png)

### The questions-as-filler / pre-saturation / position confound (the central interpretability risk)
**Mechanism (quantified).** The k distractor questions act as "preceding-context filler" for the
revealed question, but how much they SATURATE a position depends on the level's **filler-response
saturation speed**: FAST-saturating levels (d10, d12; B₁ saturates ≤50 tok) get their **late positions
pushed to the CEILING** (Q2-Q8 → 90-99% at k=8) → n=0 RISES with k (d10 38→84%, d12 68→96%) → the
aggregate B_k≈0 is a **NO-HEADROOM ARTIFACT**. The GRADUAL `sumprod_t3_d2` (n_sat~150 tok) is only
partially saturated → all k=8 positions stay 21-52% (OFF ceiling). **So `sumprod_t3_d2` is the ONLY
off-ceiling headline level**; `add_n15_d10` and `add_n12_d8` are **disqualified** (ceiling); `add_n15_d6`
stays off-ceiling but is carry-fragile.

**Strong primacy/recency at k=8, n=0:** early-listed Q1-Q5 ~21-26%, late-listed Q6-Q8 40-52% (recency:
later questions nearer the answer). **Position-resolved B_k (sumprod k=8, n=200):** filler helps
early/unsaturated positions ~0/+5pp but **HURTS recency-advantaged late positions** (Q5-Q8 −5 to −26pp)
— it ERASES the recency advantage (pulls Q6-Q8 from 40-52% down to ~25%). So the position-balanced
**aggregate B_k = −6.8pp (below null)** is a recency-erasure MIXTURE → the naive aggregate is confounded
(biased toward divided/null and below). ![position confound](../plots/position_confound.png)

[810 more lines in file. Use offset=1001 to continue.]

### In-structure 3-way power gate — VERDICT: GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2
Lead level `sumprod_t3_d2`+counting, operating **n=200 tok** (per-instance boost std≈0.54 →
SE_boost(m=2000)≈1.2pp):
| k | n/k | parallel B₁(n) | divided B₁(n/k) | Δ par−div (σ@m2000) | Δ div−null (σ) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 100 | +37 | +34.9 | +2.1 (σ1.2) | +34.9 (σ28.7) |
| 4 | 50 | +37 | +25.3 | +11.8 (σ6.9) | +25.3 (σ20.8) |
| 8 | 25 | +37 | +14.7 | +22.3 (σ13.0) | +14.7 (σ12.1) |

For **k=4 and k=8** the three hypotheses are cleanly separable (both Δ's ≫ per-cell SE). The binding
constraint — **Δ divided−null** (telling a GENUINE null from a small positive divided B_k) — is +15 to
+25pp, comfortably resolvable because sumprod's GRADUAL rise keeps divided well above 0 (a steep-step
level would pin it near 0). **k=2 is PARTIAL** (Δ par−div only +2.1 at the peak; recovers to +9.7 at a
lower operating n=100 — a per-k operating point, locked in Segment 6). Largest usable k = 8.
![3-way gate](../plots/instructure_gate.png)

### The reveal-before/after contrast (the headline disambiguator) + the likely answer
Because both reveal-before and reveal-after share the SAME k-question layout (identical recency), their
difference = the **filler×directedness INTERACTION**, neutralizing the recency-erasure confound. It is
large, consistent across ALL positions, and cross-model:
- **Opus sumprod:** reveal_after B_k −6.8 (k=8) / −6.9 (k=4); reveal_before **+12.1 / +14.8**; contrast
  **+18.9 / +21.7pp** (logit +0.83 / +0.92). Positive at EVERY position.
- **DeepSeek `add_n4_d10`:** reveal_after −1.6 / −1.5; reveal_before +6.5 / +9.6; contrast +8.0 / +11.1pp.

So the **likely headline is the `B_k≈0` undirected null**: the model uses filler when it KNOWS the
target (reveal_before clearly +) but does NOT bank computation it can't direct (reveal_after ≈0 on the
clean early positions). The design distinguishes a GENUINE null from a no-headroom artifact: sumprod is
off-ceiling AND the reveal-before positive control proves the filler-compute machinery works.
![directedness contrast](../plots/directedness_contrast.png)

### Finalized confound-aware design (validated)
- **Headline level + filler:** **`sumprod_t3_d2` + COUNTING** (off-ceiling, gradual, mid-band across k
  and positions; counting preferred — dots triggers ~1% show-work on sumprod). **Secondary consistency
  check:** `add_n15_d6`+counting (off-ceiling but carry-fragile, aggregate −16pp). `add_n15_d10` /
  `add_n12_d8` are OUT (ceiling under k).
- **Position handling:** position-BALANCE (sweep all positions equally) AND position-RESOLVED analysis
  (the position-balanced aggregate is a confounded mixture; the clean read is the early/unconfounded
  positions). Never let the random reveal silently mix wildly different positions.
- **Revealed-item-fixed (paired-across-k):** EVALUATED, implemented (`draw_instance_fixed`,
  `run_kquestion.py --design fixed_item`), validated (no-CoT 0%, boost survives exactly, contrast
  reproduces) — but **REJECTED as primary** because the variance-reduction rationale does NOT
  materialize (corr(B_k,B1)≈0 → paired SE ≈ unpaired SE). The i.i.d.-draw + position-balancing design
  (already difficulty-distribution-matched) is equally powered and simpler; the fixed design is kept as
  an exact-control robustness arm.
- **reveal-before/after contrast = PRIMARY interpretive tool**, run at k∈{4,8} **with an n=0 control**
  (so the contrast is the interaction, net of the n=0 retrieval advantage).
- **Analysis scales:** report **both additive AND logit** (the contrast is robust on both).
- **What a confident answer looks like:** parallel ⇒ reveal_after B_k≈B₁(n)≈+37 on early positions (NOT
  seen); divided ⇒ ≈+15-25 (NOT seen); **null ⇒ ≈0 on clean early positions WITH a strong reveal-before
  positive control** (SEEN) → a genuine undirected null, not a no-headroom artifact.

**Review-driven follow-ups (3 reviewers; details in `results/segment5_verdict.md` §7).** (1) The
directedness contrast **generalizes off sumprod**: `add_n15_d6` k=8 contrast **+32.8pp** (after −16.2 vs
before +16.6) — though d6's undirected B_k is negative everywhere (carry-fragile), the CONTRAST is large;
DeepSeek cross-model/cross-task corroborates. (2) Gate re-expressed in the **position-resolved primary
frame**: sumprod k=8 reveal-after EARLY positions (Q1-Q4) B_k = **+2.8pp [+1.2,+4.5]** — EXCLUDES
parallel (+37) AND divided (+14.7), consistent with the null; Segment 6 must size on the per-position
variance (~4-8× aggregate m), not the k=1 aggregate σ. (3) Scope: the null is the EARLY-POSITION read
(the position-balanced aggregate is net-disruptive, −6.8pp, = recency-erasure/interference); it is
OPERATIONAL ("undirected filler not banked under encouraging framing"), not a capability proof
(told-vs-not-told = Segment 10). Even DIRECTED, a k-prompt question gets only +12-18pp vs the lone +37
(context interference). (4) Under the likely NULL the headline statistic is the **3-way resolution +
contrast, NOT α** (α is ill-defined at B_k≈0) — for Segment-6 pre-registration.

**Validation pilots (all pass):** Opus fixed-item (`results/fixed_validate_opus.md`) + DeepSeek confirm
— no-CoT holds, boost survives, reveal-before > reveal-after, position-resolved boosts non-degenerate,
the gate cells behave as predicted. Data-inspection: `results/inspect_instructure.md`,
`results/inspect_fixed_{opus,ds}.md` (55k+ rows, 0% truncation/reasoning/CoT; ~0.05% sumprod
spaced-digit handled by robust scoring).

**`add_n15_d10` canonical? N/A (NOT adopted)** — rejected because it ceilings under k.

**Judgment-heavy choices (with rationale, LLM + research-reviewer checked):** (1) choosing the
off-ceiling level (sumprod) is sound, not cherry-picking the answer — it is the only level where B_k is
MEASURABLE (others have no headroom by construction); the reveal-before positive control guards against a
foregone null. (2) Position-resolve, don't aggregate the recency-erasure mixture. (3) Reject the
revealed-item-fixed primary on measured evidence (corr≈0). Full detail: progress_log.md (Segment 5).

## Segment 6: power analysis + operating-point lock-in + analysis pre-registration (the LOCKED plan)
The pre-experiment lock-in. Sized the cells on **position-resolved** variance, locked the grid, ran a
confirmation pilot, and wrote the formal **pre-registration** `results/preregistration.md` (the binding
plan the Segment-7 run + Segment-8 analysis follow verbatim). Did NOT run the full experiment or compute
the regime verdict. Full chronology: `progress_log.md` (Segment 6). Key plot:
![power lock-in](../plots/power_lockin.png)

### The locked grid (the one-line lock)
`sumprod_t3_d2`+counting (PRIMARY, off-ceiling/gradual) & `add_n15_d6`+counting (SECONDARY consistency);
k∈{1,2,4,8}; reveal-after & reveal-before each + n=0; `--reveal-mode sweep_positions` (position-balanced)
analyzed **position-resolved** with the **early-position pool = first ⌈k/2⌉ listed positions** as the
primary read (Q1-only co-reported). **Per-k operating points: n=100 tok (k=2), n=200 tok (k=4,8)** in
realized tokens (counting ≈2 tok/item), + a bounded secondary per-k n-sweep with shared n/k anchors
(n/k∈{25,50,100}) for the conditional collapse/α plots. k=1 reveal-after IS the structure-matched B₁(n)
reference. Revealed-item-fixed kept as a robustness arm. No 3rd level added (bounded probe:
`results/probe_2nd_level.md`).

### Per-cell sizing (POSITION-RESOLVED variance — the key correction) + budget
Sized on the per-instance paired-boost SD (~0.40 early k=8 → ~0.53 k=2; pilot-confirmed 0.39–0.56), NOT
the Segment-5 k=1 aggregate σ (a feasibility upper bound). Locked **m_inst = 3000/2000/1000/1000**
(k=1/2/4/8) → early-pool m ≈ 2000/2000/4000 rows, **cluster-bootstrapped over drawn instances** (cluster
SE ≈ naive SE, ratio 1.00–1.06 — each position reveals a different item). Resulting early-pool SE ≈
**1.18 / 1.12 / 0.61pp** (k=2/4/8) → every primary cell **excludes the divided reference by ≥10σ** and the
directedness **contrast excludes 0 by ~4.7σ (k=2, op-point n=100) / ~15σ (k=4,8)**. **Total Segment-7 budget ≈ 272,000
Opus calls ≈ $697 gross** (much already cached from Segment 5). Table: `results/power_table.md`.

### Confirmation pilot — all four validity guards PASS (`results/pilot_confirm.md`)
At the EXACT locked cells (Opus sumprod k=2 @ n=100 + a **FRESH** Opus k=4,8 @ n=200 confirm using inst
beyond the Segment-5 design max + DeepSeek `add_n4_d10` k∈{2,4,8}, provider-pinned): (a) per-position SD
matches the sizing assumption (0.39–0.56); (b) genuine no-CoT holds (≤0.045% truncation, 0% reasoning,
genuine-no-CoT violation ≤0.08% [proper operand-detector; the narrow letters-before-digit metric was
≤0.012%], ~0.4% show-work handled by the robust scorer); (c) the **reveal-before
positive control is significantly positive INCLUDING on the FRESH headline-cell subsample** (+16.3
[+13.2,+19.4]); (d) positions are off-ceiling (18–56%) and the cells resolve the references. **No guard
failed → the design is validated, not fixed.** Science note (not a guard, not the result): the headline
cell k=8 reveal-after early B_k is a small SIGNIFICANT positive (**+3.3pp full / +5.0pp [+1.7,+8.3]
fresh**) that EXCLUDES parallel/divided/0 → a **NEAR-NULL / WEAK-POSITIVE PARTIAL** picture (not exactly
0); the k=4 early pool is net-negative but Q2-driven (Q1-only ≈ 0). The decision rules classify each cell
in Segment 8.

### ⛑ Headline framing EVOLVED → pre-registered as an explicit deviation
Because the likely outcome is near-null, the OVERALL_PLAN's **α-fit (`n_eff=n/k^α`) is ILL-DEFINED at the
null** (no `n_eff` maps the positive B₁ curve onto ~0). So the pre-registration **PROMOTES** the
**position-resolved 3-way (parallel/divided/null) resolution + the reveal-before/after directedness
contrast** to PRIMARY (both additive AND logit), and **DEMOTES α to a conditional SECONDARY** (computed
only where B_k is meaningfully positive). This is an enrichment of the SAME reveal-after k×n data driven by
the Segment-5 pre-saturation discovery, pre-registered transparently (incl. the goalpost-moving exposure +
a mathematical defense) so the Segment-8 write-up is bulletproof. Two load-bearing caveats are stated: the
**parallel reference is unreachable in-prompt** (context interference caps even the directed boost at
~+17–20pp ≪ +37) and the **divided reference B₁(n/k) is interference-naive** (so "excludes divided" alone
≠ "null" — the null is carried by the contrast + the positive control). Decision rules are a
mutually-exclusive CI-based partition with a single headline-declaration template; full detail in
`results/preregistration.md`.

### Judgment-heavy choices (LLM + research-reviewer checked, fixed)
(1) Size on position-resolved (not aggregate) variance; cluster bootstrap over instances. (2) Per-k
operating points (n=100 for k=2) to recover the PARTIAL cell while keeping divided−null large. (3) Proceed
with sumprod+d6 — no 3rd level (harder sumprod variants floor; `sumprod_t2_d3` is off-ceiling+boosts but
floor-adjacent and not clearly better than d6; the directedness contrast already generalizes
cross-family/cross-model). (4) De-circularize the headline cell with a FRESH confirm. (5) Pre-register the
α-demotion as an explicit deviation. Reviews + fixes: `progress_log.md` (Segment 6).

## Segment 8: the pre-registered §5 regime + directedness verdict (the scientific deliverable)

Applied `results/preregistration.md` §5 **verbatim** to the Segment-7 data — **analysis-only, no new data,
no change to the locked grid/harness/scorers/pre-registration.** Machinery: `seg8_lib.py`
(+`analyze_seg8_verdict.py` → `results/seg8_verdict.md`, `seg8_regime_cells.csv`, `seg8_alpha_collapse.md`;
`plot_seg8.py` → the 5 figures). Inference unit = the drawn INSTANCE (cluster bootstrap, seed 0; 95%
descriptive CIs, 98.3%/z=2.39 Bonferroni for the 3-test confirmatory family). The comparison CI is
`(B_k−R̂) ± z·√(SE_Bk²+SE_R²)` with **SE_R the genuine cluster-bootstrap SE of the linearly-interpolated
k=1 reference** (K1Curve resamples the k=1 instances and interpolates within each resample → captures
sampling + interpolation; NOT 0). δ_add = 5pp (TOST equivalence, symmetric incl. null); δ_logit =
logit(p₀+0.05)−logit(p₀) at the cell's n=0 accuracy.

### ⭑ The single headline declaration (§5.8, instantiated)
> **At the headline cell (sumprod, k=8, n=200), the early-position reveal-after `B_k` is PARTIAL
> (sub-divided) — +2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7] additive, agreeing on the logit scale (+0.14 [+0.07,+0.21]) — and the
> directedness contrast is DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE (+16.2pp [+14.3,+18.3], with a significantly-positive
> reveal-before control +18.7 [+17.2,+20.3]), giving the combined interpretation "partial directed": a
> small undirected benefit that is ≪ the divided reference (+13.7) and ≪ the directed boost.** Qualifiers
> (§5.9): _not robust to held-out_ + _position-sensitive_ — on Q1-only (+1.2 [−1.1,+3.6]) and the fresh-only
> held-out subsample (+0.9 [−1.1,+2.9]) the regime reads **NULL** (both far below divided). So the precise
> **NULL-vs-PARTIAL** split is genuinely on the boundary and is not robust; **the robust conclusions are
> `B_k ≪ divided` (~10σ, SE_R-inclusive) and DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE.** Both NULL and PARTIAL map to the same combined
> "directed" reading, so the falsifying outcomes (DIVIDED / PARALLEL) are excluded regardless.

![regime verdict](../plots/seg8_regime_verdict.png)

### §5.1 regime classification (additive AND logit; position-resolved early-pool + Q1-only)
Headline-cell additive **PARTIAL (sub-divided)**: the comparison CIs EXCLUDE null, divided AND parallel
(B_k significantly > 0 but ≪ the +13.7 divided ref, ≪ the +35.5 parallel ceiling). Logit AGREES → not
scale-sensitive. The two-way (instance+item) bootstrap (Cameron–Gelbach–Miller; SE_2way 0.65pp ≈ SE_inst
— items barely cluster because each position reveals a different item) does NOT change the category. The
per-cell table (all operating cells), additive + logit + Q1-only, is in `results/seg8_verdict.md`. **Per-k
picture (descriptive, NON-monotone — do NOT read as a trend):** sumprod early-pool k=2 **+7.8 [+5.5,+10.1]
PARTIAL**, k=4 **−4.0 [−6.2,−1.8] NEGATIVE** (Q2-driven: the k=4 early pool includes the mildly
recency-elevated Q2; **Q1-only k=4 = +0.1 [−2.9,+3.2] NULL** → position-sensitive), k=8 **+2.5 PARTIAL**
(Q1-only +1.2 NULL). The k=4 NEGATIVE is a recency-erasure mixture, NOT extra resolving power — the headline
is deliberately k=8 (the most-shared cell, where the early pool Q1–Q4 is a clean flat primacy band 20–25%).

### §5.2 directedness contrast (the genuinely-falsifiable co-primary)
**DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE at every k>1 on BOTH levels**, with a significantly-positive reveal-before positive
control everywhere (guard #1 PASS): sumprod k=2 **+10.9 [+7.8,+13.9]**, k=4 **+24.2 [+21.2,+27.2]**, k=8
**+16.2 [+14.3,+18.3]**; d6 k=2 +15.2, k=4 +21.8, **k=8 +31.9 [+30.0,+33.8]**. Guard #4 check: the contrast
moves immaterially with vs without the flagged spaced-digit/show-work rows (k=8 +16.2 vs +16.5). ![contrast](../plots/seg8_contrast.png)

### §5.6 Bonferroni confirmatory family (3 pre-declared tests, 98.3% CIs) — ALL hold
(1) **sumprod k=8 regime** = +2.5 [+0.9,+4.0] → still excludes null+divided → **PARTIAL** (logit confirms);
(2) **sumprod k=8 contrast** = +16.2 [+13.9,+18.8] → **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE**; (3) **d6 k=8 contrast**
(cross-FAMILY generalization) — the **formal test is the PAIRED Haldane-LOGIT contrast = +1.50
[+1.39,+1.60] → DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** (computed on logit because d6's k=8 reveal-before Q4 ceilings at
95%, additive-headroom-limited). **Important honesty point: the d6 additive contrast +31.9 — and sumprod's
k=4 +24.2 — are MAGNITUDE-INFLATED**: their reveal-AFTER arms are strongly negative (d6 −16.8, sumprod k=4
−4.0) because those early pools are NOT flat primacy bands (d6 k=8 n=0 Q1–Q4 = 55/56/69/71%), so the
recency-erasure does NOT cancel in the contrast — it inflates it. **The cross-family takeaway is the
directedness-positive SIGN, not the magnitude**; the only CLEAN directedness magnitude is the headline
sumprod k=8 contrast (+16.2), whose after-arm is a flat-primacy band (no recency to erase). d6's *regime*
B_k is descriptive only (k=8 early-pool −16.8 NEGATIVE = a carry-disruption × recency-erasure mixture,
exactly as pre-registered — only d6's CONTRAST is confirmatory).

### §5.7 fresh-only firewall + §5.9 qualifier gates
Fresh-only (conservative ever-seen-any-size cutoff, inst>649, n=350 never-seen instances): headline
reveal-after `B_k` = **+0.9 [−1.1,+2.9] → NULL** (the pilot's fresh>full anomaly REVERSED on the full run;
still ≪ divided), fresh-only contrast +18.8 [+15.4,+22.1] (DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE holds). **The firewall is
WEAK (±~3pp on 350 instances)** — it confirms `B_k ≪ divided` but cannot crisply separate NULL from PARTIAL.
**Read it as a power-limited NON-replication of the small positive, NOT as evidence against it:** the
fresh-only CI [−1.1,+2.9] still COVERS the full-sample point (+2.5), so the disagreement is "fails to
re-establish significance on 350 instances", not "contradicts PARTIAL". The four §5.9 gates at the headline:
(1) logit AGREES (no "scale-sensitive"); (2) two-way (instance+item) CI UNCHANGED; (3) fresh-only DISAGREES
(categorically NULL) → **"not robust to held-out"**; (4) Q1-only is categorically NULL vs the early-pool's
PARTIAL → **"position-sensitive"** (a difference in the categorical CI-read across the two pre-registered
position estimands, NOT a formal position×filler interaction test). Graded-honesty label: full-sample
additive PARTIAL is the point estimate, with those two qualifiers attached. **"PARTIAL" here means
statistically non-null (CI excludes 0, even at 98.3%) but scientifically NEAR-zero (~7% of B₁, ≪ divided)
and fragile — it is NOT "appreciable partial banking".**

### §5.3 SECONDARY — α not estimated; collapse plots; fraction-retained
The **positivity gate FAILS** for both levels (reveal-after early-pool `B_k_lo > 3pp` at <2 operating cells:
sumprod passes only at k=2) → **"α not estimated — ill-defined near the null"** (the pre-registered
deviation: at/near B_k≈0 no `n_eff` maps the positive B₁ curve onto ≈0; the original α-headline plan is
replaced by the 3-way + contrast). The **collapse plots** (boost-vs-n and boost-vs-n/k) show the measured
B_k lines collapse onto the k=1 curve on **NEITHER** x-axis → neither parallel nor divided. ![collapse](../plots/seg8_collapse.png)
The intuitive **fraction-of-boost-retained `= B_k(n)/B₁(n)`** (where B_k>0) reveals a **retention gradient**:
the LESS-shared cells retain MORE — **k=2 op = 23% [16%,30%]** (a clean, robust +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1], NOT
position-sensitive; the held-out firewall was not run at k=2 — it is pre-registered for the headline only) →
**k=8 op = 7% [3%,11%]**. So "banks essentially no undirected computation" is the **k=8-specific** magnitude;
the complete picture is a **small, statistically-robust undirected boost that is smaller at higher k**
(non-monotone across k — k=4's early pool dips negative from the Q2 position confound; far below the divided
prediction at every k, but not zero at low k). ![fraction retained by k](../plots/seg8_fraction_retained.png) The in-structure
B₁(n) curve with the per-k divided/parallel reference points is
![B1 references](../plots/seg8_b1_references.png) and the per-position recency-erasure picture is
![per-position](../plots/seg8_position_bk.png).

**Supplementary within-scope diagnostics on the small +2.5 (mechanism, analysis-only — `results/seg8_verdict.md`):**
(1) **fixed-item corr(B_k, B₁) = +0.005 [−0.058,+0.070]** (same revealed item alone at k=1 vs shared at k=8,
1000 items) ≈ 0 → the per-item structure of the single-question boost is not visible in the noisy k=8 boost.
**This is LARGELY UNINFORMATIVE, not directional evidence** (a per-item k=1 boost is a single Bernoulli
difference, so any true latent correlation is heavily attenuated toward 0 *regardless of mechanism* — it
rules out a large *observed* correlation, not a substantial latent one). (2) **k-gradient at matched
n/k=25 tok:** k=2 +9.0 vs k=8 +2.5 — boost LARGER at the less-shared k=2, which is what sharing-limited
genuine banking predicts; but this is **at most WEAK/AMBIGUOUS evidence on the artifact**, because an
attention-redistribution artifact could ALSO decrease with k (at higher k the reallocated attention is
spread over a larger early pool), so the artifact's k-dependence is itself ambiguous. (3) **Early-pool
definition sensitivity (headline):** Q1 +1.2, Q1–2 +2.6, Q1–3 +2.5, Q1–4 +2.5 — the +2.5 is stable across
the ⌈k/2⌉ boundary; the Q1-only→NULL split is a POWER difference (¼ the rows), not a within-band position
dependence. **Net: the +2.5 mechanism is UNRESOLVED** — the corr≈0 is largely uninformative (attenuation)
and the k-gradient is at most weak/ambiguous on the artifact; **the attention-redistribution artifact
(caveat iv) remains a LIVE possibility** alongside weak genuine sharing-limited banking (a Segment-11
attention deep-dive). The robust conclusions (B_k ≪ divided; DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE) do **not** depend on
resolving it.

### Honest interpretation + the load-bearing caveats (do NOT overclaim)
- **§1 interpretation caution (stated precisely — two distinct claims):** the collapse/contrast test
  **cleanly adjudicates the sharing/regime of the filler benefit and its directedness** — and these regime +

[460 more lines in file. Use offset=1351 to continue.]

directedness verdicts are **robust**: `B_k ≪ divided` (~10σ, SE_R-inclusive) and DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE. **(a) On the
  sharing axis the data ARGUE AGAINST "the model does k questions' worth of latent thinking in parallel"**
  (B_k ≈ 7% of B₁, far below both the parallel and divided references) — parallel is not merely unproven, it
  is disfavored. **(b) The one mechanistic reading that IS suggestively (not conclusively) supported is the
  DIRECTED-compute story** (the model uses filler when it knows the target) — and even that does not by
  itself prove "directed parallel latent thinking": a positive contrast is consistent with banked directed
  computation OR with reveal-timing-induced attention/retrieval changes (caveats iii, iv). So: **the
  regime/directedness verdict is robust; any stronger mechanistic ('parallel hidden computation') claim is
  NOT supported on the sharing axis and only suggestive on the directedness axis.**
- **(i) Parallel is unreachable in-prompt.** Even DIRECTED, a k-prompt early-pool question gets only
  ~+15–20pp (reveal-before) vs the lone +35.5 — context interference removes ~half the boost BEFORE any
  sharing penalty. So the sharing axis is best read as a **powered re-confirmation that `B_k ≪ B₁(n/k)`**,
  and the genuine falsifiable content is the **directedness contrast**.
- **(ii) The divided reference `B₁(n/k)` is interference-naive** → "excludes divided" alone ≠ "null"; the
  null is carried by the contrast + the positive control (Frame B), not Frame A alone.
- **(iii) The directedness reading is SUGGESTIVE, not proven** — a near-null reveal-after is consistent with
  "does not bank undirected compute" OR "banks it but cannot select/retrieve it after the reveal". This
  design does not separate them (a Segment-11 mechanism question). (The large before / near-zero after split
  mildly leans toward the first, but we do not rely on that.)
- **(iv) Attention-redistribution confound on the regime axis (a LIVE-but-disfavoured possibility):** filler
  erasing the late-position recency advantage could reallocate attention to the early positions, producing a
  small positive early-pool `B_k` that is an attention artifact, not banked compute. The early-pool estimand
  guards against late-position *recency*, NOT against this early-position *reallocation*, so the regime-axis
  +2.5pp point estimate is presented as potentially confounded. **The supplementary diagnostics (above)
  leave this UNRESOLVED — the artifact remains a LIVE possibility:** the k-gradient (B_k larger at k=2) is at
  most weak/ambiguous on the artifact (which could also decrease with k), and corr(B_k,B₁)≈0 is largely
  uninformative (attenuation toward 0 regardless of mechanism) — neither decisively bears either way. **The
  contrast differences this out
  ONLY under the assumption that the attention pattern is reveal-timing-invariant** (the same in the before
  and after arms) — it cancels timing-independent position/attention artifacts but not any attention change
  that early reveal itself induces during the filler. The "directed" reading therefore rests on the contrast
  + positive control under that stated assumption, not on the regime-axis +2.5 alone.
- **(v) The positive control is partly circular** — reveal-before is BOTH guard #1 AND one arm of the
  contrast (a shared failure mode), so it is a necessary apparatus check, not a fully independent
  validation of the contrast.
- **(vi) The headline cell was pilot-known** — Segment 7 added precision / forking-paths protection /
  cross-family + cross-model generalization, NOT discovery (disclosed in the pre-registration).
- **(vii) The clean off-ceiling regime read rests primarily on ONE family (sumprod)**; d6 carries the
  directedness contrast cross-family but its regime B_k is carry-fragile (its early pool is not a flat
  primacy band).
- **(viii) SE_R curve-shape (divided-exclusion robustness):** the divided reference B₁(n/k=25 tok) sits on
  the STEEP rising edge near n_lo, where the bootstrapped SE_R could be under-estimated. The §5.1
  comparison estimator still gives a ~11pp divided gap = **~10σ SE_R-inclusive** (`results/seg8_verdict.md`),
  ≫ any plausible interpolation error → the divided EXCLUSION is robust. (The earlier "≫10σ" was the
  design-power σ that treats the reference as fixed, SE_R=0; the SE_R-inclusive ~10σ is the honest read. A
  divided CLASSIFICATION — which we do NOT make — would have needed full curve-shape uncertainty
  propagated.)
- **Operational scope of the (near-)null:** it is "undirected filler not banked **under the encouraging
  ('told') framing**", measured on the **early/unconfounded positions**. The told-vs-not-told ablation
  (Segment 10) tests whether framing matters; the position-balanced AGGREGATE `B_k` is net-disruptive
  (−6.3 at the headline) because filler erases the late-position recency advantage (recency-erasure
  mixture, NOT the science). These descriptions are mutually consistent, and the directedness conclusion is
  robust to all of them (interference cancels in the contrast).

## Segment 10: robustness of the evolved headline (Opus 4.5, sumprod) — FRAMING-DEPENDENT (V1) but k/filler-robust (V2/V3)
Stress-tested the evolved headline — at high k the **undirected** per-question filler boost is **near-null /
`B_k ≪ divided`** while the **directedness contrast (reveal-before − reveal-after) is POSITIVE** (position-
resolved early-pool + **Q1-only**, additive AND logit) — against the three pre-planned variations on **Opus
only at `sumprod_t3_d2`**, mirroring the Segment-8 methodology (comparison-SE/SE_R off the matching k=1
curve; the reveal-before positive control as the validity guard; cluster bootstrap over instances, seed 0).
**All EXPLORATORY robustness — NOT the pre-registered confirmatory family; no α-fit (ill-defined at the
null); the locked grid/harness/scorers/preregistration are untouched.** Full detail:
`writeups/write_up_segment_10_phase_0.md`; verdict `results/seg10_verdict.md`; figures `plots/seg10_*.png`.

**Gating check — genuine no-CoT holds under ALL new conditions** (`results/seg10_nocot.md`, 290k outputs):
reason% 0 everywhere (incl. not-told — the framing change does NOT enable reasoning), genuine-violation <1%
per cell (not-told 0%; dots k=4 worst 0.29%, all scored WRONG), trunc ~0, score-inconsistent 0,
answered-asked ~100%. Reconstructed-prompt inspection (`results/inspect_seg10.md`) confirms the not-told
prompt removes the "be ready for ANY of them" encouragement and outputs are bare integers.

**V1 — told-vs-NOT-TOLD framing (HIGHEST priority) — the headline PATTERN is FRAMING-DEPENDENT (BREAK), but
NOT in the way an earlier draft claimed (corrected after review).** Under not-told the undirected
reveal-after boost is **much larger and CONCENTRATES on Q1**: k=8 Q1 boost +1.2 (told) → **+26.3
[+23.0,+29.6] (not-told)**, EXCEEDING the divided reference. **NOT a baseline-headroom artifact** (at k=4,8
the not-told n=0 baseline is HIGHER than told; logit agrees; the per-position n=0 Q1 baseline is identical
20.3% vs 20.5%; the boost scales with n: +18.7@100 → +26.3@200 tok). **Disclosure disambiguation
(follow-up):** a `disclose` arm (told MINUS the "— so be ready to answer any of them" clause) behaves like
told (k=8 Q1 +2.0 ≈ told +1.2 ≪ not-told +26.3), so it is the random-target **DISCLOSURE** ("one of these,
chosen at random, will be asked"), NOT the encouragement, that suppresses the model's Q1-default banking (the
encouragement is INCIDENTAL). **What BREAKS vs SURVIVES (precise):** the **regime/near-null leg BREAKS** —
the original *interpretation* "the model does not bank UNDIRECTED computation" is **REFUTED** (it banks ~1
question's worth on Q1; position-resolution is load-bearing — the early-pool average lands AT divided, only
resolving by position shows it is Q1-concentration not divided-spreading). The **directedness-contrast leg
SURVIVES (weakened) on the PRIMARY early-pool read** (not-told k=4,8 +9.1/+10.2, sig.+, vs told +24.2/+16.2);
it collapses only on the Q1-only co-report (because Q1 is the default target) — **the earlier "contrast
vanishes / is a told-framing artifact" claim was WRONG and is RETRACTED.** The confound-minimal directedness
test (**reveal_before − disclose**, holding the random-target disclosure fixed so the baseline doesn't
pre-default to Q1) is LARGE and framing-robust (k=8 Q1 +23.7, k=4 +27.7); the +9–10 not-told number is small
ONLY because the not-told baseline already banks Q1. The deeper **"no PARALLEL latent thinking across k"
SURVIVES / is REINFORCED** (the model banks ≤~1 question's worth under EITHER framing; told even less).
![V1 framing](../plots/seg10_v1_framing.png)

**V2 — alternative k∈{3,6,16} (told, counting) — HOLDS.** Holding TOTAL filler FIXED at 200 tok, the
undirected Q1 boost stays **≪ the divided reference at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (divided +33→+13, measured
≤+7; largest at the least-shared k=2 +6.8) and the **directedness contrast is DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE at every
