## Segment 11: mechanism & confound investigation (cross-family/cross-model confirmation + genuine-computation evidence)
Hardened the evolved headline for the synthesis. EXPLORATORY (locked plan/harness/scorers/pre-registration
UNTOUCHED; no α-fit; no re-declaration), mirroring the Segment-8 methodology (position-resolved Q1-only +
early-pool, additive AND logit, cluster bootstrap seed 0, reveal-before positive control). Genuine no-CoT
re-verified for EVERY new framing/level/model/filler cell (`results/seg11_nocot.md`: 352k outputs, 0%
reasoning, genuine-viol 0.019%, scoring 0-inconsistent; the one DeepSeek not-told n=0 truncation flag is
benign answer-first-then-continue, verified). Full detail: `writeups/write_up_segment_11_phase_0.md`; verdict
`results/seg11_verdict.md`; figures `plots/seg11_*.png`.

**(1) The not-told Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression finding REPLICATES cross-family + cross-model.**
![framing replication](../plots/seg11_framing.png) Undirected **Q1-only** boost B_k at k=8 (operating point),
told / disclose / **not-told**: sumprod (original) +1.2/+2.0/**+26.3**; **add_n15_d6 (Opus, cross-FAMILY)**
−2.2/−3.4/**+10.6**; **add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek, cross-MODEL)** +3.4/+2.2/**+6.2** — not-told ≫ disclose ≈ told
(on DeepSeek the clean separation is not-told **>** disclose; not-told-vs-told CIs just touch — a weaker,
partial cross-model leg). The DIRECTION replicates everywhere, but the magnitude **attenuates cross-family
and especially cross-model even on the headroom-robust LOGIT scale** (not-told−told Δlogit: sumprod +1.16 ≫
d6 +0.52 ≫ DeepSeek +0.12) — only PARTLY headroom; there is a genuine family/model-specific weakening
(DeepSeek ~10× weaker on logit). **The reframe is NOT sumprod-specific** (the confound-minimal
reveal_before−disclose contrast is large + framing-robust on both new families/models). d6 is carry-fragile
→ its leg rests on Q1 + logit alone (huge late-position negatives = the Tier-3 carry anomaly).

**(2) The genuine-hidden-computation interpretation is STRENGTHENED (honestly bounded).**
![directed dose-response](../plots/seg11_dose.png) **(A) DIRECTED dose-response (necessary, not sufficient):**
the reveal_before Q1 boost rises with filler tokens (−1.6→+27.1 over 24→300 tok) and TRACKS the lone B₁(n)
shape; the not-told committed Q1 boost likewise scales. A *pure-step* attention/recency-reallocation account
predicts no graded n-dependence, so the graded rise DISFAVOURS a pure-step account — but a *graded* attention/
length account can also scale with n, so this is necessary not sufficient (selectivity (B) is the stronger
lever). **(B) compute-selectivity:** the directed boost RISES with item difficulty (max-2×2-product quartile
+6.7→+10.8→+14.7→+16.2; logit +0.27→+0.66) at a ~FLAT n0 baseline (52→48%) = NOT headroom — filler
selectively helps the more compute-bound items. The cross-check is honest: it RISES cross-family (d6 directed
+11.0→+19.6 with #carries) but d6's baseline ALSO declines ~8pp (a headroom confound, unlike sumprod's flat
baseline), and on DeepSeek the baseline is flat but the boost is FLAT too (no selectivity) — so the CLEANEST
difficulty-selectivity is sumprod; the genuine-computation evidence is Opus-leaning, not uniformly clean. **(B2)
directedness is target-following AND primacy-modulated:** the (before−after) contrast is sig.+ at ALL 8
positions (so directing to a NON-Q1 target gains everywhere, not just primacy), but the directed
*absolute* boost is large early (Q1 +25.7, Q2 +18.7) and small late (Q6 +1.7) — so directed efficacy is
strongly primacy-graded. **(C) banks-vs-retrieves:** the not-told profile banks predominantly Q1 (+26.3)
with a small residual early boost; the evidence against diffuse all-questions banking is that Q2–Q5 are
~+3 (≪ divided). **Calibrated claim (sharpened after LLM review) — SUPPORTED:** genuine, dose-responsive,
difficulty-selective, **filler-amount-dependent** computation committed to ONE question (the named
target — target-following but primacy-modulated; or the positional default Q1); no parallel. **UNPROVEN /
honest bounds:** (i) a *pure-step* attention account is disfavoured but a *graded* attention/bandwidth
account is NOT formally excluded (it can also yield a monotone dose + a difficulty gradient at flat
baseline; selectivity is the stronger lever, not decisive); (ii) LOCALIZATION is not established — filler
ENABLES the benefit and it scales with filler AMOUNT, but the computation is not shown to occur AT the
filler positions (vs at decode reading them as scratch) — hence "filler-amount-dependent", NOT
"filler-time"; (iii) content-vs-length (dots also banks Q1, so not counting-specific, but both are length);
(iv) the dose-response + selectivity **evidence base is mainly Opus** (selectivity also on d6, flat on
DeepSeek). **No "parallel latent thinking" claim (disfavoured); no proven mechanism.**

**(3) The default target ("why Q1?") is POSITIONAL (primacy / first-listed), not content-based.**
![default + cap](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png) Fixed-item re-ordering (the SAME revealed item at positions
{Q1,Q2,Q4,Q8}, not-told k=8): B_k +25.1/−2.1/+3.0/−18.0 → the boost follows the listed POSITION, not the
content; Q1 is boosted for BOTH easy and hard items, later positions for neither.

**(4) The context-interference cap is mostly STRUCTURE (the first distractor), ~saturating.** Directed
(reveal_before) boost at a FIXED item/position-0 vs #distractors (200 tok): lone (k=1) **+43.0** → k=2 +23.6
→ k=4 +30.1 → k=8 +25.3 (logit +1.84→+0.97/+1.25/+1.03; **lead with logit** — part of the additive drop is
headroom pre-consumption: the distractor questions act as preceding-context filler, raising the pos0 n0
29%→38% even at n=0). The bulk of the cap appears at the first distractor and ~saturates. In reachable-
accuracy terms the gap is modest (in-prompt directed Q1 ~63% vs lone ~72%, ~9pp), so the cleanest mechanism
is "part of the compute budget is already spent on questions-as-filler", not "distractor context halves the
compute"; either way the lone/parallel boost is not reachable in-prompt.

**Light probes.** ![selectivity](../plots/seg11_selectivity.png) **dots×k** is FRAMING-INDEPENDENT — a
uniform ~6–7pp weaker filler than counting (roughly doubling from k=1's ~+3.5pp gap); under not-told dots ALSO
banks a large Q1 boost (+18.2), so it is NOT a categorical disengagement signal (the Segment-10 "dots is
disruptive" view is this constant penalty seen through the near-zero told cell). The **d6 carry anomaly**
(k=8 Q8 −60pp under not-told) is a noted open puzzle for the synthesis (tied to the Segment-2 saturation
puzzle; does not bear on the Q1-banking headline).

## How to reproduce (the full guide)

**Model / provider config.** Opus = **`claude-opus-4-5-20251101`** (temp 0, extended thinking **OFF**,
`max_tokens=48`, genuine no-CoT via assistant **prefill** `"The answer to Qj is"`; `llm.py` reads
`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Open model = **DeepSeek-V3-0324** `deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`, reasoning OFF
(`extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":False}}`), **provider-pinned to the good cluster**
`--only-providers deepinfra modelrun siliconflow` (unpinned routing inflates the boost ~2× — a
load-bearing gotcha; `llm_openrouter.py` reads `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`).

**Canonical datasets + loader.** `data/<tag>.jsonl` (6 locked levels, 5000 deterministic items each, seed
12345) loaded via `from canonical import load_canonical; load_canonical(tag, n, offset)` — pass
`offset>=500` for fresh draws (items `[0,500)` were the calibration sample). Headline levels: **Opus
`sumprod_t3_d2`** (primary, gradual/off-ceiling), **Opus `add_n15_d6`** (secondary, carry-fragile),
**DeepSeek `add_n4_d10`** (generator level, `or_levels.py`, seed 7777).

**The LOCKED plan + harness.** `results/preregistration.md` is the binding pre-registration (grid + §5
decision rules; **do not change**). The apparatus is `kharness.py` (`build_k_messages` — the minimal-reveal
4-turn reveal-by-index prompt) driven by `run_kquestion.py` (model-agnostic runner; `--assert-cached`
supported). All cells stream to `results/*.jsonl` with a `_meta` line-1 (model + git hash + grid).

**Per-segment runners + analysis libs + where each headline number/figure comes from:**

| result | runner | analysis lib → verdict file | figure |
|---|---|---|---|
| Opus main run (274k calls) | `run_segment7.sh` → `results/seg7_*.jsonl` | `seg7_lib.py`, `analyze_seg7_{guards,summary}.py` → `seg7_guards.md`, `main_run_summary.md` | `plots/seg7_*.png` |
| **§5 regime + directedness verdict** | (analysis-only) | `seg8_lib.py`, `analyze_seg8_verdict.py` → **`seg8_verdict.md`** (+ `seg8_regime_cells.csv`) | `plots/seg8_*.png` |
| DeepSeek cross-model | `run_seg9.sh` → `results/seg9_*.jsonl` | `seg9_lib.py`, `analyze_seg9_verdict.py` → `seg9_verdict.md` | `plots/seg9_*.png` |
| Framing/k/filler robustness | `run_segment10.sh` → `results/seg10_*.jsonl` | `seg10_lib.py`, `analyze_seg10_verdict.py` → `seg10_verdict.md` | `plots/seg10_*.png` |
| Mechanism | `run_segment11.sh` → `results/seg11_*.jsonl` | `seg11_lib.py`, `analyze_seg11_*.py` → `seg11_verdict.md` | `plots/seg11_*.png` |
| `B₁(n)` single-question curve + power gate | `run_b1curve.py` | `analyze_b1curve.py` → `b1curve_summary.md` | `plots/b1curve*.png` |
| Canonical datasets | `canonical.py` / `run_lock.py` | `validate_canonical.py` | `plots/calibration.png`, `dataset_quality.png` |

The headline numbers map to files thus: **sumprod k=8 early `B_k` +2.5 / Q1 +1.2 / contrast +16.2 /
retention 23%→7% / α-not-estimated** ← `results/seg8_verdict.md` (+ `seg8_regime_cells.csv`); **not-told
Q1 +26.3, disclose +2.0, cross-family/model replication** ← `results/seg10_verdict.md` +
`results/seg11_verdict.md` (+ `seg10_cells.csv`, `seg11_framing.csv`); **DeepSeek directedness Q1 k4 +2.6 /
k8 +1.9** ← `results/seg9_verdict.md`.

**`--assert-cached` reproducibility audit (verified Segment 12, all at $0, no cache miss):**
- `ASSERT_CACHED=1 bash run_segment7.sh` — re-streams the full **274k-call** Opus grid (16 cells) from
  cache, **$0**. Likewise `ASSERT_CACHED=1 bash run_seg9.sh` / `run_segment10.sh` / `run_segment11.sh`.
- The analysis/verdict scripts (`analyze_seg8_verdict.py`, …) and `plot_*.py` make **no API calls** — they
  reproduce the verdict files + figures from the cached JSONL at $0 (the only paid calls this segment were
  a few review/vision LLM queries, logged in `total_cost.jsonl`).
- Cost ledger: `total_cost.jsonl` (append-only; cumulative project spend ≈ **$2168**, within the $5k
  budget; an early ~$13 over-count of Segment-3 kimi pricing is documented in `continuation_context.md`).

## Segment 0: harness + replication de-risk (the detailed record)

> The Segment-0 de-risk built and verified the genuine-no-CoT harness and established that the filler
> boost reproduces and is **selective**. **These are the SEGMENT-0 findings, NOT the project's
> conclusion** (the conclusion is in the "Final synthesis (READ THIS)" section at the top). They are kept
> as the methodological / replication record the downstream segments stand on. The **project-level**
> limitations and future work are in the synthesis, not here.

### Segment-0 replication & selectivity (the de-risk)
Paired no-CoT accuracy, counting filler in the assistant prefill, ~300 filler tokens (measured via
the Anthropic `input_tokens` delta), bootstrap 95% CIs over problems.

**(1) The boost robustly reproduces on multi-digit-arithmetic tasks (n=500/task):**

| Task | no-filler baseline | +~300 tok | **boost** | 95% CI |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n15_d6` (15× 6-digit sum) | 49.2% | 79.6% | **+30.4** | [+26.0, +34.8] |
| `add_n12_d10` (12× 10-digit sum) | 61.8% | 83.4% | **+21.6** | [+17.0, +26.4] |
| `add_n15_d8` (15× 8-digit sum) | 54.4% | 71.6% | **+17.2** | [+12.8, +21.6] |
| `mul_a4_b4` (4d × 4d) | 59.8% | 70.6% | **+10.8** | [+6.8, +14.8] |
| `gsm_hard` (word + big arithmetic; **positive control**) | 50.6% | 63.8% | **+13.2** | [+9.8, +16.6] |

All p(boost≤0) ≈ 0.000, and the boost survives on a **logit scale** (Δlogit +0.48 to +1.39;
`analyze_nocot_integrity.py`). The positive control boosting → **the harness is a valid measurement
instrument** (for an arithmetic-precision boost — see limitation); procedural arithmetic also
boosting → **the phase success case**. The effect is much larger than Redwood's reported ~5–10 pt
(see "Why is the boost so large?").

**(2) The boost is SELECTIVE — it tracks computation STRUCTURE, not difficulty.** It is robust when
the bottleneck is a single **deep multi-digit computation**, but ~absent when the bottleneck is
**aggregating many easy checks** — *even at a matched baseline*:

| Task (structure) | baseline | boost prefill / user | note |
|---|---|---|---|
| `count_correct_k6` (count correct of 6 small eqns) | **47.5%** | **+0.0 / +0.0** | **difficulty-MATCHED to addition; still null** (lone marginal: dots +5%, p=.04, ≪ arithmetic's dots +12..+29) |
| `count_correct_k12` | 25.8% | −0.8 / +2.5 | n.s. |
| `count_correct_k18` | 18.8% | −0.5 / +7.5 | only one user cell significant; inconsistent |
| `aime2025` (competition math) | 66.7% | −13.3 / −13.3 | **DEMOTED: likely memorized + messy filler outputs → uninterpretable** |

![Selectivity of the boost](../plots/selectivity.png)

**Interpretation:** filler helps precisely when the single forward pass is **compute-bound on one
deep multi-digit arithmetic computation** (carries/precision), and helps little when each sub-step is
easy but there are many to aggregate (`count_correct` — highly parallelizable, yet null even at a
**matched ~48% baseline**, ruling out a difficulty confound; logit Δ≈0). This **refines the Pfau
"parallelizable computation" framing** (not *any* parallelizable task benefits) and the **selectivity
argues against a generic format/length artifact** (which would help all tasks).
- **AIME 2025 is demoted (unreliable), not evidence of a reasoning null.** Two reasons: (i) its
  baseline is clean ~6-token recall (no-CoT 66.7% on genuinely-hard competition math, with
  AIME 1983–2024 at ~90–97% ⇒ likely partial memorization — AIME 2025 is *pre*-cutoff for a
  Nov-2025 model); (ii) under filler its outputs get messier (avg 6.0→9.3 tokens; 0%→3–7% show
  working), which can create first-integer parse artifacts in either direction. It is also
  underpowered (n=30). So the −13 pp is uninterpretable; we do NOT use AIME to claim Redwood's
  competition-math result fails to reproduce.
- **Caveat (count_correct still embeds arithmetic):** detecting an off-by-1 equation needs exact
  2-digit arithmetic, so this is "deep-single vs shallow-many" computation, not a pure
  reasoning-vs-arithmetic contrast. An equally consistent phrasing of the same selectivity is
  "filler helps when the *answer itself* is a multi-digit number needing carry precision" (count
  answers are 0–6); we don't claim deep-vs-shallow is established over this alternative.

**(3) The boost saturates EARLY.** Finer sweep (counting/prefill, n=300): accuracy reaches most of
its boost by ~10 tokens (`add_n15_d8`), ~40 tokens (`add_n15_d6`), or ~150 tokens (`gsm_hard`), then
plateaus with **no degradation up to ~1000 tokens**. The **rising region is narrow** — important for
Segment 4 (the k-question power gate needs n/k_max to stay in the rising region).
![Filler-amount sweep](../plots/ngrid.png)

The per-task de-risk panel (accuracy/boost/placement/filler-type): ![De-risk](../plots/derisk_full.png)

### Segment-0 evidence the boost is genuine computation (not an artifact)
- **Error structure (`analyze_robustness.py` §3):** 83–91% of baseline-wrong addition problems are
  *near-misses* (≤2 digits off ⇒ carry/precision slips), and filler fixes near-misses more than
  far-misses. The near−far fix-rate gap is **+22 pp [+5,+38]** for `add_n15_d8` (significant) and
  directionally consistent (+11, +13) but underpowered for the other two (far-misses are rare,
  n≈22–24). Suggestive — not crisp — evidence that filler repairs precision errors.
  ![error structure](../plots/error_structure.png)
- **Selectivity at matched baseline** (headline 2): arithmetic boosts, aggregation doesn't even at
  ~48% baseline → not a generic artifact and not a difficulty confound.
- **GSM-Hard balking/parse confound ruled out:** on the subset where BOTH n=0 and the filler
  condition emit a bare integer (n=430), the boost is **+13.3%** ≈ the full +13.2%
  (`analyze_robustness.py` §2). *Caveat:* this conditions on a post-treatment variable (output
  format) so it is a collider-prone control; it merely corroborates the pre-treatment `truth≥0`
  filter (+14.3%) and the full result.
- **No-CoT integrity (`analyze_nocot_integrity.py`):** arithmetic + count_correct have **0%**
  outputs that start with words; only GSM-Hard leaks (~11% at n=0 / user, ~1% at prefill — another
  reason prefill is preferred). dots ≈ counting in **plateau boost magnitude** (filler-type robust)
  → not numeric/semantic priming. (Segment 2 caveat: the plateau magnitude is filler-type-robust, but
  the small-n **rise shape** is NOT — counting steps sharply, dots rises gradually; see the dots check.)

### Why the Segment-0 boost is large (~10–30 pp vs Redwood's ~5–10)
Most likely a **task-nature** difference, not harness inflation: multi-operand addition is the
*maximally* filler-amenable regime — one deep, highly parallelizable carry computation — exactly
where Pfau et al. predict the largest gains, and our difficulty knob places the baseline on the
steep part of the curve (mid-band) where there is maximal headroom. The error-structure result
(filler repairs carry near-misses) is consistent with this. Redwood's number is on competition math,
whose bottleneck is more reasoning/recall than deep arithmetic — and indeed our reasoning-bound
tasks show little/no boost. So the magnitude gap is *expected* and good for downstream power.

### Key harness choices (Segment 0, with rationale)
- **Genuine no-CoT via assistant prefill.** Confirmed Opus 4.5 supports prefill (it predates
  4.6/4.7/4.8 which don't). Assistant turn seeded with `"The answer is"`; extended thinking never
  enabled. **Verified by inspection**: procedural-arithmetic outputs are immediate single integers
  (0% truncation, 0 text-before-number). `max_tokens=48` ≫ longest answer (≤13-digit, ≤9 tokens).
- **Filler placement = assistant prefill** (filler before the `"The answer is"` lead-in). Mirrors
  Pfau et al. (filler in the response), **mechanically prevents** post-hoc reasoning, and gives a
  larger/cleaner boost than user placement (we still ran user placement as a comparison).
  - **Critical early bug we fixed:** framing user-prompt filler as *"scratch space to work"* induced
    explicit CoT on word problems (truncation 76%!), breaking no-CoT and corrupting extraction. Fix:
    strictly **neutral "ignore this padding"** framing + a stronger "Do not show any work"
    instruction. After the fix, filler *helps* GSM-Hard (was a spurious −28%). Single most important
    harness lesson of the phase.
- **Answer extraction = FIRST integer** (the correct *conservative* no-CoT measure; a later
  reasoning-derived integer must NOT be counted). A robust variant (`parse_answer_robust`) that
  concatenates the rare "spaced-digit" output format is used only for a sensitivity re-score — it
  raises the multiplication boost only +10.8→+11.0 (see "format artifact" below).
- **Primary procedural family = multi-operand addition** (parallelizable). Difficulty knob =
  (#operands, #digits); **Segment 1 finalized the canonical levels** (see the Segment 1 section):
  3 mid-band 8-digit-addition levels spanning 47→55→68% (primary `add_n15_d8`), a max-boost d6
  alternative, a homogenized multiplication second family, and an all-positive sum-of-products third
  family (`sumprod_t3_d2`). Signed expression-eval was explored (boosts) but dropped (sign-bimodal).
  Canonical data + loader: `data/*.jsonl` + `canonical.load_canonical` /
  `tasks.load_task(loader="canonical")`.
- **Positive control = GSM-Hard** (GSM8K word problems with large numbers; non-memorizable,
  mid-range 50.6%). Standard benchmarks are **memorized → ceiling** for Opus 4.5 no-CoT (GSM8K 95%,
  MATH-500 85–93%, AIME 1983–2024 97.5%, all with ~6-token recall outputs — see
  `scripts/check_memorization.py`). GSM-Hard is *computation-bound* like the procedural tasks; we
  added the **aggregation/reasoning** controls (count_correct k6/k12/k18; AIME 2025) to test a
  different mechanism — which is how the selectivity finding emerged. **AIME 2025 turned out to be
  partially memorized** (clean recall outputs) so we demoted it (see headline 2).
- **Filler amount in tokens via Anthropic `input_tokens` delta** (primary); tiktoken cross-check
  agrees (counting 75/150 → 149/299; `scripts/tiktoken_crosscheck.py`).

### Format artifact (Segment 0; documented, minor, conservative)
Counting filler in the prefill occasionally makes the model emit a multiplication answer
digit-by-digit (e.g. `" 28 8 9 6 1 9 2\n\n28896192"`); first-integer parsing then grabs `28`. This
hits **~22/500 `mul_a4_b4` count300_prefill rows** (and ~3–6 GSM-Hard), and produces a handful of
false negatives. It is **absent on addition** and biases the multiplication boost *downward*
(robust re-score: +10.8→+11.0). `[ERRATUM corrected]` an earlier note said "only 2 rows" — that was
an undercount (the regex missed two-digit leading groups).

### Models / parameters (Segment 0 / Segment 2 de-risk runs)
- Model `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`, temperature 0, extended thinking off, `max_tokens=48`.
- Conditions: n=0; counting 75 & 150 items (~150 & ~300 tok) × {prefill, user}; dots 300 (~300 tok,
  prefill). n=500/task (arithmetic de-risk), n=400/task (count_correct k6/k12/k18), n=300/task
  (finer sweep), n=30 (AIME). Result files: `derisk_full_*`, `derisk_reasoning_*`,
  `derisk_reasoning2_*` (k6), `ngrid_*`.
- **Segment 2 `B₁(n)` curve:** counting filler / prefill, fresh items (`offset=500`), 19-point
  realized-token grid 0–1200 tok; **n=1000/cell for all five levels** (addition in `b1curve_main`;
  mul/sumprod topped up to 1000 in `b1curve_mulsumprod1k`). Densification of the d6 rise + d8/d12
  small-n (`b1curve_dense_*`). Dots-filler small-n robustness check on d6 (`b1curve_d6_dots`). Harder
  probe `harder_probe_main`. Robust parser for mul/sumprod. Analysis must be passed ALL the b1curve
  files together (`analyze_b1curve.py`/`plot_b1curve.py` merge + de-dup).

### Segment-0-era limitations & next-steps (SUPERSEDED — see the "Final synthesis" for the project's)
> These were the **Segment-0 de-risk-era** limitations and next-steps. Most are **resolved** by the later
> segments (the power gate was resolved → GO in Segment 2; the level choice / operating point were locked
> in Segments 4–6; the k-question parallel-thinking question was answered in Segments 7–11). The
> **project-level limitations and future work** are in the **"Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section at the
> top of this document. Two Segment-0 facts that remain part of the standing record:
- **The harness validates an *arithmetic-precision* filler boost, not Redwood's *competition-math* boost.**
  No filler boost was reproduced on any reasoning/competition task (the only one tried, AIME 2025, is
  contaminated/underpowered → demoted). The selectivity claim rests on the *absence* of a boost on
  aggregation tasks, not on demonstrating filler helping a non-arithmetic task. (This is the
  "no clean reasoning-bound positive control" limitation in the synthesis.)
- **Small-filler non-monotonicity is real** (`add_n15_d6` dips ~8pp below baseline at ≤11 tok before
  rising; reproduces with dots → genuine carry disruption, not a counting-content artifact). The design
  keeps `n/k ≥ n_lo` so the divided prediction is never a dip/sub-threshold value. The **divided
  prediction `B₁(n/k)` is an interference-naive abstraction** (read off the single-question curve) — the
  load-bearing caveat that recurs throughout (carried by the directedness contrast + positive control, not
  the divided-reference comparison alone).

Detailed chronology, per-condition numbers, and data-inspection notes: `writeups/progress_log.md` (the
Segment 0–8 chronological log) + the phase-specific `progress_log_segment_{9,10,11,12}_phase_0.md`.

# Progress Log

(Unified, append-only chronological record. Most-recent work at the bottom of each section.)

---

## Segment 0: Harness + procedural arithmetic + replication de-risk + positive control — 06/03/2026 04:08 - commit ee43ded

### What was done
Built the full project infrastructure and the core no-CoT + filler harness, calibrated a
procedural arithmetic difficulty knob, assembled a positive control, and ran a paired
no-filler-vs-filler de-risk on Opus 4.5. **Result: the filler boost reproduces strongly** on both
procedural arithmetic and the positive control; harness validated.

Chronological steps:
1. **Manual smoke test first** (`scripts/smoke_test.py`): confirmed (a) assistant **prefill works on
   Opus 4.5** (seeded "…the lazy" → " **dog**"); (b) forced immediate integer answers with thinking
   off (raw `' 43.'`, `' 1353.'`); (c) filler token measurement (180 counting items ≈ +367 input
   tokens). Note: prefill must NOT end in trailing whitespace (API 400) — lead-in is `"The answer is"`.
2. **Infra**: copied `file_cache.py`, `cost_tracker.py`, `llm_helper.py`; wrote `.gitignore`
   (`file_cache_dir/` ignored, results/plots/writeups tracked); built `llm.py` (cached Opus 4.5,
   env-var key, semaphore, retry, cost tracking, returns usage + stop_reason).
3. **Generator** (`arithmetic.py`): seeded, deterministic, families addition/multiplication/
   expression/modular, difficulty tags, dedup.
4. **Filler** (`filler.py`): counting / dots / repeat-problem; size in items.
5. **Harness** (`harness.py`): prefill-based no-CoT, filler placement (user/prefill), first-integer
   parse (commas/signs), exact-match scoring; extensible to k>1 (reveal-by-index).
6. **Calibration** (`run_calibrate.py`, 24 difficulties × 40): found mid-band addition difficulties
   (n12_d6 70%, n15_d8 60%, mul_a4_b4 60%, n12_d10 55%, n15_d6 42.5%); expression/modular at floor
   with truncation → rejected.
7. **Positive control search**: standard benchmarks are memorized → ceiling for Opus 4.5 no-CoT
   (GSM8K 80%, MATH-500 85–93%, AIME 1983–2024 89%). Chose **GSM-Hard** (52.5%, non-memorizable).
8. **Pilot 1** (scratch-space framing): procedural +14% but GSM-Hard spuriously **−28%** with 76%
   truncation → diagnosed as the framing inducing CoT. **Fixed** to neutral "ignore padding" framing
   + stronger no-work instruction; added clean prefill placement.
9. **Pilot 2** (n=150): clean boosts — addition +12–16%, GSM-Hard +10% (prefill). Prefill > user.
10. **Full de-risk** (`run_derisk.py`, n=500, 5 tasks × 6 conditions = 15000 calls): see findings.
11. **Data inspection** (`sample_inspection.py`) + reproducibility (`--assert-cached` = $0) +
    tiktoken cross-check.

### Details and examples
- **Prompts**: user = `"Compute <expr>.\n\nDo not show any work or reasoning. Answer with only the
  final integer, nothing else."` (arithmetic) or the word problem as-is (GSM-Hard). Prefill =
  `"<filler> The answer is"` (prefill placement) or `"The answer is"` (user placement, filler in
  the user message under a neutral "ignore this padding" prefix). See `harness.py:build_messages`.
- **Result files**: `results/calibrate_20260603_104304.jsonl` (calibration);
  `results/derisk_pilot_*` / `derisk_pilot2_*` (pilots, the pilot used the buggy scratch-space
  framing — superseded); `results/derisk_full_20260603_105934.jsonl` (the headline n=500 run);
  summaries in `results/derisk_full_summary.csv`.
- **Data inspection file**: `results/inspect_derisk_full_20260603_105934.md` (random
  correct/incorrect/strange per task×condition with full prompt, prefill, raw output, parsed, truth,
  correctness). Plot: `plots/derisk_full.png`.
- **Conditions / config**: n=0; counting 75/150 items (~150/~300 tok) × {prefill, user}; dots 300
  (~300 tok, prefill). Realized filler tokens (Anthropic input_tokens delta): count75≈151 (prefill)
  /184 (user); count150≈301/334; dots300≈300.

### Key findings
- **Filler boost reproduces strongly on Opus 4.5** (paired, n=500, ~300 tok counting, prefill):
  add_n15_d6 **+30.4** [+26,+35], add_n12_d10 **+21.6** [+17,+26], add_n15_d8 **+17.2** [+13,+22],
  mul_a4_b4 **+10.8** [+7,+15], gsm_hard **+13.2** [+10,+17]. All p(≤0)≈0.000.
  ![](../plots/derisk_full.png)
- **Harness validated** by the positive control (GSM-Hard boosts). Procedural arithmetic also boosts
  → phase success case.
- **Prefill placement ≥ user placement** (cleaner, larger). **Counting ≈ dots** (filler-type robust).
- Accuracy **saturates by ~150–300 tokens** — relevant for Segment 4's rising-region requirement.
- Effect (~10–30 pt) is **larger than Redwood's ~5–10 pt** → good downstream power.

### Data inspection notes (verifying we measure what we intend)
- **Genuine no-CoT confirmed**: procedural outputs are immediate single integers (0% truncation, 0
  text-before-number). Overall parse_fail 0.07%, truncation 0.72% (almost all GSM-Hard).
- **Scoring correct**: `parsed==truth XOR correct` = 0 rows. Incorrect addition cases are genuine
  close-but-wrong computations (e.g. 65210820745 vs 65209820755). Negatives parse correctly
  (`-4358966`).
- **Filler placement correct**: verified prefill shows `1 2 3 … 150 The answer is`; input_tokens
  +301 over baseline.
- **GSM-Hard quirks documented**: (i) ~12% degenerate negative/nonsensical problems (model says
  "impossible") — wrong in all conditions, **paired boost unaffected** (boost holds at +14.3% on the
  non-negative subset, n=441); (ii) 177/15000 (1.2%) rows where the model writes a brief caveat
  before the number — first-integer parse is the correct conservative no-CoT extraction (a later
  reasoning-derived integer must NOT be counted). Digit-spelling pattern: only 2 rows, both already
  wrong. `[ERRATUM added 06/03/2026 — UNDERCOUNT; actual rate ~22/500 mul count300_prefill rows with
  a few genuine false negatives. My quantifying regex missed two-digit leading groups. Impact is
  minor and biases the multiplication boost DOWNWARD; addition is unaffected. See follow-up entry.]`

### Notes
- Cost so far this phase: ~$28.7 (tracked in `total_cost.jsonl`); well within the $5k budget.
- The early scratch-space-framing bug is the key cautionary tale: prompt framing for word problems
  can silently break no-CoT and corrupt scoring. Always inspect outputs.
- Next: Segment 1 (dataset/difficulty calibration) and Segment 4 (`B₁(n)` curve) need a finer n-grid
  in the *rising* region (current grid saturates by ~150 tok).

## Segment 1: Canonical procedural-arithmetic datasets + difficulty calibration — 06/03/2026 17:58 - commit 63d88bd

### What was done
Finalized, validated, and saved the canonical procedural-arithmetic datasets the downstream `B₁(n)`
curve and k-question experiment will stand on. Followed the `dataset-creation` skill: wrote
desiderata first (LLM-critiqued), ran a rigorous Opus-4.5 no-CoT baseline-vs-difficulty calibration
(reusing cached Segment-0 baselines), locked in mid-band levels, saved canonical datasets with
metadata + a deterministic loader, validated quality, and ran a `dataset-reviewer` pass (fixing what
it surfaced).

Steps:
1. **Desiderata** (`writeups/desiderata_procedural_arithmetic.md`): D1 determinism, D2 unique exact
   big-int answers, D3 non-degenerate items, D4 difficulty knob, D5 band/selection/model-pinning,
   D6 deep-arithmetic + filler-responsiveness gate, D7 robust scoring, D8 homogeneity, D9
   anti-requirements. **Critiqued by opus-4.8 + gpt-5.5** (`scripts/critique_desiderata.py`); folded
   in: reconciling the operands knob with deep-vs-aggregate selectivity, a filler-responsiveness
   gate, model-snapshot pinning, selection-bias/held-out re-confirmation, per-item difficulty
   proxies + a concrete homogeneity criterion, stricter parser tests, mathematical dedup.
2. **Calibration grid** (`run_calibrate.py`, n=300, seed=12345 so 4 Segment-0 baselines cache-hit):
   addition (#operands × #digits), multiplication, expression. **8-digit addition is a clean
   monotonic knob** (n10=89%→n20=17%); 6-digit is noisier (n14_d6=35% outlier). Plot:
   `analyze_calibration.py` → `plots/calibration.png`.
3. **Lock-in** (`run_lock.py`): for each candidate level, n=500 baseline + **disjoint held-out
   (items 3000–3499)** re-confirm + **filler-responsiveness gate** (≈300 counting filler, prefill,
   paired). All candidates show a positive boost; held-out agrees with baseline within CI.
4. **Saved canonical datasets** (`canonical.py` → `data/*.jsonl`, 5000 items each) with full metadata
   (family, params, seed, git hash, generator version, pinned model config, calibrated baseline +
   CI + held-out + gate boost, answer range, item_filter, calibration lock file) and **per-item
   difficulty proxies** (answer, #digits, #carries, max operand, trailing-zeros). Deterministic
   loaders: `canonical.load_canonical(tag)` and `tasks.load_task({"loader":"canonical","tag":...})`.
5. **Validation** (`validate_canonical.py`): D1 regeneration/prefix-stable, D2 independent big-int
   recompute, D3 uniqueness + **canonical-form (reorder-invariant) dedup** + degeneracy/digit-pattern
   checks, D7 scoring unit tests + verification on 10k+ real cached outputs (parsed==truth ≡ correct,
   0 inconsistencies), D8 homogeneity by artifact buckets. **All checks PASS.**
6. **`dataset-reviewer` pass** → fixed everything it surfaced (see below).

### Details and examples
- **New scripts:** `run_lock.py` (baseline/held-out/gate), `analyze_calibration.py` (+plot),
  `canonical.py` (registry/save/load), `validate_canonical.py`, `sample_canonical.py` (inspection),
  `scripts/critique_desiderata.py`. Extended `run_calibrate.py` (finer grid) and `tasks.py`
  (canonical loader). `arithmetic.py` gained per-item features, an independent recompute validator,
  named `item_filter`s, and canonical-form dedup.
- **Result files:** `results/calibrate_20260604_002011.jsonl` (grid n=300);
  `results/lock_20260604_005356.jsonl` (final lock: per level 500 baseline + 500 gate + 500
  held-out; reproduces via `run_lock.py --assert-cached` = $0). Data inspection:
  `results/inspect_canonical.md` (per-level random items + ground truth + features + correct/
  incorrect/strange cached outputs + a format-cleanliness table).
- **Locked canonical levels (Opus 4.5 no-CoT, n=500, bootstrap CI; paired gate boost):**
  | tag | role | baseline | held-out | filler-gate boost |
  |---|---|---|---|---|
  | `add_n16_d8` | addition lower | 45.4% [41.0,49.8] | 48.8% | +13.8pp [9.2,18.4] |
  | `add_n15_d8` | addition central **(PRIMARY)** | 54.4% [50.0,58.8] | 55.8% | +17.2pp [13.0,21.6] |
  | `add_n12_d8` | addition higher (k-headroom) | 68.2% [64.0,72.2] | 68.2% | +20.8pp [16.8,24.6] |
  | `add_n15_d6` | addition max-boost alt | 49.2% [44.8,53.6] | 54.8% | +30.4pp [26.0,34.8] |
  | `mul_a4_b4_hom` | second family (homogenized) | 47.0% [42.6,51.4] | 47.2% | +11.0pp [6.6,15.4] |

### Key findings
- **Clean baseline-vs-difficulty calibration; 8-digit addition is a monotonic difficulty knob.**
  ![](../plots/calibration.png)
- **Multiplication had a real within-level heterogeneity** (D8 check): raw 4×4 products with trailing
  zeros (79% vs 53%) and 7-digit (small) products (80% vs 56%) are much easier — a 27% gap. **Fixed**
  by homogenizing (`mul_a4_b4_hom`: 8-digit products, no trailing zeros) → gap 0%, still boosts
  (+11.0pp), baseline 47% (the easy items removed → lower than raw 60%).
- **Addition is highly homogeneous** (per-feature accuracy gaps ≤6%); reinforces it as the primary.
- **n=0 baselines are clean for all families** (0% truncation, 0% parse-fail, ≤0.2% shows-working).
  **Under filler, mul/expr show ~7% spaced-digit/"shows-working" outputs** (the Segment-0 format
  artifact, conservatively handled by first-integer parse); **addition stays clean (≤0.2%) even
  under filler** — another reason addition is the primary.
- **Filler boost generalizes beyond addition:** multiplication +11pp and expression(sum-of-products)
  +16pp both boost → the effect isn't addition-specific (regime-generality evidence).

### Data inspection notes (verifying we measure what we intend)
- `results/inspect_canonical.md`: addition/mul outputs are immediate single integers; incorrect cases
  are genuine near-miss miscomputations (e.g. 785686632 vs 785687242), not parse failures; the gate
  (filler) visibly fixes some errors. Scoring is consistent with `correct` on all 10k+ cached rows.
- **Difficulty-homogeneity:** drove the mul homogenization fix and the expr drop (below).

### Judgment calls (with rationale)
- **Primary family = multi-operand addition, fixed 8-digit operands, varying #operands.** The 8-digit
  column is the cleanest monotonic knob; addition is the most difficulty-homogeneous family and stays
  clean under filler. `add_n15_d8` (54%) is the designated PRIMARY; `add_n12_d8` (68%) is kept as the
  higher-baseline k-headroom level (the k=0 baseline drops as k grows); `add_n15_d6` (49%, +30pp) is
  the max-boost alternative for the downstream power gate.
- **Second family = homogenized multiplication** (`mul_a4_b4_hom`), not raw 4×4: the raw level failed
  the homogeneity check (trailing-zero/small-product easy subpopulation). Reviewer-endorsed,
  principled structural filter.
- **Expression-eval explored but NOT shipped.** A signed sum-of-products level (`expr_t3_d2`) shows a
  clear filler boost (+16pp) and, with a |answer|≥1000 floor, clean multi-digit answers — BUT the
  homogeneity check revealed it is **difficulty-BIMODAL by sign** (negative-answer items ~7% vs
  positive ~32% no-CoT accuracy), violating "one difficulty distribution"; it also sits at the band
  floor (~25%) and has ~6% ragged outputs under filler. Per "don't over-invest in the second family"
  (multiplication already is the clean second family), shipping a flawed third family would violate
  D8, so it was dropped. Its boost is retained as regime-generality evidence. (The negative-scoring
  requirement is still unit-tested.)
- **Canonical-form dedup** (operand multiset / unordered factor pair) added after the reviewer/LLM
  critique flagged `a*b` vs `b*a`; removed 1 such pair in the raw mul pool.

### dataset-reviewer pass — what it surfaced and how it was FIXED
1. expr short answers / below band / unvalidated homogeneity → added magnitude floor, extended the D8
   check to expression, which exposed the sign bimodality → **dropped expr** (clean outcome).
2. `canonical.py` provenance comment cited the wrong lock file → fixed to `LOCK_FILE` and recorded
   `calibration_lock_file` in each level's metadata.
3. desiderata D1 text imprecise for filtered levels → clarified (filtered levels reject by design).
4. Confirmed-good items the reviewer validated: determinism, exact big-int answers (hand-checked),
   robust scoring verified on real outputs, clean primary family with a monotonic knob + band span +
   designated primary + higher-baseline headroom, the mul homogenization, complete metadata.

### Notes
- Cost this segment: ~$11.4 (calibration grid $4.80 + lock $5.58 + small re-runs + critique); cumulative
  ~$68.7 of the $5k budget. Most lock re-runs were ~$0 (cached); `--assert-cached` reproduces at $0.
- **Scope:** did NOT build the `B₁(n)` power-gate sweep/analysis, the k-question experiment, any open
  model, or a new competition-math control (all later segments).

## Segment 2: B₁(n) single-question boost curve + POWER-GATE verdict — 06/03/2026 20:05 - commit ef04241

### What was done
Measured the fine-grained **B₁(n)** single-question filler-boost curve on Opus 4.5 over the
finalized canonical datasets (**fresh** items, `offset>=500`), characterized the rising region per
level, ran the **power-gate separability analysis** (`Δ(n,k)=B₁(n)−B₁(n/k)`), delivered an explicit
**go/no-go verdict**, ran a bounded harder-difficulty probe, and produced a consolidated fresh-item
replication figure. The make-or-break gate **PASSES (GO)** — but only on the max-boost level
`add_n15_d6`, not the originally-designated primary `add_n15_d8`.

Steps:
1. **`run_b1curve.py`** (76,000 calls, $124): paired B₁(n) on 5 levels at a 19-point counting-filler
   grid (realized tok ≈ {0,5,11,15,21,25,31,41,51,65,81,101,125,151,201,301,501,801,1201}), counting
   filler in the prefill, fresh items `offset=500`: `add_n15_d8`/`add_n15_d6`/`add_n12_d8` at n=1000,
   `mul_a4_b4_hom`/`sumprod_t3_d2` at n=500. Robust parser used for mul/sumprod in analysis.
2. **Densification** (12,000 calls, ~$23): added rise-region points for `add_n15_d6`
   ({6,8,9,11,13,14,16,18} items → 13–37 tok) — the gate hinges on d6's rise — and {1,3}-item
   small-n points for d8/d12 to pin n_lo.
3. **`analyze_b1curve.py`** + **`b1lib.py`**: paired bootstrap over items (4000 reps), rising-region
   stats (n_lo, n_hi, plateau, small-n dip), power-gate Δ(n,k) with per-k optimal operating points
   subject to n/k≥n_lo and n≤n_hi, and a back-of-envelope power statement. Outputs
   `results/b1curve_{summary.md,points.csv,gate.csv}`.
4. **`plot_b1curve.py`**: `plots/b1curve.png` (accuracy+boost, linear & log-x, n_lo/n_hi markers),
   `plots/b1curve_gate.png` (Δ(n,k) feasible regions + optimal points), `plots/b1curve_replication.png`
   (consolidated fresh-item boost at ~300 tok). GPT-vision-checked the curve; applied its polish.
5. **`run_harder_probe.py`** + **`analyze_harder_probe.py`** (12,000 calls, $29): B₁(n) at
   add_n20_d8 / add_n15_d10 / add_n20_d10 (diagnostic only; no new canonical data).
6. Data inspection (`sample_b1curve.py` → `results/inspect_b1curve.md`); `--assert-cached` reproduces
   the full curve at **$0**.

### Details and examples
- **Run script** `run_b1curve.py` (counting filler, prefill, `harness.answer_problem`); fresh items
  via `load_canonical(tag, n, offset=500)`. Result files: `results/b1curve_main.jsonl` (76k rows),
  `results/b1curve_dense_d6.jsonl`, `results/b1curve_dense_d8d12.jsonl` (the analysis merges all 3).
- **Realized-token mapping** (measured via Anthropic `input_tokens` delta, level-independent for the
  same filler): realized ≈ 2·items + 1 across the whole range (counting 75 items → 151 tok, 150 → 301,
  500 → 1001), confirming/extending the Segment-0 mapping.
- **Data inspection** `results/inspect_b1curve.md`: outputs are clean (addition 0% truncation/parse-fail;
  the `starts_nonnumeric` 5–11% is the benign `":\n\n<number>"` continuation after the prefill, parsed
  fine). Robust parser fixes only 0.34% (mul) / 0.19% (sumprod) of filler rows (spaced-digit format),

[1042 more lines in file. Use offset=451 to continue.]

but correctly (e.g. `' 19 5 9 5 0 6 4.\n\n19595064'` → 19595064) — so mul/sumprod boosts use it.
  The **d6 dip is genuine**: at 5–11 tok, baseline-correct→wrong (176) exceeds wrong→correct (94),
  net −82 items; the new errors are high-digit carry slips (e.g. 6516202 vs 7516202, off by exactly
  1,000,000). Then 25→29 tok flips +294 net (the sharp step).

### Key findings
**(1) Rising-region characterization (realized tokens; paired bootstrap CIs):**
| level | base | plateau boost | n_lo | n_hi | small-n dip |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n15_d8` (PRIMARY) | 55% | **+15.5pp** [12.4,18.6] | **3** | **7** | none |
| `add_n15_d6` (max-boost) | 49% | **+31.7pp** [28.6,34.7] | **25** | **501** | **yes: −8.2pp @ 5–11 tok** |
| `add_n12_d8` | 68% | **+22.5pp** [19.6,25.4] | **3** | **21** | none |
| `mul_a4_b4_hom` | 47% | +8.1pp [4.2,12.0] | 31 | 31 | yes: −4.2pp @ 5 tok |
| `sumprod_t3_d2` | 39% | +24.8pp [19.9,29.6] | 21 | 125 | yes: −6.2pp @ 5 tok |
  - **8-digit addition saturates almost instantly** (n_hi 7–21 tok): the boost jumps to near-plateau
    by 3–11 tok. **No degradation** to 1200 tok for any level.
  - **`add_n15_d6` is the outlier**: a small-filler **dip** (−8pp at 5–11 tok, carry disruption), a
    flat ~0 region (15–23 tok), a **sharp step** at 25→29 tok (+7 → +29, confirmed densely), then a
    **wide flat plateau** (29–33pp) with no degradation to 1200 tok. **Rising-region width ∝ boost
    magnitude**: d6 (+32pp, wide) > sumprod (+25) > d12 (+22) > d8 (+15, narrow) > mul (+8).
  - **Small-n non-monotonicity CONFIRMED and refuted-where-absent**: the large-boost/wider-region
    levels (d6, sumprod, mul) genuinely **hurt** at ≤11 tok; the fast-saturating d8/d12 do **not** dip.

**(2) POWER-GATE Δ(n,k)=B₁(n)−B₁(n/k), k∈{2,4,8} (feasible = n/k≥n_lo & n≤n_hi):**
| level | k=2 | k=4 | k=8 |
|---|---|---|---|
| `add_n15_d8` | +6.9pp @n=7 | **infeasible** (n_lo·4=12>n_hi=7) | **infeasible** |
| `add_n15_d6` | **+24.0** @n=50 | **+20.0** @n=100 | **+23.3 @n=200** |
| `add_n12_d8` | +11.5 @n=7 | +13.2 @n=12 | infeasible (n_lo·8=24>21) |
| `sumprod_t3_d2` | +9.5 @n=50 | +13.4 @n=84 | infeasible (168>125) |
| `mul_a4_b4_hom` | infeasible | infeasible | infeasible |
  - Power: paired-boost SE ≈ sqrt(2p(1−p)/m) ≈ 1.5pp at m=2000; the d6 Δ≈20–24pp is **~13σ**
    (trivially powered); the k≤4 partials (Δ 6–13pp) are ~4–9σ.

**(3) VERDICT: GO** — at least one canonical level (**`add_n15_d6`**) separates parallel
(B_k=B₁(n)) from divided (B_k=B₁(n/k)) across the full planned k range **k∈{2,4,8}** with a large,
easily-resolvable Δ (~20–24pp), enabled by its **wide no-degradation plateau** (operate at total
filler n≈25·k so n/k≈25 tok sits just above the helping-threshold/steep rise while n stays on the
plateau). The originally-designated PRIMARY `add_n15_d8` (8-digit) is the **worst** level for the
gate (saturates by ~7 tok → only k=2 feasible, Δ=6.9pp). `sumprod_t3_d2` and `add_n12_d8` separate
for **k≤4** with more **gradual (robust) rises**. **Recommendation: switch the main-experiment
primary to `add_n15_d6`**; candidate n-grid ≈ {30,50,100,200,400} tok × k∈{2,4,8} (most-informative
cell per k is n≈25·k); keep n/k ≥ 25 tok (above the dip/threshold); largest usable k = 8.
  - **Caveat (carry-forward):** d6's rise is **steep** (+7→+29 over 25→29 tok), so the divided
    prediction near threshold is sensitive to the exact realized n/k — control filler precisely; the
    gradual-rise levels (sumprod/d12) are more robust but cap at k≤4. The dip means a sub-threshold
    n/k predicts a **negative** divided boost — avoid n/k < 25 tok.

**(4) Harder-difficulty probe:** harder addition does **NOT** reliably widen the rising region.
add_n20_d8 (base 16%) and add_n20_d10 (base 10%) fall to the **floor** where the boost **vanishes**
(+0–1pp, no headroom); only the still-mid-band add_n15_d10 (base 43%) boosts (+15–20pp, rise to
~81 tok then mild degradation). So the wide plateau of `add_n15_d6` is tied to its large boost /
6-digit structure, **not** raw difficulty — naively harder tasks just hit the floor.
![](../plots/b1curve.png) ![](../plots/b1curve_gate.png) ![](../plots/harder_probe.png)

**(5) Consolidated replication (fresh items, ~300 tok, bootstrap CI):** d8 +15.1, d6 +30.5, d12 +22.4,
mul +8.4, sumprod +25.8 pp — confirms the Segment-1 gate boosts on fresh items. ![](../plots/b1curve_replication.png)

### Notes
- Cost this segment: ~$176 (b1curve $124 + densify $23 + harder probe $29); cumulative **~$249** of
  the $5k budget. `--assert-cached` reproduces the full curve at $0.
- **Scope held:** did NOT build the k-question experiment, the rigorous per-cell power analysis /
  operating-point lock-in / pre-registration, any open model, a new competition-math control, or
  finalize/save any new canonical dataset (the harder probe is diagnostic, generated on the fly).
- The gate PASSING means the **conditional harder-task-search segment (Segment 3) is NOT triggered**

  a wider-AND-gradual-rise level (d6 is wide-but-steep; sumprod/d12 are gradual-but-k≤4).

## Segment 2 follow-up: data-inspection-review-driven gate FIX (corrected verdict) — 06/03/2026 20:34 - commit f7e8655

### What was done
Ran the `empirical-ml-data-inspector` (full Segment-2 data review). It confirmed the measurement is
trustworthy (scoring integrity; addition outputs FULLY clean no-CoT — 0 operators/words/truncation
even at 1201 tok across 19k rows; realized tokens level-independent ≈2·items+1; fresh items confirmed
[500,1500)/[500,1000), zero calibration/held-out leakage; d6 dip + 25→29 step are GENUINE carry
effects). It also surfaced **real issues, all FIXED** (not just documented):

1. **(Headline) The power-gate logic was WRONG (`analyze_b1curve.py`).** It capped operating `n` at
   the *saturation point* `n_hi`, but the operating `n` should range over the **whole
   no-degradation plateau** (the parallel prediction `B₁(n)` needs `n` at the high-boost plateau, not
   in the rising region). With the bug, the gate falsely declared k=4/8 "infeasible" for d8/d12 and
   made it look like only `add_n15_d6` worked for k≤8. **Fixed**: the gate now operates `n ∈ [n_sat,
   n_top]` (plateau) with `n/k ≥ n_lo`. **Corrected result: EVERY wide-plateau level is feasible for
   k∈{2,4,8}** (the plateau is so wide that `n_lo·k ≪ n_top`); what differs is Δ magnitude (≈ plateau
   − B₁(n_lo)) and the divided-prediction fragility.
2. **Added a placement-SENSITIVITY metric** (addresses the reviewer's "knife-edge" concern): the
   local slope of B₁ at the divided point `n/k` (pp/tok) and Δ if realized `n/k` is mis-set ±5 tok.
   This revealed the crucial distinction: **`sumprod_t3_d2` has a GRADUAL rise (div-slope 0.3 pp/tok →
   robust: Δ stays 17–19pp under ±5-tok mis-placement)** while **`add_n15_d6` is STEEP (2.9 pp/tok →
   its larger Δ collapses if `n/k` drifts ~5 tok above threshold)**. This flips the recommended primary
   from d6 to **sumprod** (best Δ×robustness; Δ grows with k).
3. **Robust, deterministic plateau/saturation detection.** Replaced the noise-sensitive `n_hi`
   (which the reviewer flagged: a single mid-plateau wiggle at 101 tok pushed d6's old `n_hi` to 501)
   with `n_sat` (saturation onset, within ~2 SE of the denoised peak — d6 now 29 tok, not 501) and
   `n_top` (no-degradation upper bound). Also flagged **degradation** (mul: peaks +12.8@101 →
   +6.2@1201, `degrades=YES`) — corrected the earlier "no degradation any level" over-claim.
4. **Determinism**: the bootstrap seed used Python's salted `hash(level)` → CIs not reproducible
   across runs. **Fixed** to `hashlib.md5` (now bit-reproducible).
5. **mul/sumprod small-n dip is partly a shows-working format confound** (reviewer point C): at ~5–15
   tok, **2.9% of sumprod** rows show working (e.g. `' 4 5 2 4 + 2 0 9 1 + 2 3 2 5 = 8 9 4 0'`,
   first-int-scored wrong) — so the sumprod −6pp dip is inflated by up to ~3pp; **mul small-n is 0%
   shows-work** and **the d6 (primary) dip is clean addition**. Softened the dip claim accordingly.

Re-ran `analyze_b1curve.py` + `plot_b1curve.py` (gate plot now shows the plateau operating region +
div-slope per k, 4 levels incl. sumprod). Rewrote the write_up.md verdict box + Segment-2 gate
section.

### Key findings (CORRECTED gate)
| level | base | plateau | n_lo | n_sat | n_top | degr | Δ k=2/4/8 (pp) | div-slope | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sumprod_t3_d2` | 39% | +24.7 | 21 | 81 | 1201 | no | +9.1/+13.4/**+20.3** | **0.3 robust** | **RECOMMENDED (k≤8)** |
| `add_n15_d6` | 49% | +30.3 | 25 | 29 | 1201 | no | +23.1/+18.7/+22.9 | 2.9 fragile | largest Δ, needs precise filler |
| `add_n12_d8` | 68% | +22.1 | 3 | 7 | 1201 | no | +11.5/+12.4/+13.7 | 2.1 | works |
| `add_n15_d8` | 55% | +16.1 | 3 | 7 | 1201 | no | +6.9/+8.7/+9.5 | 1.6 | works, smallest Δ |
| `mul_a4_b4_hom` | 47% | +10.1 | 31 | 81 | 801 | **YES** | +6.9/+5.8/+3.8 | — | NO-GO (small+degrades) |

**VERDICT unchanged at the top level: GO** — but now correctly **GO for k≤8 on multiple levels**, not
just d6. SE≈1.5pp@m=2000 → sumprod/d6 Δ≈20pp are ≥12σ; d8 Δ≈7–10pp are ~5σ; all GO levels resolvable.
**Recommended main-experiment primary: `sumprod_t3_d2`** (robust gradual rise, Δ grows with k);
`add_n15_d6` as a large-Δ alternative if filler is controlled to ~±1 tok.

### Notes

- `[ERRATUM — applies to the prior Segment 2 entry above]` That entry's power-gate table (which

### What was done
- Ran 3 review subagents (2× research-reviewer, 1× empirical-ml-data-inspector). All flagged the SAME
  top issue: **OpenRouter provider routing was unpinned**, so each call hit a random provider at a
  different quantization. Investigated and CONFIRMED it was a real, large confound; fixed it and
  re-measured. Also added the should-fix robustness items.

### Details and examples
- **Provider-quantization confound (the big one).** DeepSeek-V3-0324 is served on OpenRouter by two
  clusters: HIGH-accuracy (DeepInfra 54.3% / ModelRun 54.1% / SiliconFlow 55.1%) and LOW-accuracy
  (AtlasCloud 27.6% / Novita 22.4% / GMICloud 29.0%, aggressive quants). The provider mix was
  **size-dependent**: the bad cluster served ~45% of n=0 baseline calls but only ~20% of filler cells
  → depressed the baseline → **inflated the boost** (and the bad providers produced the ~3% pathological
  truncations). Fixed via `run_openrouter.py --only-providers deepinfra modelrun siliconflow` (a
  homogeneous-accuracy allowlist, load-balanced). Clean re-run (`results/orboost_GC_deepseek_{dots,
  counting}.jsonl`, n=1000/cell): base 52% (was 39% unpinned), boost **+5.7\u20135.9pp** (was +11), truncation
  3%\u21920.01%, and **counting\u2248dots** (the first-pass "dots>counting" was also an artifact). Provider-stratified
  table in `results/or_deepseek_robustness.txt` confirms the good cluster is homogeneous (54\u201356%).
- **Logit-scale robustness** (OVERALL_PLAN \u00a72): add_n4_d10 logit boost +0.24 [0.12,0.35] counting / +0.23
  [0.12,0.34] dots \u2014 CI excludes 0 (`analyze_or_robust.py`).
- **Error structure** (genuine-computation evidence, addresses the "reduced give-up?" worry): of
  baseline-wrong add_n4_d10 items, 81% carry near-misses, 19% far, **0% give-ups**; filler-plateau fix-rate
  39.8% near vs 18.6% far \u2192 filler differentially repairs carry-precision errors.
- **Config-fair survey** (addresses "compared DeepSeek's best vs others' worst"): re-ran kimi-k2-0905,
  glm-4.6, qwen3-235b at the SAME `add_n4_d10`+dots config (`results/orboost_fair_*.jsonl`). At this config
  GLM +0.2 (null), Qwen \u22121.2 (null/neg), **Kimi \u221252.8 (collapse, not an empties artifact: parse_fail 0.4%)**
  \u2192 strengthens the selection (DeepSeek's boost is genuinely model-specific).
- **Separator-aware open parser** (`or_analysis.parse_answer_open`): handles comma/space/narrow-space(\u202f,
  \u2009)/period thousands separators DeepSeek emits under filler (data-inspector found `228\u202f031\u202f349`\u2192parsed
  228). Applied to open-model analysis only (Opus `parse_answer` untouched). Unit-tested.
- **Disqualification evidence persisted** (`results/inspect_openrouter_disqualified_extra.md`): gpt-oss-120b
  400 "Reasoning is mandatory"; llama-3.3-70b shows working (`'12345678 + 87654321 = ... so ... 133333333'`
  \u2192 first integer = operand 12345678).
- **Plots redone on clean data**: `plots/openrouter_boost_deepseek.png` (add_n4_d10 boost + add_n4_d12 null),
  `plots/openrouter_selection.png` (fair-config bar chart). GPT-vision-checked.
- New/changed code: `run_openrouter.py` (+`--provider`/`--only-providers`/`--ignore-providers`),
  `run_or_calibrate.py` (+`--provider`/`--big`), `or_levels.py` (BIG_GRID + d10\u2013d14 levels),
  `or_analysis.py` (separator parser + open-model robust scoring), `analyze_or_robust.py` (new:

### Key findings

  the Segment-8 open-model main experiment.

## Segment 5 — in-structure B₁(n) re-measurement + pre-saturation/position confound + design lock-in — 06/04/2026 05:54 - commit 32680a3

### What was done
The pre-experiment segment that the Segment-4 pilot triggered. Re-measured the IN-STRUCTURE B₁(n)
curve (multi-turn, **minimal-reveal**, k=1, Opus 4.5, fresh items, m=1000), deeply characterized the
"questions-as-filler"/pre-saturation/position confound, re-confirmed the power gate **in-structure**
3-way (parallel/divided/null), and **finalized + validated the confound-aware design**. Consolidated
verdict: `results/segment5_verdict.md`.

1. **In-structure B₁(n) curves** (`run_kquestion.py --ks 1 --reveal-style minimal`, fine token grid
   {0,24,50,76,100,150,200,300,400,600,800} via counting items {0,12,25,38,50,75,100,150,200,300,400};
   dots 1:1). 5 curves @ m=1000: `kq_instr_{sumprod_cnt,d6_cnt,d6_dots,d10_cnt,d12_cnt}.jsonl`.
   Analysis `analyze_instructure.py`+`instr_lib.py` → `results/instructure_summary.md`,
   `plots/instructure_b1.png` / `_narrowing.png` / `_gate.png`.
2. **Pre-saturation/position confound** (`--reveal-mode sweep_positions`, k∈{2,4,8}, sizes 0/100,
   m=400-500): `kq_pos_{sumprod,d6,d10,d12}.jsonl`. Analysis `analyze_position.py` →
   `results/position_confound.md`, `plots/position_confound.png`.
3. **Reveal-before contrast** sumprod (`kq_pos_sumprod_before.jsonl`) +
   `plots/directedness_contrast.png` (`plot_contrast.py`).
4. **Finalized design + validation:** implemented the **revealed-item-fixed** design
   (`kharness.draw_instance_fixed`, `run_kquestion.py --design fixed_item --positions`); validated on
   Opus (`kq_fixed_opus_{after,before}.jsonl`, `analyze_fixed_validate.py` →
   `results/fixed_validate_opus.md`) and a DeepSeek confirm (`kq_fixed_ds_{after,before}.jsonl`).
5. Robustness fix: `llm_openrouter._is_retryable` now retries `json.JSONDecodeError` (a provider
   returning malformed JSON crashed a DeepSeek run; would have hit the main experiment).

### Key findings
- **The in-structure curve is NOT narrower than Seg-2 single-turn** (the Seg-4 "narrower" claim was a
  FULL-reveal artifact). With the adopted MINIMAL reveal, sumprod's in-structure boost is HIGHER at the
  peak (+37.2pp @150-200 tok vs single-turn +26) but PEAKS-then-DECLINES to +28 by 800 tok (operate
  near the peak). Addition levels transfer within a few pp and keep a flat plateau.
- **sumprod is the ONLY off-ceiling headline level.** The questions-as-filler effect saturates late
  positions to the CEILING (Q2-Q8 → 90-99%) on FAST-saturating levels (add_n15_d10 base 38%→ceilings;
  add_n12_d8 base 68%→ceilings) → their aggregate B_k≈0 is a NO-HEADROOM ARTIFACT. sumprod's GRADUAL
  rise (n_sat~150 tok) means preceding questions only partially help → all k=8 positions stay 21-52%
  (off ceiling). So **add_n15_d10 is REJECTED as a headline/canonical level** (Task 6 = N/A).
- **Position-resolved B_k (sumprod k=8, n=200):** filler helps early/unsaturated positions ~0/+5pp but
  HURTS recency-advantaged late positions (Q5-Q8 −5 to −26pp) — it ERASES the recency advantage
  (pulls Q6-Q8 from 40-52% down to ~25%). So the position-balanced aggregate B_k = −6.8pp (below null)
  is a recency-erasure MIXTURE — the naive aggregate is confounded.
- **The reveal-before/after contrast is the clean disambiguator** (matched layout/recency → the
  difference = the filler×directedness interaction). sumprod k=8: reveal_after B_k −6.8 vs
  reveal_before +12.1 → contrast **+18.9pp** (k=4 +21.7); positive at EVERY position; robust on logit
  (+0.83/+0.92). DeepSeek add_n4_d10 contrast +8.0/+11.1. → **the headline is the B_k≈0 undirected
  null**: the model uses filler when it KNOWS the target but does NOT bank undirected computation.
- **In-structure gate (sumprod counting): GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2.** At n=200: Δ par−div
  +11.8/+22.3 (k4/k8) AND Δ div−null +25.3/+14.7 — both ≫ per-cell SE, so all THREE hypotheses are
  resolvable for k=4,8. k=2 par−div is small at the peak (+2.1) but recovers to +9.7 at n=100. The
  binding constraint (div−null, telling a genuine null from a small divided B_k) is comfortably met
  because the gradual rise keeps divided well above 0.
- **Revealed-item-fixed design: validated but REJECTED as primary** — the variance-reduction rationale
  doesn't materialize (corr(B_k,B1)≈0 → paired SE ≈ unpaired SE). The i.i.d.-draw + position-balancing
  design (already distribution-matched) is equally powered and simpler; fixed kept as an exact-control
  robustness arm.

### Details and examples
- Prompts: minimal-reveal 4-turn (`kharness.build_k_messages(..., reveal_style="minimal")`); fixed
  design = `draw_instance_fixed` (same revealed item across k at a fixed slot). Verified by
  reconstruction (`sample_kquestion.py`). Data-inspection: `results/inspect_instructure.md` (55k rows:
  0% truncation/reasoning/CoT, only ~0.05% sumprod spaced-digit handled by robust scoring),
  `results/inspect_fixed_{opus,ds}.md`.
- LLM review of the design choices (`llm_helper.query_all_models`, claude+gpt): flagged (a) position-
  resolve don't aggregate the confounded mixture, (b) Q1 is a bad fixed slot (distance grows with k),
  (c) add an n=0 reveal-before control (the contrast = the interaction), (d) both scales — all adopted.
- Result files: `kq_instr_*` (curves), `kq_pos_*` (position/confound), `kq_pos_sumprod_before`,
  `kq_fixed_{opus,ds}_{after,before}`. Analyses: `analyze_instructure.py`, `analyze_position.py`,
  `analyze_fixed_validate.py`; plots `instructure_b1/_narrowing/_gate`, `position_confound`,
  `directedness_contrast`.
- Cost this segment ~$272 (Opus in-structure curves are the driver; DeepSeek confirm ~$1). Cumulative
  ~$702.

### Notes
- Next (Segment 6): per-cell sample-size lock-in + n-grid/k-set lock-in (per-k operating n so k=2 gets
  separation) + analysis pre-registration, using these in-structure curves + the validated design.

  DIFFERENT item → within-instance boosts ~independent), so the locked m delivers SE≈1pp under cluster-robust

# Segment 9 — cross-model generalization on DeepSeek-V3-0324 (phase write-up)

> Phase-specific detail for Segment 9. Key takeaways are also in the unified `write_up.md` "Segment 9"
> section. Chronology in `progress_log_segment_9_phase_0.md`. The verdict file is
> `results/seg9_verdict.md`.

## Goal
Does the **Opus headline** — at high k the **undirected** per-question filler boost is **near-null
(≪ any divided reference)** while the **directedness contrast (reveal-before − reveal-after) is
positive** — generalize to a second, **open** model (DeepSeek-V3-0324)? **⚠ What replicates is the
*directedness contrast* and the *`B_k ≪ divided` at k=8*, NOT the near-null MAGNITUDE:** on the clean Q1
read DeepSeek's undirected k=8 boost is +3.4pp (~39% of B₁, significantly POSITIVE) — below divided but
NOT ≈0 (Opus's Q1 was a true null ~7%). "Near-null" appears only on DeepSeek's recency-confounded early
pool. So the replication is of the *sharing/directedness structure*, not the absolute near-null. Scoped as a **focused
generalization**, NOT a full Opus-grade pre-registered power gate (DeepSeek's boost is modest ~+6pp, so
its own divided-vs-null separation is weak; the headline is Opus regardless). Mirror the Opus
Segment-8 methodology on the model-agnostic harness for a fair comparison.

## Setup (all DeepSeek; no Opus re-runs)
- **Model/level:** `deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`, parser=open, reasoning OFF, **provider-pinned good
  cluster** {deepinfra,modelrun,siliconflow} (mandatory — unpinned routing mixes quantizations).
  Level **`add_n4_d10`** (4× 10-digit addition, in-structure k=1 n=0 base **55.8%**). Counting filler,
  minimal reveal, told framing, sweep_positions (position-resolved). `n` in realized tokens (counting ≈
  2 tok/item, re-measured).
- **Runs** (`run_seg9.sh`): k=1 in-structure B₁(n) curve (m=2000, item grid {0,12,25,50,100,200}); k∈{2,4,8}
  reveal_after AND reveal_before, sweep_positions, sizes {0,100,200} items = {0,200,400} tok, m_inst=6000 (k=4,8).
  Per-k operating points keep n/k ≥ ~50 tok: k=2,4 @ 200 tok, k=8 @ 400 tok (alternates co-reported).

## Result 1 — genuine no-CoT re-verified in the k-structure (the gating correctness check)
`results/seg9_nocot.md` (+ reconstructed prompts in `results/inspect_seg9_kquestion.md`). Across **168k**
k-prompt outputs: reasoning field **0%**, output-token median **6**, genuine-violation (operand-detector)
**0.013%** (≤0.036% per cell, all scored WRONG), broad-CoT upper bound ≤0.23%, **score-inconsistent = 0**
(parser=open `correct ⇔ parsed==truth`; benign `:\n<int>` / `: **<int>**` separators parse fine),
truncation ~0.07%, **answered-the-asked-Q = 100%** (0% match a distractor). **add_n4_d10 is genuine no-CoT
in the k-structure.**

## Result 2 — add_n4_d10 is off-ceiling; but only Q1 is a clean primacy read
`results/seg9_offceiling.md`. n=0 reveal-after accuracy by position stays in [5%,85%] at every k (Q1
~53-55%, later positions ~69-77%) → **add_n4_d10 adopted** (no harder level needed). ⚑ **Model-specific
wrinkle:** add_n4_d10 is *fast-saturating*, so Q2-Q4 are **recency-ELEVATED** at n=0 (~70-76%) — unlike
Opus sumprod's flat 20-25% early band. So the undirected reveal-after **early pool is recency-confounded**
(adding filler erases Q2-Q4's recency → drags the early-pool B_k negative), and the cleanest undirected /
contrast read is **Q1-only**. We report BOTH (early pool = Opus-matched; Q1-only = clean) and **lead the
regime read with Q1-only**. **Trade-off (deliberate, scoped-down):** among DeepSeek ADDITION levels only
`add_n4_d10` shows a measurable boost (`add_n4_d12`/`add_n5_d10`/`add_n5_d8` are NULL, Segment 3); we did
NOT calibrate a more GRADUAL non-addition DeepSeek level (e.g. a DeepSeek sum-of-products, the family that
gave Opus its flat early band), so the early-pool cross-model comparison is **not apples-to-apples** —
hence we lean on Q1-only. Searching for a gradual DeepSeek level was out of scope for this focused
generalization.

## Result 3 — light in-structure B₁(n) (the references)
`results/seg9_b1curve.md` (densified 11-pt grid, m=4000). B₁ dips slightly at 11 tok (−1.1, the
carry-disruption small-n dip), rises to ~+7-8pp on the plateau (49-200 tok), +8.7pp @400 tok; n_lo ≈ 23
tok. **The modest boost makes the parallel B₁(n) and divided B₁(n/k) references CLOSE** (e.g. @k=8 n=400
tok: parallel +8.7, divided B₁(50 tok) +7.7) → the 3-way regime is power-limited (expected). The curve is
mildly non-monotone (a noise dip at 299 tok); the divided refs at the operating n/k (50/100/200 tok) are
stable. ![B1](../plots/seg9_b1curve.png)

## Result 4 — the cross-model verdict: PARTIALLY REPLICATES
`results/seg9_verdict.md`. Cluster bootstrap over instances (seed 0). Additive AND logit. The headline
figure: ![contrast](../plots/seg9_contrast.png)

**PRIMARY — directedness contrast (reveal_before − reveal_after, paired, each net of its own n=0):**
positive and **GROWS with k**, sig.+ at k=4,8 on BOTH pools, with a **robustly sig.+ reveal_before
positive control at every k** (the validity guard).

| k | Q1-only contrast (clean) | early-pool contrast (Opus-matched) | reveal_before ctrl (Q1) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | −0.3 [−2.8,+2.1] (NO-EFFECT) | −0.3 (NO-EFFECT) | +7.9 (sig.+) |
| 4 | **+2.6 [+0.8,+4.3]** (POSITIVE) | +5.1 [+3.9,+6.4] (POSITIVE) | +7.1 (sig.+) |
| 8 | **+1.9 [+0.2,+3.6]** (POSITIVE) | +8.9 [+8.0,+9.7] (POSITIVE) | +5.3 (sig.+) |

(m=6000 for k=4,8; for k=2 the early pool = Q1.)

**SECONDARY — undirected regime read (reveal-after, Q1-only least-recency-contaminated):** the undirected
boost **DECLINES with k and is sig. below divided at k=4 AND k=8** — Q1 B_k k2 +8.2 → k4 +4.5 → **k8 +3.4**
(~101% / ~55% / ~39% of the parallel B₁; B_k−divided = −3.1 [−5.2,−1.1] at k4 and −4.3 [−6.3,−2.2] at k8,
both exclude 0; **logit-scale agrees** — sig. below divided at k=4,8). At k=2 the undirected boost is FULL
(≈parallel). The early pool at k=8 even goes slightly **NEGATIVE** (−1.5) via recency-erasure of Q2-Q4 —
the same questions-as-filler effect as Opus. ![position](../plots/seg9_position_bk.png)

**The cleanest cross-k view holds TOTAL filler FIXED** (199 tok, more questions sharing the same pool):
Q1 undirected B_k falls **+8.2 → +4.5 → +1.7** (k=2→4→8) — a clean **divided-pool-shaped decline**
(Δ vs k=2 sig. at k=4 −3.7 [−6.0,−1.4] and k=8 −6.5 [−8.8,−4.3]), falling below even the divided
prediction by k=8. This is the strongest single piece of the "undirected declines with sharing"
evidence (it holds total filler constant, unlike the per-k operating points).
![decline](../plots/seg9_undirected_decline.png)

**Verdict = PARTIALLY REPLICATES (a weaker, task-limited replication).** The qualitative Opus picture
reproduces: (1) a **directedness contrast that EMERGES at k≥4** (≈0 at k=2), positive + sig.+ at k=4,8 on
both pools, with a sig.+ positive control (the cross-k Q1 trend +2.9pp [−0.0,+5.7] (k4−k2) is suggestive but not
formally significant; the early-pool trend +5.1→+8.9 is significant but recency-amplified — so "emerges
at k≥4" is the honest cross-pool read, "grows with k" holds only on the early pool); and (2) the
**undirected boost declines with k and is sig. below divided at k=8**. Two honest cross-model
**differences**:
- **(a) k=2: no directedness penalty.** DeepSeek's k=2 contrast is ≈0 (robust + tight CI + sig.+ control
  — NOT a power artifact) whereas Opus penalizes undirected even at k=2 (contrast +10.9). **NB at k=2 the
  fast-saturating curve makes divided B₁(n/2)=+6.7 ≈ parallel +8.2, so the FULL undirected retention does
  NOT distinguish parallel from divided** — the robust statement is the *contrast* ≈0 (DeepSeek banks the
  undirected boost intact at low sharing). The retention gradient is directionally shallower (Q1
  ≈101%→55%→39% across k=2/4/8 vs Opus 23%→7%). (Densifying the B₁ curve resolved the earlier k=2
  "super-parallel/135%" to ≈101% — it was an under-estimated-B₁ artifact, as the reviewer flagged.)
- **(b) power-limited regime read:** the modest ~+6-8pp boost makes **parallel ≈ divided**, so the 3-way
  regime is NOT crisply resolvable on DeepSeek (graceful degradation — **α NOT fit**, no forced 3-way
  label). The DeepSeek contrast is also ~6× smaller by boost magnitude than Opus (Q1 contrast ~+2 vs ~+25pp, ~10×), as
  expected for the ~6× smaller absolute boost (the Q1 *contrast* ratio is ~8-9×: Opus Q1 ~+24.5 vs
  DeepSeek ~+2.7; "~6×" is the boost-magnitude ratio). The Opus comparison numbers are transcribed from
  the cached Segment-7/8 outputs (`main_run_contrast.csv`; analysis-only, no Opus re-runs).
  ![opus vs ds](../plots/seg9_opus_vs_ds_contrast.png)

### Transfers vs model-specific
- **Transfers (design/scoring/the qualitative finding):** the reveal-by-index apparatus, minimal reveal,
  position-resolution, the directedness contrast + sig.+ positive control, the **questions-as-filler /
  recency-erasure** phenomenology, and the qualitative **directedness-positive + undirected-declining-with-k
  + ≪divided-at-k8** picture.
- **Model-specific (does NOT transfer):** the operating point / mid-band level (add_n4_d10 @ ~56% vs
  sumprod @ ~34%), the boost magnitude (~+6 vs ~+35pp), the realized-token mapping, the **recency profile**
  (DeepSeek recency-elevates Q2-Q4 so only Q1 is a clean read), the **k=2 full-retention** (vs Opus
  sub-divided at k=2), and the **crispness of the 3-way regime read** (power-limited on DeepSeek).

## Data integrity + provider-confound (verified on the seg9 cells)
Genuine no-CoT, scoring consistency, and answered-the-asked-Q are verified in `results/seg9_nocot.md` +
`results/inspect_seg9_kquestion.md` (see Result 1). The Segment-3 provider-quantization confound is
re-checked **on the actual seg9 cells** (`analyze_seg9_provider.py` → `results/seg9_provider.md`): the
pinned good cluster is accuracy-homogeneous (per-provider n=0 spread 6.6pp) and the provider SHARE is
~constant across filler sizes, so the mix-implied n=0 baseline drifts ≤0.66pp across sizes (and ~0pp at the
operating-point sizes) — the mix CANNOT be manufacturing the paired B_k / contrast. One condition-specific
failure mode (data inspector): a handful (~0.1%) of **reveal_before** rows at filler>0 balk ("not provided
in the sequence", parsed=2, scored WRONG) — this DEPRESSES reveal_before → is CONSERVATIVE for the
contrast + the positive control (cannot manufacture a false directedness-positive). genuine no-CoT
show-work is mildly higher in reveal_after (0.035% vs 0.003% at k=8) — anti-conservative by ~0.03pp,
negligible.

## Judgment-heavy choices (with rationale)
- **Pool = Q1-only as the least-recency-contaminated lead read (early-pool co-reported; data-motivated /
  exploratory, NOT pre-specified).** add_n4_d10's fast saturation makes Q2-Q4 recency-elevated, so the
  Opus "early pool = first ⌈k/2⌉" heuristic mixes the clean primacy Q1 with recency-elevated positions;
  on the undirected arm filler erases that recency → the early-pool B_k is dragged negative (a recency
  artifact, not the science). Q1 is the recency-free primacy read (conservative for the contrast: Q1 +2.7
  < early +9.9). Caveat: 'clean' = recency-free, NOT artifact-free (Q1 has its own primacy/low-baseline
  headroom). I report BOTH pools transparently; the directedness conclusion holds on both + the sig.+
  control. (This choice was made AFTER seeing the recency profile in the data → flagged exploratory.)
- **Operating points per-k (n/k ≥ ~50 tok):** k=2,4 @ 200 tok, k=8 @ 400 tok, so the divided reference
  stays on the plateau (not sub-threshold). Co-reported alternates confirm robustness.
- **Provider cluster (not single-provider):** the good-cluster mix is BALANCED across filler sizes
  (verified) so the paired boost is unbiased; staying on the cluster also reuses the cached pilot draws.
- **NO α-fit / NO forced 3-way label:** DeepSeek's modest boost makes parallel≈divided ill-separated;
  forcing a label would over-claim. Graceful degradation per the instructions.

## ⚠ Overarching caveat — cross-MODEL is confounded with cross-LEVEL
Opus ran on the gradual `sumprod` (flat low early band) and DeepSeek on the fast-saturating `add_n4_d10`
(recency-elevated Q2-Q4) — **different levels** (only `add_n4_d10` boosts among DeepSeek addition levels,
and a gradual non-addition DeepSeek level was NOT calibrated — out of scope). So **every cross-model
difference here (the k=2≈0, the smaller magnitude) is confounded with level/saturation** and cannot be
cleanly attributed to the MODEL. The tight k=2 CI rules out a power artifact, NOT a level effect. This is
the headline caveat of the whole cross-model read; the *direction* of the qualitative finding
(directedness-positive, undirected sub-divided at high k) transfers, the model-vs-level attribution does not.

## Interpretation caution (mirrors the Opus caveat)
A positive reveal-before − reveal-after contrast shows a **target-conditioned advantage** (knowing the
target before the filler helps), which is **SUGGESTIVE of directed hidden computation but does NOT
uniquely prove it** — alternatives include attention/routing to the known target, easier maintenance of
one answer vs several, reveal-after index-selection overhead, and position/recency interactions. The
robust, falsifiable claims are the asymmetry itself + the undirected ≪ divided read at k=8, NOT a proven
compute-allocation mechanism. (Same caution as the Opus headline.)

## Limitations / honest caveats
- The clean (Q1-only) directedness contrast at k=4,8 is **small (+2.6/+1.9pp) and only just sig.+**
  (CIs [+0.8,+4.3] / [+0.2,+3.6]); it's robust on the early pool and on the logit scale and anchored by the
  sig.+ positive control, but it is a modest effect (DeepSeek's whole boost is ~+6-8pp).
- The 3-way regime is **power-limited** (parallel≈divided) — the regime read leans on the k=8 undirected
  being below divided + the contrast + the fixed-filler decline, NOT a crisp parallel/divided/null partition.
- The **k=2 ≈0 contrast / full undirected retention** is NOT a power artifact (tight CIs, sig.+ positive
  control), but whether it is a **MODEL** property or a **LEVEL/saturation** property is unresolved
  (Opus and DeepSeek are on different levels — see the overarching caveat). At low sharing DeepSeek banks
  undirected computation readily, but the cleanest divided-vs-parallel test is degenerate there (saturated).
- One family/level (add_n4_d10); the recency-profile difference means the early-pool comparison to Opus
  is not apples-to-apples (Q1-only is); and the whole cross-model contrast is confounded with the level
  difference (overarching caveat above).
- The B₁ reference curve is mildly non-monotone (a noise dip at 299 tok); the divided refs at the
  operating n/k (50/100/200 tok) are stable so the sub-divided conclusion is robust, but the curve is
  not a perfectly clean plateau.
