# Segment 10 — robustness of the (evolved) regime verdict (Opus 4.5, sumprod_t3_d2) — phase write-up

> Phase-specific detail. Key takeaways are also in the unified `write_up.md` "Segment 10" section.
> Chronology + data-inspection notes: `progress_log_segment_10_phase_0.md`. The full verdict file is
> `results/seg10_verdict.md`; no-CoT re-verification `results/seg10_nocot.md`; figures `plots/seg10_*.png`.

## Goal
Stress-test the **evolved Opus 4.5 headline** — at high k the **undirected** per-question filler boost is
**near-null / `B_k ≪ divided`** while the **directedness contrast (reveal-before − reveal-after) is
POSITIVE**, read position-resolved (early-pool + Q1-only) on additive AND logit — against the three
pre-planned variations, **on Opus only at `sumprod_t3_d2`**, mirroring the Segment-8 methodology
(comparison-SE/SE_R off the matching k=1 curve; the reveal-before positive control as a validity guard;
cluster bootstrap over instances, seed 0). **All EXPLORATORY robustness — NOT the pre-registered
confirmatory family; no α-fit (ill-defined at the null); no re-declaration; the locked
grid/harness/scorers/preregistration are untouched.**

The three variations: **V1 told-vs-not-told framing (highest priority)**, **V2 alternative k∈{3,6,16}**,
**V3 counting-vs-dots filler (lighter)**.

## Setup (Opus 4.5 only; the locked apparatus)
- `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`, temp 0, thinking OFF, max_tokens 48, assistant prefill; the validated 4-turn
  **minimal-reveal** harness; `--reveal-mode sweep_positions` (position-balanced) analyzed position-resolved
  (early pool = first ⌈k/2⌉ positions; **Q1-only co-reported**); robust scorer for sumprod; additive AND
  logit (Haldane). **Realized tokens (verified): counting = 2 tok/item, dots = 1 tok/item.**
- **Told/counting baseline = the cached Segment-7 cells** (`results/seg7_sumprod_*`), reused not re-run.
- **reveal_before is FRAMING-INVARIANT** (it names the target in turn 1 regardless of told/not_told), so the
  not_told reveal_before cells are byte-identical to the told ones and **cache-hit ($0)** — empirically
  confirmed. Hence the told→not_told manipulation bites **only on reveal_after**.
- Run manifest `run_segment10.sh` (`ASSERT_CACHED=1` reproduces at $0 — verified) + the review-driven
  disclosure follow-up `scripts_segment_10_phase_0/run_disclosure.py` (the `disclose` arm = told minus the
  encouragement clause; built by post-processing `build_k_messages`, so the locked harness is untouched).
  Analysis: `seg10_lib.py` (→ `seg8_lib`/`seg9_lib`), `analyze_seg10_verdict.py` (→ `results/seg10_verdict.md`
  + `seg10_cells.csv` + `seg10_contrast.csv`), `analyze_seg10_nocot.py` (→ `results/seg10_nocot.md`),
  `plot_seg10.py`, `sample_seg10.py` (→ `results/inspect_seg10.md`). Marginal spend ≈ **$470** (cumulative
  ≈ $1758).

## Gating correctness check — genuine no-CoT holds under ALL new conditions
`results/seg10_nocot.md` (290k k-prompt outputs, incl. the disclose arm). The framing change is the highest
measuring-what-you-want risk (in Segment 0 a "scratch space to work" framing silently induced CoT), so this
is the binding check. **reason% = 0 everywhere** (incl. not-told — the framing change does NOT enable
reasoning), out-tok median 7, **genuine-violation < 1% in every cell** (not-told 0%; the worst is dots k=4
at 0.29% — the dots show-work quirk, all scored WRONG), trunc ~0, score-inconsistent **0**, answered-asked
~100%. Reconstructed-prompt inspection (`results/inspect_seg10.md`) confirms the not-told prompt removes the
"be ready for ANY of them" encouragement and the outputs are bare integers. **All new conditions are
genuine no-CoT.**

## V1 — told-vs-not-told framing (HIGHEST priority) — the headline PATTERN is FRAMING-DEPENDENT
The single most important result of the segment. **[This section was substantially corrected after the
review round — see "What breaks vs survives".]** Under not-told the undirected reveal-after boost is
**much larger and concentrates on Q1**; the undirected near-null REGIME leg breaks, while the directedness
contrast leg WEAKENS but SURVIVES on its primary read. A follow-up **disclosure arm** pins the driver.

**The numbers (early-pool reveal-after `B_k` + Q1-only B_k + the directedness contrast on BOTH reads):**

| k | TOLD early B_k | NOT-TOLD early B_k | NOT-TOLD Q1 B_k | TOLD contrast (early / Q1) | **NOT-TOLD contrast (early / Q1)** |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | +7.8 | +22.9 | +22.9 | +10.9 / +10.9 | **−4.2 / −4.2 (early=Q1 at k=2; sig. REVERSAL)** |
| 4 | −4.0 | +11.2 | +30.3 | +24.2 / +27.1 | **+9.1 [+5.8,+12.0] POS / −3.1 AMBIGUOUS** |
| 8 | +2.5 | +8.5 (≈divided) | **+26.3** | +16.2 / +24.5 | **+10.2 [+8.2,+12.3] POS / −0.6 AMBIGUOUS** |

The **directedness contrast on the pre-registered PRIMARY early-pool read SURVIVES** not-told at k=4,8
(+9.1, +10.2, significantly positive — WEAKENED from told's +24.2/+16.2, but not gone); it collapses ONLY
on the Q1-only co-report (AMBIGUOUS at k=4,8 — CI covers 0 but exceeds the ±5pp equivalence margin; a small
significant REVERSAL at k=2, whose early pool IS Q1). **Why:** Q1 is the model's default undirected target,
so directing to Q1 adds nothing (Q1 contrast ≈ 0), while directing to the non-default Q2–Q4 still helps
(hence the early pool stays +9–10pp). **The not-told contrast is SMALL only because the not-told
reveal_after baseline ALREADY defaults to Q1** (it lacks the disclosure). The genuinely CONFOUND-MINIMAL
directedness test is **reveal_before − disclose** (both arms carry the random-target disclosure, so the
disclose baseline does NOT default to Q1 — they differ ONLY by naming the target): it is **LARGE and
framing-robust** (k=4 early +24.8 / Q1 +27.7; k=8 early +16.2 / Q1 +23.7, sig.+). So directing latent
compute to a named target is robust and large (~+24) against any baseline that doesn't already default to
the target; the +9–10 not-told number is small ONLY because not-told pre-banks Q1.

- **NOT a baseline-headroom artifact:** at k=4,8 the not-told early-pool n=0 baseline is HIGHER than told
  (k=8: 27.2% vs 22.3%), yet the boost is larger; the boosts are much larger on the **logit** scale too
  (k=8 early: told +0.14 → not-told +0.39; Q1 even more). The per-position n=0 baselines are IDENTICAL
  between counting/dots and ~identical between told/not-told at Q1 (20.3% vs 20.5%) — so Q1's +1.2→+26.3
  jump is purely the framing. **Dose-response:** the not-told Q1 boost scales with n (+18.7 @100 tok →
  +26.3 @200 tok) → a genuine FILLER effect, not just "not-told answers Q1 better at any n".
- **The behaviour — the model concentrates filler benefit on Q1 under not-told.** The per-position k=8 boost
  profile shows the not-told boost is **concentrated almost entirely on Q1 (+26.3, ≈68% of the framing-matched
  not-told lone ~+38.5)**, with Q2–Q5 small and Q6–Q8 still recency-erased (aggregate still −3.0). Under told
  the early positions get a small DIFFUSE boost (Q1–Q4: +1.2/+4.0/+2.2/+2.5, peaking at Q2, not Q1). It's
  **SUPPRESSION, not redistribution** — the clean baseline-matched Q1 term (n=0 20.3% vs 20.5%) is +1.2 (told)
  vs +26.3 (not-told): told banks far less on Q1, it doesn't relocate it (logit agrees; the summed Q1–Q4
  +9.9 vs +33.8 points the same way but is partly headroom-mixed, so we lead with the Q1 term).
- **⭑ Disclosure disambiguation — it's the DISCLOSURE, NOT the encouragement (a review-driven follow-up,
  `scripts_segment_10_phase_0/run_disclosure.py`).** The told turn-1 sentence changes three things vs
  not-told (discloses one-of-k-random + encourages readiness + longer). A **`disclose` arm = told MINUS the
  "— so be ready to answer any of them" clause** isolates them: `disclose` behaves like TOLD (k=8 Q1 +2.0 ≈
  told +1.2 ≪ not-told +26.3; k=4 −0.5 ≈ told +0.1). So **the random-target DISCLOSURE** ("one of these,
  chosen at random, will be asked") suppresses the Q1-default banking; the **"be ready for any" encouragement
  is INCIDENTAL**. (Genuine no-CoT re-verified on the disclose arm: reason 0%, viol <0.12%.) Plausible reading:
  once the model knows ANY question may be quizzed, Q1 is no longer a safe default to invest filler compute in.
  This **resolves** the earlier "three things change at once" caveat. ![V1 framing](../plots/seg10_v1_framing.png)

**V1 verdict — what BREAKS vs what SURVIVES (precise; corrected after review):**
- The **headline PATTERN (directedness-positive + undirected near-null, as a package) is FRAMING-DEPENDENT**,
  carried by the **REGIME leg breaking**: under not-told the undirected Q1 boost jumps to +26.3 (EXCEEDS
  divided; the early-pool average +8.5 reaches ≈divided), so the original *interpretation* **"the model does
  not bank UNDIRECTED computation" is REFUTED** (it banks ~one question's worth, on Q1). **Position-resolution
  is load-bearing here:** the position-balanced early-pool average landing AT divided would naively look like
  divided-pool banking — only resolving by position reveals it is Q1-CONCENTRATION, not an even split.
- The **directedness-contrast leg SURVIVES (weakened)** on the PRIMARY early-pool read (not-told k=4,8
  +9.1/+10.2, sig.+); it collapses only on Q1-only (because Q1 is the default target). **An earlier draft
  wrongly said the contrast "vanishes / is a told-framing artifact" — that is RETRACTED.** Directedness to a
  NAMED target is framing-robust; the confound-minimal test (reveal_before − disclose, holding the
  disclosure fixed) is LARGE (k=8 Q1 +23.7, k=4 Q1 +27.7) — the +9–10 not-told number is small only because
  not-told pre-banks Q1.
- The deeper **"NO PARALLEL latent thinking across k" SURVIVES / is REINFORCED**: under NEITHER framing does
  the model bank >~1 question's worth (not-told concentrates ~1 on the single Q1; told suppresses even that).
  What the framing (specifically the disclosure) changes is how MUCH committed computation happens and WHERE
  it lands — not whether the model thinks in parallel (it never does).
- **Operational scope:** the Segment-8 "directed ≫ undirected" *magnitude* is a told-framing statement (the
  contrast is ~2× smaller on the neutral not-told prompt, though still sig.+) — the scope Segment 8 flagged
  and deferred here, now characterized; directedness itself is framing-robust.

## V2 — alternative k∈{3,6,16} (told, counting) — HOLDS (not a k∈{2,4,8} artifact)
The cleanest cross-k view holds **TOTAL filler FIXED at 200 tok** and reads the undirected per-question
boost as more questions share the SAME pool. All new k are off-ceiling (k=3 32–41%, k=6 22–46%, k=16
12–40% at n=0). The undirected Q1 boost stays **≪ the divided reference at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (divided
+33 → +13; measured ≤ +7), and the **directedness contrast is DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE at every k** (k=3
+22.8, k=6 +16.2, k=16 +15.0) with a sig.+ reveal-before control everywhere.

| k | n/k tok | undirected Q1 B_k | divided B₁(n/k) | early-pool contrast |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 100 | +6.8 [+4.5,+9.0] | +33 | +10.9 |
| 3 | 67 | +4.1 [+2.0,+6.2] | +27 | +22.8 |
| 4 | 50 | +0.1 [−2.9,+3.2] | +24 | +24.2 |
| 6 | 33 | −1.4 [−3.6,+0.8] | +17 | +16.2 |
| 8 | 25 | +1.2 [−1.1,+3.6] | +14 | +16.2 |
| 16 | 12 | +4.5 [+1.8,+7.3] | +13 | +15.0 |

The **robust claim is `B_k ≪ divided` at EVERY k, NOT a clean monotone decline** (the Q1 sequence +6.8 →
+4.1 → +0.1 → −1.4 → +1.2 → +4.5 is non-monotone with overlapping CIs; k=16 +4.5 rebounds — n/16=12.5 tok
is in the small-n dip where the divided ref is ill-defined and `np.interp`-clamps to B₁(24 tok)). What the
data establish is: the undirected boost stays far below divided (+33→+13) at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16},
largest at the least-shared k=2 — i.e. the model does NOT bank a divided-pool's worth that GROWS with the
shared pool. **V2 verdict: HOLD** (the `≪ divided` regime leg + the positive contrast hold across the finer
k grid; it does NOT establish a monotone decline). ![V2 decline](../plots/seg10_v2_decline.png)

## V3 — counting-vs-dots filler (told; lighter) — HOLDS / strengthens (not counting-specific)
Dots = 1 tok/item → matched the **realized-token** operating point (200 tok = 200 dots items). The dots k=1
B₁(n) curve is similar to counting (peaks ~+32 vs +35.5) but **saturates faster** (so the dots divided
reference is HIGHER: B₁(25 tok)=+24 vs counting +14). The dots undirected reveal-after B_k is **negative**
(k=8 early −3.3, Q1 −5.2; k=4 early −10.2, Q1 −6.5) — even further below the (higher) dots divided
reference, i.e. **dots interferes MORE at every position** (the per-position profile is more negative than
counting at all 8 positions; n=0 baselines are identical since the n=0 prompt is filler-free). The
**directedness contrast is DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE and if anything larger** (k=4 +29.0, k=8 +21.7) with a
sig.+ control. Show-work (dots' ~0.1–0.3% restate-then-compute) is scored WRONG (NOT "reassembled" — that
applies only to the rare COUNTING spaced-digit ANSWER format); dropping the flagged rows leaves the contrast
identical (+21.7 vs +21.7). **⚠ Unexplained dots×k interaction (flagged for Segment 11):** dots ≈ counting
at k=1 (lone B₁ +32 vs +35.5) but is strongly DISRUPTIVE in the k-structure (negative even at the clean Q1
— k=8 −5.2, k=4 −6.5), whereas counting is ~null; one hypothesis is that a long undelimited run of dots
after k questions reads as a disengagement/no-op signal more than counting does. It only deepens `B_k ≪
divided`, so it doesn't affect the verdict. **V3 verdict: HOLD** (not counting-specific; dots strengthens
both legs). ![V3 dots](../plots/seg10_v3_dots.png)

## Consolidated robustness verdict
| variation | directedness contrast | undirected `B_k ≪ divided` / near-null | headline pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| **V1 framing** | **SURVIVES (weakened)** on the PRIMARY early-pool read (not-told k=4,8 +9.1/+10.2 sig.+, vs told +24.2/+16.2); collapses only on Q1-only (AMBIGUOUS; sig. REVERSAL k=2) | **BREAKS**: not-told Q1 +26.3 (n-scaling) EXCEEDS divided; early-pool avg ≈divided — "doesn't bank undirected" REFUTED | **BREAK (pattern) — framing-DEPENDENT**, carried by the REGIME leg; driver = the random-target DISCLOSURE (not encouragement) |
| **V2 alt-k** | HOLDS (+15 to +23, sig.+ ctrl ∀k) | HOLDS (≪ divided ∀k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}; NOT a clean monotone decline) | **HOLD** |
| **V3 dots** | HOLDS / strengthens (+22 to +29) | HOLDS / strengthens (negative, ≪ divided) | **HOLD** |

**Overall:** Within the TOLD framing (where the headline was declared), the headline is **ROBUST to k and
filler-type** (V2+V3 HOLD): the undirected boost stays `≪ divided` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16} and the
contrast is positive for counting AND dots. The headline **PATTERN is FRAMING-DEPENDENT** (V1), carried by
the **regime/near-null leg breaking**: under not-told the model concentrates ~1 question's worth of filler
benefit on its default Q1 (Q1 +1.2→+26.3, exceeding divided), so "doesn't bank undirected computation" is
REFUTED. The **disclosure disambiguation** pins the driver to the random-target **DISCLOSURE** (the
encouragement is incidental). **The directedness-contrast leg SURVIVES (weakened) on its primary early-pool
read** — the earlier "contrast vanishes" claim was WRONG and is retracted; directedness to a named target is
framing-robust (the confound-minimal reveal_before−disclose contrast is ~+24, Q1 +23.7). The deeper **"no PARALLEL latent thinking across k"
SURVIVES / is REINFORCED** (the model banks ≤~1 question's worth under EITHER framing). Position-resolution
is load-bearing: the not-told early-pool average lands AT divided, and only position-resolution shows it is
Q1-concentration, not divided-spreading.

## Judgment-heavy choices (with rationale)
- **Reuse the cached Segment-7 told/counting cells as the baseline** (instructed; deterministic draws →
  byte-identical) and **reuse the framing-invariant reveal-before cells for the not-told before arm**
  (verified cache-hit). This makes the told-vs-not-told comparison exactly paired and free.
- **Lead the V1/V2 reads with Q1-only as the cleanest recency-free position** (early-pool co-reported). The
  early pool mixes recency-confounded positions (Q2–Q4) whose behaviour differs between conditions; Q1 is the
  recency-free primacy read. This is essential for V1 (the early-pool contrast stays positive under not-told
  for a different reason than the Q1 contrast — see above). I report both transparently.
- **Build a not-told k=1 reference curve** (rather than reuse the told curve) so the not-told regime read
  uses a fully not-told reference. The two k=1 curves are similar (not-told n=0 ~5pp lower at k=1, B₁ shape
  comparable) — documented; the divided references differ by ≤3pp.
- **k=16 included but caveated** (off-ceiling, but n/16=12.5 tok < n_lo → its divided reference sits in the
  small-n dip; reported for the decline-extension only, not as a clean regime cell).
- **Dots matched on realized tokens, not item count** (instructed; dots=1 tok/item vs counting=2) +
  robust scorer + show-work monitoring (Segments 2/4 caveat).
- **Labeled everything EXPLORATORY robustness**; no α-fit (ill-defined at the null); the locked
  grid/harness/scorers/preregistration are untouched.

## Honest caveats / limitations
- **The V1 driver IS isolated (disclosure, not encouragement)** via the `disclose` arm — this resolves the
  earlier "three things change at once" caveat. The remaining nuance: "disclosure" still bundles the
  *information* (one-of-k will be asked) with the implied *uniform target prior*; we don't separate those
  (both are "the model now knows it can't safely bet on Q1"). The exact cognitive mechanism (active
  suppression vs not-committing-to-Q1) is not separable from this data; all readings yield the same empirical
  conclusion (not-told concentrates ~1 question's worth on Q1; disclosure suppresses it).
- **The directedness-contrast leg SURVIVES (weakened) on the primary early-pool read** (a corrected claim —
  the earlier "contrast vanishes / is a told-framing artifact" was WRONG). Only the *magnitude* of the
  Segment-8 "directed ≫ undirected" contrast is told-framing-specific (~2× smaller, still sig.+, on not-told);
  directedness ITSELF (directing latent compute to a named target) is framing-robust, and not-told is its
  reference (reveal_before−disclose ~+24 vs +9–10 against the not-told baseline that pre-banks Q1). The original "does-not-bank-undirected-computation" *interpretation* IS refuted under
  not-told (the model banks ~1 question's worth on Q1); only the deeper "no parallel banking across k"
  survives. (None of this changes Segment 8's verdict, which scoped the null to the told framing.)
- **One family/level (sumprod_t3_d2)** — the off-ceiling headline level, as in Segments 7/8.
- **⚠ Unexplained dots×k interaction** (V3): dots ≈ counting at k=1 but strongly disruptive (negative even at
  Q1) in the k-structure — flagged for Segment 11; it only deepens `B_k ≪ divided`, so it doesn't affect the
  V3 HOLD.
- **k=16 divided reference is ill-defined** (n/16=12.5 tok in the dip; `np.interp` clamps it to B₁(24 tok));
  the k=16 Q1 rebound is honestly flagged; k=16 is a decline-EXTENSION, not a clean regime cell.
- Sample sizes (m_inst 600–3000) give tight CIs at the operating points but the alt-k grid is leaner than the
  Segment-7 headline (exploratory, by design).

### Key findings
- **V1 (framing) — the headline is FRAMING-DEPENDENT (BREAK); the "told" encouragement is LOAD-BEARING.**
  Under not-told the undirected boost is much larger and CONCENTRATES on Q1: k=8 Q1 reveal-after
  +1.2 (told) → **+26.3 (not-told)** ≈ the directed reveal-before Q1 boost (+25.7) → the Q1-only
  **directedness contrast COLLAPSES to ~0** (k=8 −0.6 [−5.1,+4.1] NO-EFFECT; k=4 −3.1; k=2 −4.2). NOT a
  baseline-headroom artifact (not-told n=0 is HIGHER at k=4,8; logit agrees; n=0 Q1 baselines identical).
  Per-position profile: not-told concentrates on Q1 while told spreads thin (Q1–Q4 +1.2/+4.0/+2.2/+2.5).
  **The deeper "no parallel latent thinking" conclusion is REINFORCED** — under both framings the model banks
  ≤1 question's worth; the framing only moves WHERE that single-question budget lands.
  ![V1](../plots/seg10_v1_framing.png)
- **V2 (alt-k {3,6,16}) — HOLDS.** Fixed-total-filler (200 tok) undirected Q1 boost stays ≪ divided at every
  k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16} (divided +33→+13, measured ≤+7), declining from +6.8 (k=2); contrast DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE
  at every k (k=3 +22.8 … k=16 +15.0), sig.+ control. Not a k∈{2,4,8} artifact. (Decline not perfectly
  monotone — k=16 Q1 +4.5 sits in the n/16=12.5-tok dip; honestly flagged.) ![V2](../plots/seg10_v2_decline.png)
- **V3 (dots) — HOLDS / strengthens.** Dots undirected B_k is NEGATIVE (k=8 Q1 −5.2, early −3.3) — even
  further below the (higher, fast-saturating) dots divided ref (+24); dots interferes more at every position.
  Contrast DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE, even larger (k=4 +29.0, k=8 +21.7), sig.+ control. Not counting-specific.
  ![V3](../plots/seg10_v3_dots.png)
- **Consolidated:** within the told framing the headline is ROBUST to k and filler-type (V2+V3 HOLD); it is
  FRAMING-DEPENDENT (V1 BREAK). The robust, framing-independent conclusion is the deeper anti-parallel one.

# Segment 11 — mechanism & confound investigation (phase write-up)

> Phase-specific detail. Key takeaways are also in the unified `write_up.md` "Segment 11" section.
> Chronology + data-inspection notes: `progress_log_segment_11_phase_0.md`. Consolidated verdict:
> `results/seg11_verdict.md`; per-tier: `results/seg11_{framing,mechanism,default,cap,dots,nocot}.md`;
> figures `plots/seg11_*.png`.

## Goal
Harden the EVOLVED headline for the final synthesis (Segment 12). Two motivations: (1) the Segment-10
V1 reframe ("not-told → the model banks ~1 question's worth on its DEFAULT Q1; the random-target
DISCLOSURE suppresses it") rested on **one family (Opus sumprod)** — confirm it generalizes; (2) the
genuine-hidden-computation interpretation has been "suggestive, not proven" since Segment 8 — move it
to "evidenced or honestly bounded." Plus characterize the default-target ("why Q1") and the
context-interference cap, and lightly probe the dots×k and d6 puzzles. **All EXPLORATORY mechanism work
— the locked grid/harness/scorers/preregistration are UNTOUCHED; no α-fit; no re-declaration.** Mirrors
the Segment-8 methodology (position-resolved early-pool + **Q1-only**, additive AND logit, cluster
bootstrap over instances seed 0, the reveal-before positive control as a validity guard) and re-verifies
genuine no-CoT for every new framing/level/model/filler cell.

## Setup
- Opus 4.5 (`sumprod_t3_d2`, `add_n15_d6`) + DeepSeek-V3-0324 (`add_n4_d10`, parser=open,
  provider-pinned good cluster). Minimal-reveal 4-turn harness, `sweep_positions`, counting (2 tok/item)
  + a dots probe (1 tok/item). told baselines REUSE the cached Segment-7 (Opus) / Segment-9 (DeepSeek)
  cells. The **disclose** arm (told MINUS the "— so be ready to answer any of them" clause) is built by
  post-processing `build_k_messages` (`scripts_segment_11_phase_0/run_disclosure_gen.py`, generalized
  from Segment 10 to any level/model) — the locked harness is NOT modified.
- Runner manifest `run_segment11.sh` + `scripts_segment_11_phase_0/launch_all.sh`. Analysis libs
  `seg11_lib.py` (→ seg7/8/9/10 machinery), `analyze_seg11_{nocot,framing,mechanism,default,cap,dots,
  verdict}.py`, `plot_seg11.py`, `sample_seg11.py`. Marginal spend ≈ **$409** (cumulative ≈ $2167;
  DeepSeek provider-pinned ~free; the d6/sumprod-k8 runs are the cost — long prompts).

## Gating check — genuine no-CoT holds for EVERY new cell (`results/seg11_nocot.md`)
352k k-prompt outputs across 15 new cells. **reason% = 0 everywhere** (the not-told/disclose framing
change on a NEW family (d6) and a NEW model (DeepSeek) does NOT silently enable reasoning),
**genuine-violation 0.019% overall** (worst per-file ≤0.05%, all scored WRONG), **score-inconsistent =
0**, answered-asked ~100%. The only flag is **DeepSeek not-told k=4 truncation 1.15%** — verified BENIGN
"answer-first-then-continue" (the asked answer is the FIRST token, parsed correctly; the model then
keeps answering Q2,Q3…/adds a "(Note:…)" epilogue to max_tokens; only 2/369 truncated rows are genuine
CoT; the Q1 boost is robust to dropping ALL truncated rows, +7.4→+7.8; concentrated at n=0 so if
anything conservative). The d6 not-told/disclose cells are COMPLETELY clean (0% on every metric).
Reconstructed-prompt inspection (`results/inspect_seg11.md`) confirms the not-told prompt drops the
encouragement, the disclose prompt keeps "chosen at random." but drops the encouragement, and the
fixed_item draws place the SAME revealed item across positions.

## TIER 1.1 — the not-told Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression finding REPLICATES (cross-family + cross-model)
`results/seg11_framing.md` (+ `seg11_framing.csv`); figure ![framing](../plots/seg11_framing.png).
Undirected **Q1-only** boost B_k at k=8 (operating point), told vs disclose vs not-told:

| level / model | n0 Q1 | told | disclose | **not-told** | divided ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sumprod (Opus, original) | 20% | +1.2 | +2.0 | **+26.3 [+23.0,+29.6]** | +13.7 |
| **add_n15_d6 (Opus, cross-FAMILY)** | 48–55% | −2.2 | −3.4 | **+10.6 [+8.1,+13.1]** | +15.0 |
| **add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek, cross-MODEL)** | 53–58% | +3.4 | +2.2 | **+6.2 [+4.7,+7.8]** | +7.7 |

(k=4 agrees: d6 told +1.9 / disclose +2.2 / **not-told +12.5**; DeepSeek told +4.5 / **not-told +7.4**.)
**REPLICATES.** On BOTH the new family and the new model — as on sumprod — the model commits a positive
undirected Q1 boost ONLY under not-told, and the random-target **DISCLOSURE** (disclose ≈ told ≪
not-told) suppresses it. The magnitude tracks the **Q1 headroom** (sumprod n0 20% → +26; d6/DeepSeek n0
~50% → +6–11) and the model's overall boost size — so the smaller cross-family/cross-model magnitudes
are expected, not a failure to replicate. **Logit agrees** (not-told logit sumprod +1.23, d6 +0.43,
DeepSeek +0.27, all ≫ told). The **directedness contrast also behaves as on sumprod**: the confound-
minimal **reveal_before − disclose** test is large and framing-robust (d6 k=8 Q1 +20.0; DeepSeek k=8 Q1
+5.1), while the contrast against the not-told baseline weakens (because not-told pre-banks Q1). **The
reframe is NOT sumprod-specific.** (d6's later positions go strongly negative under not-told via
carry-disruption × recency-erasure — its regime is carry-fragile, so we lead with Q1 + logit per the
plan; the DIRECTION is unambiguous. The huge d6 late-position negatives — k=8 Q8 −60pp — are the
Tier-3.6 carry anomaly, below.)

## TIER 1.2 — genuine hidden computation vs attention/retrieval reallocation: STRENGTHENED (honestly bounded)
`results/seg11_mechanism.md` (+ `seg11_dose.csv`, `seg11_selectivity.csv`). Opus sumprod, k=8.

**(A) DIRECTED dose-response — necessary, not sufficient.** ![dose](../plots/seg11_dose.png) The directed
(reveal_before) Q1 boost RISES with filler tokens and TRACKS the lone B₁(n) shape:

| n (tok) | 24 | 50 | 100 | 150 | 200 | 300 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DIRECTED reveal_before Q1 | −1.6 | +9.7 | +21.5 | +24.5 | +25.7 | +27.1 |
| lone B₁(n) [told] | +13.3 | +23.6 | +33.5 | +35.3 | +35.5 | +33.0 |
| NOT-TOLD committed Q1 | +2.1 | +6.5 | +18.7 | +26.8 | +26.3 | +23.1 |
| lone B₁(n) [not-told] | +9.5 | +26.9 | +34.6 | +38.1 | +38.5 | +38.0 |

Both curves rise then plateau (Spearman +1.00 directed / +0.77 not-told — but Spearman over 6 points is
weak alone; the substance is the monotone-rise-then-plateau tracking the lone B₁(n)). The directed curve
tracks the lone closely; the not-told curve DIVERGES slightly at 300 tok (+26.8→+23.1 while the lone
not-told stays flat-high ~+38) — a small competing dilution term, reported not buried. A *pure-step*
attention/recency-reallocation account predicts no graded n-dependence — so the graded rise is NECESSARY
but NOT SUFFICIENT evidence (a *graded* attention/length account can also scale with n; **(B)
selectivity is the stronger lever**).

**(B) compute-selectivity (a difficulty interaction).** ![selectivity](../plots/seg11_selectivity.png)
At a fixed directed cell (reveal_before k=8, 200 tok, all positions), splitting the revealed item by
max-2×2-product quartile, the directed boost RISES with difficulty: **+6.7 → +10.8 → +14.7 → +16.2**
(logit +0.27 → +0.44 → +0.60 → +0.66) at a **~FLAT n0 baseline (52→48%)** — so it is NOT a headroom
effect; filler selectively helps the more compute-bound items. This is the k-structure analogue of
Segment 0's "filler helps deep arithmetic, not aggregation" selectivity. **Cross-check ($0, cached
directed cells):** the difficulty-selectivity REPLICATES cross-FAMILY (d6 directed boost +11.0→+19.6
with total #carries) but is **FLAT cross-MODEL** (DeepSeek ~+7 at every carry level) — so the
difficulty-selectivity is Opus-strong (sumprod max-product AND d6 carries) but does NOT clearly transfer
to the weaker open model.

**(B2) directedness is target-following AND primacy-modulated (a review-driven check).** A confound:
the not-told default is positional (Q1), so "banks Q1" alone could be primacy. The **(before−after)
contrast is significantly POSITIVE at ALL 8 positions** (+7 to +27pp, every CI excludes 0) — so
directing to a NON-Q1 target does produce a directed gain everywhere; directedness is NOT merely
"position 1 is read best". **But it is strongly PRIMACY-MODULATED:** the directed *absolute* boost is
large early (Q1 +25.7, Q2 +18.7, Q3 +20.6) and small late (Q6 +1.7, Q7 +2.4) — the model directs
compute WELL to early-listed targets and barely to Q6/Q7; the large late-position contrast there is
carried mostly by the strongly NEGATIVE reveal-after arm (recency-erasure), not a positive directed
boost. So the honest claim is **target-following AND strongly primacy-modulated** (which reinforces,
rather than separates from, the positional theme of Tier 2.3). The contrast's clean decomposition
assumes the revealed-position recency-erasure term is the same under reveal_before/after (plausible,
not airtight).

**(C) banks-vs-retrieves.** The not-told per-position profile banks **PREDOMINANTLY Q1 (+26.3)** with a
small residual early boost (Q3 +5.6, Q5 +4.5 sig.+ but ≪ the +13.7 divided prediction); the model banks
its single guessed target and retrieves it IF asked. The valid evidence against "diffuse all-questions
banking" is that the **early-mid Q2–Q5 boosts are ~+3 (≪ divided)** — NOT the late-position negatives
(recency-erasure would mask any banking there). This does NOT by itself prove the banked quantity is
arithmetic *computation* vs a maintained partial answer — that is what (A)+(B) address.

_Operating points (note): Opus uses n=200 tok for both k=4,8; DeepSeek uses k=4 @ 200 tok and k=8 @ 400
tok (holding n/k≈50 const, the Segment-9 convention) — each cell's arms share the same n, so the framing
comparison is internally clean, but cross-k/cross-model magnitudes are at slightly different n._

**Calibrated claim (sharpened after the LLM review).** **SUPPORTED:** the filler benefit is a genuine,
**dose-responsive**, **difficulty-selective**, **filler-amount-dependent** boost committed to ONE
question — the NAMED target (reveal_before, target-following per B2) or the positional default Q1
(undirected); the model does NOT spread compute across the k questions (no parallel). **UNPROVEN / honest
bounds:** (i) a **pure-step** attention-reallocation account is disfavoured (it predicts no graded
n-dependence), but a **graded** attention/bandwidth account is NOT formally excluded — a graded
"longer-context read" or a decode-time KV-buffer can also produce a monotone dose AND a difficulty
gradient at flat baseline; the difficulty-selectivity is the stronger lever but is not decisive. (ii)
**Localization is NOT established:** the data show filler ENABLES the benefit and that it scales with
filler AMOUNT, but do NOT prove the computation happens AT the filler positions vs at the decode step
reading them as scratch/KV-buffer — hence "filler-amount-dependent", NOT "filler-time". (iii) **Content
vs length:** counting is structured/numeric; the dots probe (a different filler that also banks Q1 under
not-told) shows it is not counting-specific, but both are "length" — a length-matched RANDOM filler +
activation/logit-lens probing of the last filler position are the decisive future tests (flagged for the
synthesis). (iv) **Evidence base:** the dose-response + the sumprod selectivity are Opus-sumprod-only
(selectivity also confirmed on d6 by #carries, but FLAT on DeepSeek) — the genuine-computation reading
rests mainly on Opus. We do **NOT** claim parallel latent thinking (disfavoured) and do **NOT** assert a
proven mechanism.

## TIER 2.3 — the DEFAULT-TARGET ("why Q1?") is POSITIONAL (primacy / first-listed)
`results/seg11_default.md`; figure panel ![default](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png) (a). Fixed-item
re-ordering (the SAME revealed item placed at positions {Q1,Q2,Q4,Q8}, content held FIXED, not-told
k=8): B_k = **+25.1 / −2.1 / +3.0 / −18.0** → the boost follows the listed POSITION, not the content.
Content cross-check (iid sweep, split by item difficulty): Q1 is boosted for BOTH easy (+27.7) and hard
(+24.9) items, and later positions are banked for neither → **positional/primacy default, NOT
content-based**. This directly explains the Q1-concentration in the headline.

## TIER 2.4 — the CONTEXT-INTERFERENCE CAP is mostly STRUCTURE (the first distractor)
`results/seg11_cap.md`; figure panel ![cap](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png) (b). Directed (reveal_before)
boost at a FIXED item/position-0 vs the number of distractor questions (k−1 ∈ {0,1,3,7}), 200 tok:

| k (#distractors) | 1 (0) | 2 (1) | 4 (3) | 8 (7) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| directed B_k | **+43.0** | +23.6 | +30.1 | +25.3 |
| logit | +1.84 | +0.97 | +1.25 | +1.03 |

The bulk of the cap appears IMMEDIATELY at the first distractor (k=1→k=2: +43→+24, logit +1.84→+0.97)
and is then ~flat/noisy across k=2,4,8 (a +23–30pp / +0.97–+1.25 logit band), NOT a smooth decline with
load. So the cap is **mostly imposed by the multi-question STRUCTURE (the first distractor), ~saturating
after one distractor**, with only a modest additional load component. **Lead with the LOGIT (data-
inspector):** part of the ADDITIVE drop is baseline-elevation — the n0 of the SAME fixed item at pos0
RISES with #distractors (29%→43/38/38%) because the distractors act as preceding-context filler (the
Segment-5 questions-as-filler effect), pre-consuming headroom; the logit cap (baseline-robust) shows the
SAME big-drop-at-k2-then-flat shape, so the structure-dominated conclusion holds. This BOUNDS in-prompt
latent compute: the +35–43pp lone/parallel boost is unreachable in-prompt because ANY distractor context
roughly halves the directed boost.

## TIER 3 — light probes
**3.5 dots×k (`results/seg11_dots.md`).** The dots disruption is **FRAMING-INDEPENDENT**: a ~6–7pp
uniform penalty vs counting (told +6.4, not-told +8.1) that roughly DOUBLES from k=1 (~+3.5pp lone gap)
to k=8. Under **not-told dots ALSO concentrates a large committed Q1 boost (+18.2 vs counting +26.3)** —
so dots is NOT a categorical "undelimited-run = disengagement/no-op" signal; it just is a uniformly
weaker k-structure compute substrate (plausibly a long undelimited dot run carries less positional/
structural signal than a counting sequence). The Segment-10 "dots is disruptive in the k-structure"
observation is this constant penalty viewed through the TOLD (near-zero) cell, where a negative offset
reads as "disruptive." Bounded; changes no verdict (only deepens B_k ≪ divided under told).

**3.6 d6 carry anomaly (open puzzle).** Under not-told d6's later positions go strongly negative (k=8
Q8 −60pp) — a carry-disruption × recency-erasure mixture far larger than sumprod's. The structured d6
per-position swing (tied to the open Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit instant-saturation puzzle) remains an OPEN
PUZZLE documented for the synthesis. It does not bear on the Q1-banking headline (we lead d6 with Q1 +
logit, where the direction is clean).

## Consolidated verdict (`results/seg11_verdict.md`)
The evolved framing headline (not-told → Q1-default banking; the random-target disclosure suppresses it)
**generalizes IN DIRECTION** cross-family (Opus d6) AND cross-model (DeepSeek) (magnitude attenuates even
on logit; DeepSeek partial). The filler benefit is **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective,
filler-amount-dependent computation** committed to a SINGLE question (the named target — target-following
but primacy-modulated; or — absent disclosure — the **positional** default Q1), **NOT parallel** and **not
a pure-step attention artifact** (a graded attention account is not formally excluded; localization not
established; the genuine-computation evidence is Opus-leaning); the in-prompt directed boost is **capped
at ~half the lone** by the multi-question structure (≈saturating after one distractor; partly headroom
pre-consumption). Genuine no-CoT holds throughout. All EXPLORATORY; the locked
plan/harness/scorers/pre-registration are untouched.

## Judgment-heavy choices (with rationale)
- **Lead d6 + DeepSeek with Q1-only + logit (early-pool co-reported).** d6 is carry-fragile and DeepSeek
  is fast-saturating (Q2–Q4 recency-elevated), so the early pool mixes recency-confounded positions; Q1
  is the recency-free primacy read (the Segment-9/10 convention). The Q1 DIRECTION is unambiguous.
- **Disclose arm via post-processing** (not a harness edit) — keeps the locked harness untouched; the
  told/not-told cells are byte-identical to Segments 7/9/10 (cache-hit).
- **Fixed-item, position-0 for the cap** — the only position valid for k=1; holding item+position fixed
  isolates #distractors. **Fixed-item re-ordering for the default-target** — holds content fixed, varies
  position, the clean positional-vs-content test (corroborated by the iid difficulty split).
- **max-product as the difficulty proxy** — the 2×2 multiply is the compute bottleneck; it gives a boost
  gradient at a ~flat baseline (cleaner than a headroom-confounded proxy).
- **DeepSeek not-told n=0 truncation treated as benign** (answer-first-then-continue, verified) — not a
  no-CoT failure; boost robust to dropping truncated rows.
- **dots probe kept light** (one not-told k=8 cell) — enough to show the disruption is framing-
  independent, per the Tier-3 "light/optional" scope.

## Honest caveats / limitations
- **Genuine computation is STRENGTHENED, not PROVEN.** The dose-response + selectivity disfavour a
  pure-step attention account but do not formally exclude a graded attention mechanism, and do not
  separate "banked arithmetic computation" from "a maintained partial answer." We state the strongest
  SUPPORTABLE claim and stop there.
- **Cross-family/cross-model magnitudes are smaller** (d6 +11, DeepSeek +6 vs sumprod +26) — expected
  from Q1 headroom + the model's boost size; the DIRECTION + disclosure-suppression replicate, the exact
  magnitude does not transfer (and need not).
- **d6's regime is carry-fragile** (huge late-position negatives) — only its Q1/logit + contrast are
  clean; its early-pool/aggregate regime is a carry × recency mixture (an open puzzle).
- **One clean off-ceiling family per model** (the absolute-regime single-clean-family limitation carried
  from earlier segments) — the directedness/banking findings are cross-family + cross-model, but the
  absolute B_k magnitudes rest on sumprod (Opus) / add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek).
- **The context-cap is read at position 0** (primacy-disadvantaged); a middle position can't be held
  constant across k=1, so position-0 is the principled fixed point. The +23–30 band across k=2,4,8 is
  mildly non-monotone (k=4 > k=2) — reported honestly as "structure-dominated, ~saturated," not a smooth
  load curve. Part of the additive drop is baseline-elevation (distractor-as-filler raises n0) — we lead
  the cap with the baseline-robust logit.
- **Framing shifts the n0 baseline slightly even with IDENTICAL draws** (d6 told 55% vs not-told 48%;
  DeepSeek 53 vs 58, opposite sign) — a small genuine framing effect on baseline; the logit agrees and
  sumprod's baselines are EQUAL across framing yet show the largest effect, so the Q1-banking is NOT a
  headroom artifact (verified by the data-inspector). The cross-MODEL leg is the weakest (DeepSeek
  not-told vs told CIs just touch; the clean separation is not-told ≫ disclose), and the cross-FAMILY d6
  leg rests on **Q1 + logit alone** (its early-pool/aggregate is a carry×recency mixture).

### Key findings
- **TIER 1.1 — REPLICATES cross-family + cross-model.** Undirected Q1 boost k=8 told/disclose/not-told:
  sumprod +1.2/+2.0/**+26.3**; d6 −2.2/−3.4/**+10.6**; DeepSeek +3.4/+2.2/**+6.2** → not-told ≫ disclose
  ≈ told everywhere (logit agrees). Magnitude tracks Q1 headroom (sumprod n0 20% → +26; d6/DeepSeek n0
  ~50% → +6–11). The reframe is NOT sumprod-specific. ![framing](../plots/seg11_framing.png)
- **TIER 1.2 — genuine computation STRENGTHENED (honestly bounded).** (A) the DIRECTED reveal_before Q1
  boost rises MONOTONICALLY with filler (−1.6→+27.1 over 24→300 tok; Spearman +1.00) and TRACKS the lone
  B₁(n) shape; the not-told committed Q1 boost likewise scales — a pure-step attention account predicts
  no graded n-dependence. (B) the directed boost RISES with item difficulty (max-product quartile
  +6.7→+16.2; logit +0.27→+0.66) at a ~FLAT baseline (52→48%) = NOT headroom. (C) the not-told profile
  banks ONLY Q1 (+26.3) and retrieves it if asked. SUPPORTED: genuine, dose-responsive,
  difficulty-selective filler-time computation to ONE question; UNPROVEN: the precise mechanism / full
  exclusion of a *graded* attention account. No "parallel" claim. ![dose](../plots/seg11_dose.png)
- **TIER 2.3 — the default target is POSITIONAL.** Fixed-item re-ordering (SAME item at {Q1,Q2,Q4,Q8},
  not-told k=8): B_k +25.1/−2.1/+3.0/−18.0 → follows position, not content; Q1 boosted for easy AND hard
  items, later positions for neither. ![default+cap](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png)
- **TIER 2.4 — the context-interference cap is mostly STRUCTURE.** Directed boost vs #distractors (fixed
  item/pos0, 200 tok): lone +43.0 → k2 +23.6 → k4 +30.1 → k8 +25.3 — the cap appears at the FIRST
  distractor and ~saturates (a +23–30 band), not a smooth load decline.
- **TIER 3.5 — dots×k is framing-INDEPENDENT.** Dots penalty vs counting ~constant across framing
  (told +6.4, not-told +8.1; roughly doubles from k=1's ~+3.5pp); under not-told dots ALSO concentrates
  a large Q1 boost (+18.2) → not a categorical disengagement signal, just a uniformly weaker
  k-structure filler. **TIER 3.6 — d6 carry anomaly** (k=8 Q8 −60pp under not-told) documented as an
  open puzzle for the synthesis.
