(~1 question's worth). The framing-conditional refinements + the directedness contrast are
    supporting findings.

- **Segment 11 — Mechanism & confound investigation (reframed around the evolved headline).**
  **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0).** Exemplary, honest, fully-reviewed mechanism work (locked
  plan/harness/scorers/pre-registration UNTOUCHED; genuine no-CoT re-verified on **352k** new k-prompt
  outputs — reason% 0 everywhere incl. the new family/model, genuine-viol 0.019%, scoring 0-inconsistent).
  - **TIER 1.1 — the Segment-10 framing reframe REPLICATES (in DIRECTION) cross-family (Opus `add_n15_d6`)
    AND cross-model (DeepSeek `add_n4_d10`):** on both, the model commits a positive undirected Q1 boost
    ONLY under not-told (k=8 not-told Q1: sumprod +26.3 / d6 +10.6 / DeepSeek +6.2), and the random-target
    DISCLOSURE (disclose ≈ told ≪ not-told) suppresses it. **Magnitude ATTENUATES even on logit**
    (Δlogit sumprod +1.16 ≫ d6 +0.52 ≫ DeepSeek +0.12 — ~10× weaker cross-model, a weak/partial leg; d6
    rests on Q1+logit alone, carry-fragile). The reframe is **NOT sumprod-specific**.
  - **TIER 1.2 — genuine-hidden-computation STRENGTHENED but honestly BOUNDED:** the directed
    dose-response RISES with filler & tracks the lone `B₁(n)` (necessary, NOT sufficient — disfavours a
    *pure-step* attention account, not a *graded* one); compute-selectivity rises with item difficulty at
    a ~flat baseline (the stronger lever, cleanest on sumprod, Opus-leaning — d6 baseline-confounded,
    DeepSeek flat); banks-vs-retrieves refutes diffuse all-questions banking. **Calibrated claim:**
    genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, **filler-amount-dependent** (NOT "filler-time" —
    localization not established) computation committed to ONE question; **NOT parallel.** Graded attention
    not formally excluded; evidence base Opus-leaning. No overclaiming.
  - **TIER 2:** the **default target is POSITIONAL** (primacy/first-listed, not content-based — fixed-item
    reorder); the **context-interference cap is mostly STRUCTURE** (imposed at the first distractor,
    ~saturating; partly questions-as-filler headroom pre-consumption; led with logit).
  - **TIER 3 (light):** dots×k is a framing-INDEPENDENT uniform penalty (a weaker k-structure substrate,
    not a disengagement signal); the **d6 per-position carry anomaly** (k=8 Q8 −60pp under not-told, tied
    to the Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit saturation puzzle) documented as an OPEN PUZZLE for the synthesis.
  - **Process:** 5 review rounds (LLM sanity + `empirical-ml-data-inspector` + 2× `research-reviewer` +
    a completion review) with **all fixes applied AND propagated to the verdict file** (`seg11_verdict.md`,
    which Segment 12 lifts from); several overclaims caught & fixed (filler-time→filler-amount-dependent;
    pure-step vs graded attention; directedness≠primacy → a target-following-but-primacy-modulated B2
    check). Verdict unchanged through review. Marginal ≈ $409 (cumulative ≈ $2167).

  *(Original asked scope, for the record.)* Understand *why* and **harden the interpretation** for the
  synthesis. **TIER 1 (essential):** (a)
  **confirm the Segment-10 V1 framing / Q1-default-banking / disclosure-suppression finding GENERALIZES**
  — it rests on one family (Opus sumprod); run told-vs-not-told(-vs-disclose) on **Opus `add_n15_d6`**
  (cross-family) and **DeepSeek `add_n4_d10`** (cross-model), genuine-no-CoT re-verified; (b) **strengthen
  or honestly bound the GENUINE-HIDDEN-COMPUTATION interpretation vs attention/retrieval reallocation**
  (the "suggestive, not proven" crux flagged since Segment 8) via a **directed dose-response** (boost
  scales with filler amount → genuine filler-time compute), **compute-selectivity** (harder items boosted
  more), and the **banks-vs-retrieves** framing from the per-position not-told data — with a calibrated
  "supported vs unproven" statement (no overclaiming "parallel latent thinking"). **TIER 2:** the
  **default-target ("why Q1") reorder** experiment (positional/primacy vs content-based) and the
  **context-interference-cap** characterization (directed in-prompt ~+15–20 ≪ lone +35 — vs the number of
  distractor questions). **TIER 3 (light/optional):** the **dots×k** interaction and the **d6 per-position
  carry anomaly / Segment-2 saturation puzzle** — probe if cheap, else document as open puzzles. Mirror
  the Segment-8 methodology (position-resolved, additive+logit, comparison-SE/SE_R, the positive control);
  **exploratory** mechanism work — do NOT change the locked grid/harness/scorers/pre-registration, re-declare
  the verdict, or fit α.

- **Segment 12 — Final synthesis, write-up, reproducibility & review.** **(NEXT — the LAST segment.)**
  **Synthesis + reproducibility + review ONLY — no new experiments;** the science is complete (locked
  grid/harness/scorers/pre-registration untouched; no re-declaring the §5 verdict; no α-fit). Produce a
  **clean, self-contained final synthesis** in `write_up.md` that a fresh reader can read top-down: an
  executive synthesis leading with the **evolved headline** — *(i)* the framing-robust core (**no
  parallel latent thinking; ~one question's worth of compute committed to ONE question; undirected
  `B_k ≪ divided` at every k**), *(ii)* the pre-registered §5 told-framing result (near-null undirected /
  directedness-positive / "partial directed" / retention 23%→7% / α ill-defined), *(iii)* the
  framing-conditional WHERE-it-lands (directed→named; undirected+disclosure→suppressed; undirected+
  not-told→default Q1; driver = the random-target **disclosure**), *(iv)* the mechanism (genuine,
  dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, **filler-amount-dependent** computation; positional default;
  structure-imposed cap) — with an explicit, honest **§5-verdict-vs-Segment-10-reframe reconciliation**
  (pre-registration honored; reframe exploratory; a sharpening, NOT a reversal), the **answer to the
  original research question**, the **side-findings** kept prominent, and the **honest interpretation
  caution** (parallel **disfavored**; genuine computation strengthened-not-proven; no overclaiming).
  **Remove/rewrite the stale Segment-0-era trailing "Headline results / Limitations / Open questions"
  sections** (they describe only the Segment-0 de-risk); update the Status block + ⚑ verdict box to
  COMPLETE. Polish ~4–7 **headline figures** (inspected + GPT-vision-checked, captions de-overclaimed,
  realized-token axes). **Reproducibility audit:** `--assert-cached` reproduces the key results at ~$0 +
  a how-to-reproduce guide + a numbers-match consistency check vs the verdict files. **Full review
  battery:** `empirical-ml-data-inspector` + `research-reviewer` (overall & measuring-what-you-want) + an
  `llm_helper.query_all_models` headline sanity check — **fix what they surface.** State the
  **limitations + future work** (the decisive genuine-computation tests — a length-matched random-filler
  control + activation/logit-lens probing — are out of scope; the single-clean-family absolute regime +
  cross-model/cross-level confound; no clean reasoning-positive; the d6/dots×k open puzzles).

## 6. How this plan may adapt

This decomposition is a starting point; phase planners should adjust based on what actually happens.
**Update after Segment 5:** Segments 0–5 are DONE. The new Segment 5 (inserted after Segment 4)
**resolved the in-structure picture and locked the confound-aware design.** Its results sharpen the
framing for the rest of the project: (1) the Segment-4 "in-structure curves are narrower" worry was a
**full-reveal artifact** — with the adopted minimal reveal the in-structure boost ≈ single-turn (a small
offset that cancels in the gate Δ / contrast); (2) the **pre-saturation confound** is real and decisive
for level choice — fast-saturating addition (d8/d10/d12) **ceilings** at high k, so the gradual
**`sumprod_t3_d2` is the ONLY off-ceiling headline level** (with `add_n15_d6` as a carry-fragile
secondary); (3) the **in-structure 3-way gate is GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2**; (4) the **likely
headline is the `B_k≈0` undirected null**, carried by a large, cross-level, cross-model
**reveal-before/after directedness contrast** (the primary interpretive tool). **⚑ The headline
statistic has evolved:** because `B_k≈0` makes the pre-specified **α-fit ill-defined**, the **PRIMARY**
analysis is now the **3-way (parallel/divided/null) resolution + the directedness contrast, read
position-resolved** (the position-balanced aggregate is a recency-erasure mixture); the **α-fit/collapse
plots are SECONDARY** (only where B_k is meaningfully positive). Segment 6 must **pre-register this
explicitly as a deviation** and **size on position-resolved variance**. This is a clean,
plan-anticipated, *informative* outcome — a genuine, powered null (vindicated by the off-ceiling level
+ a significantly-positive reveal-before control + position-resolution) is a real result, **not** a
failure.

**Update after Segment 8 (DONE — the headline is now confirmed and clean):** the pre-registered §5 verdict
is delivered: **at the most-shared k=8 the undirected per-question filler boost is NEAR-NULL (~7% of B₁,
≪ divided at ~10σ; NULL on the cleaner Q1-only/held-out estimands), while the directedness contrast is
robustly POSITIVE — combined "partial directed".** Parallel is *disfavored* on the sharing axis; the
directedness reading is *suggestive* (not a proven mechanism). This is the clean, plan-anticipated,
*informative* end-state. **Streamlining decision now actioned:** (a) **Segment 9 (cross-model) is kept but
SCOPED DOWN** to a focused DeepSeek generalization of the directedness-positive + undirected-near-null
picture (NOT a full Opus-grade gate — its modest boost can't power that; graceful degradation expected and
acceptable). (b) **Segments 10 (robustness) and 11 (mechanism) remain separate for now, but the
phase planner after Segment 9 should actively reconsider MERGING them** into one focused
"robustness + mechanism + measuring-what-you-want" segment if Segment 9 leaves few open threads (the
robust headline does not depend on resolving the +2.5 mechanism or the cross-model arm). The genuinely-
novel **side-findings to keep prominent in the final write-up regardless**: the
**questions-act-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure** phenomenology, the **context-interference
cap** (~+15–20pp ≪ the lone +35.5), the **retention gradient** (23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8), and the **d6
per-position carry anomaly**. The told-vs-not-told ablation (Segment 10) is interpretively central — it
tests whether the null is framing-dependent (the null is currently "operational under the told framing").

**Update after Segments 6–7 (both DONE):** the experiment is **locked + pre-registered**
(`results/preregistration.md`) and the **full run is executed** (Opus 4.5, 274k calls, all binding
validity guards PASS on the full data). The pilot-anticipated headline is **CONFIRMED descriptively**
(sumprod k=8 reveal-after early `B_k` = +2.5pp [+1.2,+3.7]; directedness contrast +16.2pp; d6 contrast
+31.9pp) — the **NEAR-NULL / WEAK-POSITIVE PARTIAL + DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** picture ("directed ≫
undirected; undirected ≪ divided"). Segment 8 (NEXT) applies the §5 decision rules to deliver the formal
verdict. **Strategic note for the run loop — a post-Segment-8 decision (do NOT act on it yet):** because
the headline is now essentially known and clean, once Segment 8 confirms it the planner should consider
whether the remaining segments can be **streamlined or merged** — e.g. the **cross-model arm (9)** may
degrade gracefully to a qualitative "directedness contrast reproduces on DeepSeek" point (the modest
~+5pp DeepSeek boost likely can't power its own gate), and **robustness (10)** + **mechanism (11)** could
be combined into one focused "robustness + mechanism" segment if Segment 8 leaves few open threads. Keep
the genuinely-novel **side-findings** prominent in the eventual write-up regardless: the
**questions-act-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure** phenomenology, the **context-interference
cap** (~+12–18pp ≪ the lone +37), and the **d6 per-position carry anomaly**. For now the plan stands as
numbered.

**Update after Segment 9 (DONE — cross-model arm complete):** the headline **PARTIALLY REPLICATES** on
DeepSeek-V3-0324 — the *directedness-positive + undirected-declines-with-k + ≪divided-at-k8 structure*
generalizes (with the questions-as-filler/recency-erasure phenomenology and the retention gradient), but
**(a) the absolute near-null magnitude does NOT** (DeepSeek banks more undirected compute, ~39% retention
at k=8 vs Opus ~7%; contrast ~8–9× smaller) and **(b) cross-MODEL is confounded with cross-LEVEL** (Opus
sumprod vs DeepSeek add_n4_d10; a gradual boosting DeepSeek level was not calibrated — largely intrinsic,
since DeepSeek's boost is level-specific). This is an honest, scoped, *informative* cross-model result;
the headline stands on Opus regardless. **On the 10/11 merge I flagged earlier:** I **kept Segments 10
(robustness) and 11 (mechanism) separate** — they are genuinely distinct, and Segment 11's
**attention-redistribution disambiguation** (a live confound on the regime axis, flagged in Segment 8 as
needing NEW experiments) is important enough that bundling 6+ sub-investigations into one phase risks
shallow treatment. **The post-Segment-10 planner should reconsider** whether the *lighter* mechanism
items (the d6 carry anomaly; characterizing the context-interference cap) can fold into the final
synthesis (12), keeping a focused Segment 11 mainly for the attention-redistribution disambiguation.
Segment 10's **told-vs-not-told ablation is interpretively central** (it tests whether the null is
framing-dependent). Side-findings to keep prominent in the final write-up (now cross-model-confirmed):
the **questions-as-filler / pre-saturation / recency-erasure** phenomenology, the **context-interference
cap**, and the **retention-declines-with-sharing** gradient.

**Update after Segment 10 (DONE — the headline EVOLVED; mechanism is next):** the told-vs-not-told
ablation (V1) was the major result and **reframed the headline.** The Segment-8 "undirected near-null" is
**TOLD-framing-specific**: under **not-told** the model banks ~one question's worth of filler compute,
**concentrated on its DEFAULT first-listed Q1** (+26.3 at k=8, dose-responsive, exceeds divided), and the
**random-target DISCLOSURE** (not the encouragement — pinned by a `disclose` arm) is what suppresses that
under told. The directedness contrast **survives** (weakened; confound-minimal `reveal_before−disclose`
~+24). alt-k (V2) and dots (V3) **HOLD** (`B_k ≪ divided` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}; counting≈dots). **⚑
The framing-ROBUST core headline is now "NO PARALLEL latent thinking":** the model commits a roughly
single-question compute budget to ONE question (directed target, or default Q1 absent disclosure), so the
per-question undirected boost is `≪ divided` and never spread across k. **Decision — KEPT Segment 11
(mechanism) separate and REFRAMED it** rather than merging into 12: the V1 finding *expanded* the
mechanism landscape (it must be confirmed cross-family/cross-model before the synthesis leans on it, and
the genuine-computation-vs-attention crux still needs new experiments), so a focused mechanism segment is
warranted. Segment 11 priorities: **Tier 1** = confirm the V1 framing/Q1-default finding generalizes
(Opus d6 + DeepSeek) + strengthen/bound the genuine-hidden-computation interpretation (directed
dose-response, compute-selectivity, banks-vs-retrieves); **Tier 2** = the default-target ("why Q1")
reorder + the context-interference-cap-vs-distractor-count characterization; **Tier 3 (light)** = the
dots×k interaction + the d6 carry anomaly (probe or defer to 12). The attention-redistribution concern is
now *partly addressed* by V1's dose-response (genuine, n-scaling compute), so Tier 1(b) solidifies it
rather than starting from scratch. The **side-findings to keep prominent in the synthesis** are unchanged
(questions-as-filler/recency-erasure; the context-interference cap; the retention gradient; dots×k; the
d6 anomaly), plus the **framing-conditional Q1-default-banking** finding itself.

**Update after Segment 11 (DONE — mechanism complete; the synthesis is the LAST segment):** the
mechanism work landed cleanly. **The Segment-10 framing reframe REPLICATES in DIRECTION** cross-family
(Opus d6) AND cross-model (DeepSeek) — not-told concentrates a positive Q1 boost that the random-target
disclosure suppresses — though the **magnitude attenuates even on logit** (DeepSeek ~10× weaker, a
partial leg; d6 rests on Q1+logit). **The genuine-hidden-computation reading is STRENGTHENED but
honestly BOUNDED:** the directed dose-response (necessary-not-sufficient — disfavours a *pure-step*
attention account) + compute-selectivity (the stronger lever, cleanest on sumprod, **Opus-leaning**) +
banks-vs-retrieves support a **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, *filler-amount-dependent***
(NOT "filler-time" — localization not shown) computation committed to ONE question; a *graded* attention
account is **not formally excluded**, so the synthesis must say "strengthened, not proven" and must NOT
claim parallel latent thinking (disfavoured). The **default target is positional** (primacy, not
content); the **context-interference cap is structure-imposed** (the first distractor). dots×k is a
framing-independent uniform penalty; the d6 carry anomaly is an open puzzle. Five review rounds with all
fixes applied + propagated to `seg11_verdict.md`. **No merge needed now** — Segment 12 is the natural
last segment. **The central Segment-12 challenge** is writing the EVOLVED headline as a **clean
convergent result** (not a sequence of reversals): lead with the framing-robust "no parallel latent
thinking / single-question budget to ONE position" core, then the framing-conditional WHERE-it-lands and
the mechanism, and **reconcile the pre-registered §5 verdict (correct within its told scope) with the
Segment-10 reframe (exploratory scope-widening) transparently.** Note the unified `write_up.md` currently
has **stale Segment-0-era trailing "Headline results / Limitations / Open questions" sections** that the
synthesis must remove/rewrite. The genuine-computation **decisive future tests** (a length-matched
random-filler control + activation/logit-lens probing) are out of scope — flag as future work.

Current numbering: 6 power/lock-in/pre-reg (DONE), 7 main-exp full runs (DONE), 8 main analysis (DONE),
9 open-model cross-model generalization (DONE — partially replicates),
10 robustness (DONE — headline framing-dependent; anti-parallel core robust),
11 mechanism (DONE — reframe generalizes in direction; genuine computation strengthened-not-proven),
**12 final synthesis + reproducibility + review (NEXT — the LAST segment)**. Other risks unchanged: the open-model cross-model arm (Segment 9) may be under-powered (modest/narrow DeepSeek
boost → fall back to a 2nd strong closed model or report DeepSeek as a "boost reproduces" point;
headline is Opus regardless); analyze on **both additive and logit** scales (the baseline shifts a lot
with k). The graceful-degradation deliverable (clean replication + `B₁(n)` curve + the directedness/null
finding + "what task would resolve it") remains an acceptable end state if the parallel/divided question
ultimately can't be cleanly powered on the real k-prompts.

# Segment 12 — final synthesis, reproducibility & review (phase write-up)

> Phase-specific detail for the project's **final** segment. The actual synthesis lives in the unified
> `writeups/write_up.md` **"Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section (that is the canonical final
> document). Chronology: `progress_log_segment_12_phase_0.md`. **Synthesis + reproducibility + review
> ONLY — no new experiments; the locked grid/harness/scorers/pre-registration and the §5 verdict are
> untouched; α is not fit.**

## Goal
Consolidate the whole Segment 0–11 arc into a clean, coherent, honest final write-up that a fresh reader
can read top-down; reconcile the pre-registered §5 verdict with the Segment-10 reframe transparently;
polish + verify the headline figures; verify `--assert-cached` reproducibility; and run the full review
battery, **fixing what it surfaces.**

## What was delivered

### 1. The clean final synthesis (the central deliverable, in `write_up.md`)
- Added a self-contained **"## ⭑ Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section near the top (after the research
  question, before the Status block): an **abstract**, the **final headline in the right shape** (the
  framing-robust anti-parallel core; the pre-registered told-framing near-null/partial-directed result;
  the framing-conditional WHERE-it-lands; the mechanism), an explicit **answer to the original research
  question** (parallel **disfavored** / divided **excluded** / realized **near-null**; **α not estimated**;
  the **23%→7% retention gradient**; the directedness contrast as the falsifiable result), the **key
  supporting findings + their figures**, a transparent **§5-verdict-vs-Segment-10-reframe reconciliation**
  (a *sharpening*, not a reversal — pre-registration honored, reframe exploratory), the **side-findings**,
  the **honest interpretation caveats**, the **limitations + future work**, and a **reproducibility
  pointer**.
- **Updated the Status block** to PROJECT COMPLETE (Segments 0–12) + added a Segment-12 entry.
- **Rewrote the ⚑ verdict box** (was the chronologically-accreted Segment-2 "Power-gate verdict") into a
  clean **"## ⚑ Final verdict"** that states the project's answer and points to the synthesis + the
  Segment-8 verdict; the design-phase gate prose (fully duplicated in the Segment 2/5 sections) was
  trimmed to a short historical pointer.
- **Removed/rewrote the stale Segment-0-era trailing sections.** The "## Headline results / Evidence /
  Why so large / Key choices / Format artifact / Models-params / Limitations / Open questions" headers
  (which described only the Segment-0 de-risk yet read as the project's conclusion) are now folded under a
  single **"## Segment 0: harness + replication de-risk (the detailed record)"** appendix with clearly
  Segment-0-scoped `###` sub-headers; the stale top-level "## Limitations" and "## Open questions / next
  steps" are replaced by a short **"Segment-0-era limitations & next-steps (SUPERSEDED)"** note pointing to
  the synthesis. The detailed Segment 1–11 sections remain below as the record.
- Added a **"## How to reproduce (the full guide)"** section (model/provider config; canonical datasets +
  loader; the locked pre-registration; the harness; the per-segment runner→analysis-lib→verdict→figure
  map; the `--assert-cached` audit; the cost ledger).
- Fixed the stale `continuation_context.md` header ("unified write-ups, not phase-specific") to describe
  the **evolved** convention (unified-only for Segments 0–8; unified + phase-specific from Segment 9) and
  marked the project COMPLETE.

### 2. Headline figures (polished, inspected, GPT-vision-checked, de-overclaimed)
Identified the ~11 figures that carry the final story and **opened/inspected every one**: the regime
verdict (`seg8_regime_verdict.png`, `seg8_b1_references.png`), the directedness contrast
(`seg8_contrast.png`), the per-position recency-erasure (`seg8_position_bk.png`), the collapse plots
(`seg8_collapse.png`), the retention gradient (`seg8_fraction_retained.png`), the framing reframe
(`seg10_v1_framing.png`, `seg11_framing.png`), the dose-response + selectivity (`seg11_dose.png`,
`seg11_selectivity.png`), and the default-target + cap (`seg11_default_cap.png`). The Segment-8/10/11
review work had already de-overclaimed the titles (e.g. "filler-amount-dependent" not "filler-time";
"consistent with — not proof of — compute-selective processing"; axes in realized tokens + pp), which I
re-confirmed. **GPT-vision-checked** the three most complex figures (`query_gpt(..., image_path=...)`;
`gemini-3.1-pro` is unavailable here). Two concrete fixes the vision review surfaced, applied + verified:
- `plots/seg11_default_cap.png` panel (b) — added a combined legend so the purple-left/blue-right
  two-axis mapping (logit B_k vs additive B_k) is explicit.
- `plots/seg10_v1_framing.png` panel (c) — the title was clipping off the right edge; rewrapped + sized
  it so it fits.
The other vision flags were interpretive-title comments ("erases", "drives", "SURVIVES") that the
experimental design supports and the prose hedges — left as-is (changing them would create
synthesis↔figure inconsistency).

### 3. Reproducibility audit (`--assert-cached`, all at $0)
- `ASSERT_CACHED=1 bash run_segment7.sh` re-streams the full **274k-call** Opus main grid (16 cells) from
  cache at **$0**, no cache miss. Same for `run_seg9.sh`, `run_segment10.sh`, `run_segment11.sh`, **and**
  the seg10/seg11 disclosure arms (`scripts_segment_10_phase_0/run_disclosure.py`,
  `scripts_segment_11_phase_0/run_disclosure_gen.py`).
- The analysis pipeline is **deterministic**: re-running `analyze_seg8_verdict.py` regenerates
  `results/seg8_verdict.md` + `seg8_regime_cells.csv` **byte-identically** (seed-0 cluster bootstrap).
- **Numbers-match consistency check (PASS):** every headline number in the synthesis matches its source
  file — sumprod k=8 early +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] / Q1 +1.2 / div +13.7 / par +35.5 / contrast +16.2 ←
  `seg8_regime_cells.csv`; told/disclose/not-told Q1 = +1.2/+2.0/+26.3, cross-family d6
  −2.2/−3.4/+10.6, cross-model DeepSeek +3.4/+2.2/+6.2 ← `seg11_framing.csv`; retention 23.5%@k=2 →
  7.0%@k=8 ← `seg10_cells.csv`; DeepSeek directedness Q1 k4 +2.6 ← `seg9_contrast.csv`.

### 4. Review battery (run + FIXED)
All three were run; full findings + fixes are in `progress_log_segment_12_phase_0.md`. Summary:
- **`empirical-ml-data-inspector`** over the final synthesis + headline result files → **every headline
  number matches its source file** (no transcription drift), genuine-no-CoT / scoring / answered-asked all
  sound, the recency-erasure aggregate is correctly NOT presented as the science, cross-model generalization
  not overstated. Surfaced 3 minor clarity notes (the multiple "lone boost" reference numbers; the
  two-point retention "gradient"; a terse cross-model number) — all addressed by added clarifying clauses.
- **`research-reviewer`** (overall + **"Not Measuring What You Want To Be Measuring"**) → confirmed the
  pre-saturation/recency-erasure/attention-reallocation confounds are handled and the synthesis states the
  strongest supportable claim. **Three substantive fixes applied:** (HIGH) scoped the "`≪ divided` /
  divided-excluded at every k" magnitude claim to the **told framing** and reframed the framing-independent
  core as "**no divided-pool spreading; banks ≤~1 question on ONE question**" (under not-told the model
  *concentrates* ~1 question on Q1, which can exceed divided — the opposite of even spreading); (HIGH)
  aligned the Segment-8 detailed-section attention-reallocation diagnostics with the more careful frozen
  `seg8_verdict.md` (the corr≈0 is **largely uninformative**, the k-gradient is **at most weak/ambiguous**,
  the artifact remains a **LIVE** possibility — not "mildly disfavoured"); (MEDIUM) softened the abstract's
  "strengthened well past suggestive" → "**meaningfully strengthened (still Opus-`sumprod`-leaning)**".
- **`llm_helper.query_all_models` (n=2, claude+gpt)** sanity check on the headline + caveats → confirmed the
  write-up does **not** overclaim parallel (correctly DISFAVORED) or a proven mechanism, and the
  §5-vs-reframe reconciliation reads as a **transparent sharpening, not a buried reversal**. Surfaced fixes
  (hedge "genuine" as "a real task-relevant effect, not an artifact"; scope "framing-robust" to the
  anti-parallel core; gloss "as if banking") — **all applied**.
- **Re-review:** the changes are local wording/scoping calibration to the synthesis (no science changed,
  no numbers changed); re-read the revised synthesis end-to-end as a skeptical reader (the headline #1 vs
  #3 tension the reviewer flagged is resolved; the abstract↔detailed-section attention-confound consistency
  is restored).

## Scope discipline (what was NOT done)
No new experiments / data collection; no change to the locked grid/harness/scorers/`preregistration.md`;
no re-declaring or altering the §5 verdict; no α-fit (ill-defined at the null — a documented result); no
re-opening the mechanism experiments or chasing the d6/dots×k puzzles. The only paid calls this segment
were a few review/vision LLM queries (logged in `total_cost.jsonl`).

## Pointers
- The final document: `writeups/write_up.md` (read the "Final synthesis (READ THIS)" section).
- The frozen-rules scientific verdict: `results/seg8_verdict.md`; robustness/mechanism:
  `results/seg{9,10,11}_verdict.md`.
- Reproduce: the "How to reproduce (the full guide)" section of `write_up.md`.

## Gotchas / learned context
- **Prefill on Opus 4.5 works**, but the prefilled assistant content **must not end with trailing
  whitespace** (API 400). Lead-in is `"The answer is"` (no trailing space).
- **Filler framing matters enormously.** Framing user-prompt filler as "scratch space to work"
  makes the model emit explicit CoT on word problems → breaks no-CoT + corrupts answer extraction.
  Use neutral "ignore this padding" framing (see `harness.py:_USER_FILLER_PREFIX`). Prefill placement
  avoids this entirely and is the chosen default.
- **Opus 4.5 memorizes standard math benchmarks** (GSM8K/MATH/AIME at ceiling, no-CoT). For a
  mid-range positive control use a non-memorizable computation-bound set (GSM-Hard). To measure
  genuine capability, prefer procedural generation.
- **GSM-Hard has ~12% degenerate problems** (negative/nonsensical answers). Paired boost is robust
  to them; filter `truth >= 0` for a cleaner baseline if needed.
- **Answer extraction = FIRST integer** (this is the correct no-CoT measure; do NOT switch to last
  integer — that would count reasoning-derived answers).
- Concurrency 100 worked with no rate-limit issues; runs are fast (15000 calls ≈ 3.5 min uncached,
  seconds when cached). Caching is keyed on full API kwargs + `sample_idx` + a code UUID in `llm.py`.
- `gemini-3.1-pro` is NOT available on this sandbox's OpenRouter (llm_helper `query_all_models`
  fails on the gemini leg); query with `models=["claude","gpt"]`. (So Gemini-vision plot checks
  aren't available; use `query_gpt(..., image_path=...)` for an LLM vision check if needed.)
- **Difficulty knob (Segment 1):** for addition, **#operands at fixed 8 digits is the clean
  monotonic knob**; 6-digit is **non-smooth** (confirmed localized `add_n14_d6`=34% dip vs ~49%
  neighbors — a model quirk; shipped n15_d6 is fine). Multiplication difficulty depends on **both**
  operands (≈ rows×width), not the smaller alone. **Single grid points are noisy at n=300** — trust
  the pooled n=1000 (calib+held-out) for locked levels.
- **Under filler, mul/expr emit ~7% spaced-digit / shows-working outputs** (the format artifact);
  addition stays clean. n=0 baselines are clean for all. `parse_answer_robust` re-scores spaced
  digits if needed downstream.

## Canonical datasets (Segment 1) — how to use them downstream
- Load the **exact** canonical items: `from canonical import load_canonical; items =
  load_canonical("add_n15_d8")` (or `tasks.load_task({"loader":"canonical","tag":"add_n15_d8"}, n=...)`).
  Each item: `{id, question, answer, family, params, task_type:"arithmetic", features}`.
- Locked tags (6 levels, 3 families; each pool = 5000 items): `add_n15_d8` (PRIMARY ~55%),
  `add_n16_d8` (~47%), `add_n12_d8` (~68%, k-headroom), `add_n15_d6` (~52%, +30pp max boost),
  `mul_a4_b4_hom` (2nd family ~47%), `sumprod_t3_d2` (3rd family, all-+ sum-of-products ~40%).
- **Selection-bias guard:** the calibrated baseline used items **[0,500)** and held-out **[3000,3500)**.
  `load_canonical(tag, n)` returns items `[0:n]` = the CALIBRATION sample; for an unbiased FRESH draw
  pass `offset>=500` (e.g. `load_canonical(tag, n=8, offset=500)`).
- All canonical data uses **seed 12345** (matches Segment-0 de-risk + calibration, so n=0 baselines
  cache-hit). Baselines are reported as the **pooled n=1000** (calib+held-out) estimate.
- Filtered levels (`mul_a4_b4_hom`; `sumprod` is unfiltered) use a named `item_filter` recorded in
  metadata; saved item ids are `<tag>_<i>` (not difficulty_tag), so join cached lock outputs by **question**.
- Metadata (line 1 of each `data/*.jsonl`): pinned model config, derived pooled baseline+CI, calib vs
  held-out, filler-gate boost, calibration/held-out item ranges, lock file, per-item feature schema.
- The d8 addition levels share a seed -> **nested prefixes** (smaller-n operands inside larger-n);
  fine for within-level matching, but the three levels are correlated across difficulties.
- **Boost-magnitude caveat:** mul/sumprod emit ~7% spaced-digit outputs UNDER filler -> use
  `harness.parse_answer_robust` (not the conservative first-int) when measuring their boost magnitude
  downstream, else the boost is under-measured. Addition is clean under filler.

## Segment 2 results & the power-gate verdict (where it lives)
- **VERDICT: GO** (for the full planned k∈{2,4,8}). The regimes are separable on **every wide-plateau
  level** because the boost plateau is flat with no degradation to 1200 tok: operate total filler `n`
  on the plateau `[n_sat, n_top]`, keep `n/k ≥ n_lo`, so Δ=B₁(n)−B₁(n/k) ≈ plateau − threshold.
  **GO carried by the m=1000 COUNTING separations:** d6 8.8σ, sumprod 6.7σ, d12 6.1σ (d8 marginal
  3.6σ; mul NO-GO, Δ_fix~0). **Binary parallel-vs-divided** → largest-Δ levels (d6/d12, counting).
  **Headline α-fit + 3-way (parallel/divided/null)** → prioritise a **GRADED-rise** level so the
  divided prediction spans intermediate values: **`add_n15_d6`+DOTS** (m=1000; +27pp plateau, graded
  div-slope ~0.3, k=4 +21/7.9σ, k=8 +23/8.7σ; k=2 marginal 2.4σ — covered by counting-d6) or
  `sumprod_t3_d2`. **Recommend ≥2 levels:** a graded-rise primary (d6+dots or sumprod) + a max-signal
  confirmatory (d6-counting / d12). `add_n15_d10` = promising gradual ADDITION option for later.
  Candidate n-grid {50,100,200,400} tok × k∈{2,4,8}, designed **per-level AND per-filler-type** so each
  k has ≥1 graded cell with n/k≥n_lo. **Caveat:** the divided prediction B₁(n/k) is an ABSTRACTION
  (read from the single-question curve); the gate proves the two references are separable, not where
  real B_k lands. Full numbers: `results/b1curve_summary.md`, `analyze_d6_dots_gate.py` + write_up.md.
- **IMPORTANT (analysis gotcha):** the gate operating `n` must range over the **whole no-degradation
  plateau**, NOT be capped at the saturation point `n_sat` (an earlier draft made that mistake and
  wrongly found d8/d12 fail for k>2 — fixed). `analyze_b1curve.py` reports `n_sat` (saturation onset),
  `n_top` (no-degradation bound), and a `div-slope`/`Delta if n/k +-5tok` placement-sensitivity metric.
- **B₁(n) curve files (pass ALL FOUR to `analyze_b1curve.py`/`plot_b1curve.py`; they merge+de-dup):**
  `b1curve_main.jsonl` (76k; addition m=1000) + `b1curve_dense_d6.jsonl` + `b1curve_dense_d8d12.jsonl`
  (densification) + `b1curve_mulsumprod1k.jsonl` (mul/sumprod topped up to m=1000). d6+dots is separate
  (`b1curve_d6_dots_1k.jsonl`, used by `analyze_d6_dots_gate.py`/`plot_d6_filler_compare.py`). Harder
  probe: `harder_probe_main.jsonl`.
- **Gotchas for Segment 2 code:** (1) pandas `.corr()` is a method — use `df["corr"]` for the
  effective-correctness column. (2) `llm_helper.query_gpt(..., thinking=...)` takes a string level
  ('low'/'medium'/...), NOT a bool; default thinking on may eat the whole max_tokens → pass a level
  and ample max_tokens for vision checks. (3) the realized-token mapping is ≈2·items+1 and
  level-independent (same filler string) — but always report the measured `input_tokens` delta.

## k-question apparatus (Segment 4) — gotchas + per-k baselines
- **Validated 4-turn structure** (see kharness.py): the n=0 baseline uses an **EMPTY** middle assistant
  turn (both Anthropic & OpenRouter accept it) so the structure is identical across filler sizes.
  Reveal is by INDEX only (turn 3); `restate=True` is a PILOT diagnostic, NOT for the real experiment.
- **⚑ ADOPTED `--reveal-style minimal`** (the recommended default for the main experiment). A *verbose*
  reveal turn ("...Do not show any work...") between filler and answer SUPPRESSES the boost; a MINIMAL
  reveal (`"Now answer Q3:"`, no-work instruction moved to turn 1) RECOVERS it to ~single-turn on every
  level/filler (incl. d6+dots) and de-inflates the n=0 baseline. Use `--reveal-style minimal` for
  characterization/main runs (the earlier `full`-reveal files are the diagnostic that motivated it).
  `run_structcompare.py` produces single/full/minimal; `scripts/minimal_reveal_probe.py` is the probe.
- **⚠ Two big facts.** (1) The **distractor questions act as filler** → the n=0 baseline **RISES with k**
  (the position effect averaged over the random reveal; Q1 primacy-disadvantaged), so fast addition
  (d8/d12) **ceilings** at high k. (2) With the FULL reveal turn the boost was suppressed, but with the
  **adopted MINIMAL reveal** it recovers to ~single-turn on EVERY level/filler (incl. d6+dots). So:
  re-measure B₁(n) IN-structure with `--reveal-style minimal`; prefer a level that stays mid-band across
  k AND keeps positions off the ceiling — **`sumprod_t3_d2`** (minimal k=1 +36pp, flat 34% across k,
  graded positions 18–56%) is the lead; `add_n15_d6` (+28 cnt / +25 dots, mid-band) and `add_n15_d10`
  (flat +15) are alternatives; d8/d12 ceiling. (d6+dots is NOT disqualified — that was a full-reveal
  artifact.) `run_structcompare.py` reproduces single/full/minimal.
- **Per-k n=0 baselines (reveal_after, told):** FULL reveal — DeepSeek add_n4_d10 54/62/70/72%; Opus
  add_n12_d8 84/87/92/96% (CEILING); add_n15_d8 61/72/86/90%; add_n15_d6 60/41/51/53%; sumprod 42/42/38/34%.
  MINIMAL reveal (de-inflated) — sumprod 34/38/33/34% (flat), add_n12_d8 74/88/90/95% (still ceilings),
  add_n15_d6 51/56/56/54%, DeepSeek 60/64/68/73%. **Strong primacy disadvantage** (Q1 worst; Opus d12
  k=8 Q1=69% vs positions 2–8 ≈100%; sumprod k=8 graded 18–56%) → balance revealed positions.
- DeepSeek k-prompt outputs use `parser=open` (`:\n<int>` / `: **<int>**` separators are benign &
  parse fine); Opus uses `parser=opus`; sumprod under filler → use the `correct_robust` field.
- Pilot B_k variance for Seg-5 sizing: paired-boost SE ≈ 3.3pp@n=250, 2.0pp@n=600.

## Segment 5 design lock-in (how to run the chosen apparatus + gotchas)
- **FINALIZED DESIGN:** lead level **`sumprod_t3_d2` + counting** (the ONLY off-ceiling level);
  secondary consistency check `add_n15_d6`+counting (carry-fragile). `add_n15_d10`/`add_n12_d8` are OUT
  (they CEILING under k: Q2-Q8 → 90-99% at k=8). **Position-BALANCE + position-RESOLVE** (the aggregate
  is a recency-erasure mixture). **reveal-before/after contrast = primary tool** (run with n=0 control).
  Report **additive AND logit**. **Revealed-item-fixed design implemented but rejected as primary**
  (corr(B_k,B1)≈0 → no variance reduction; kept as exact-control arm).
- **In-structure B₁(n) curve** = `run_kquestion.py --ks 1 --reveal-style minimal --condition reveal_after
  --filler-type counting --sizes 0 12 25 38 50 75 100 150 200 300 400 --n-instances 1000`. Curve files:
  `kq_instr_{sumprod_cnt,d6_cnt,d6_dots,d10_cnt,d12_cnt}.jsonl` (dots grid = items=tok). sumprod peaks
  **+37 @150-200 tok then DECLINES** (operate near the peak); use `correct_robust` for sumprod.
- **Position/confound study** = `--reveal-mode sweep_positions --ks 2 4 8 --sizes 0 100`:
  `kq_pos_{sumprod,d6,d10,d12}.jsonl` (after) + `kq_pos_sumprod_before.jsonl` / `kq_pos_d6_before.jsonl`
  (reveal_before contrast; d6 contrast +32.8pp k=8 confirms directedness generalizes off sumprod).
  Sub-n_lo points `kq_instr_lown.jsonl` (sumprod DIP at ~6 tok → n_lo≈12). Inspection:
  `results/inspect_position.md`. Structure-attribution: `structcompare_opus.jsonl` (single vs minimal
  on MATCHED items → in-structure boost only ~+5pp > single-turn, baseline NOT inflated; cancels in k>1).
- **Revealed-item-fixed** = `--design fixed_item [--positions ...]` (`draw_instance_fixed`: same revealed
  item across k at a fixed slot; `inst` indexes the revealed item). Validation files
  `kq_fixed_{opus,ds}_{after,before}.jsonl`.
- **GATE VERDICT (in-structure, sumprod+counting): GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2.** At n=200 tok
  Δ par−div +11.8/+22.3 (k4/k8), Δ div−null +25.3/+14.7 — all three hypotheses resolvable for k=4,8.
  k=2 needs a lower operating n=100. The likely outcome is the **B_k≈0 undirected null** (reveal_after
  ≈0 on clean positions; reveal_before clearly + — contrast +18.9pp k=8 Opus, +8.0 DeepSeek).
- **Gotcha:** the naive position-balanced aggregate B_k is **below null** (−6.8pp) because filler ERASES
  the recency advantage of late positions — NOT the science. Position-resolve + use the contrast.
- **`llm_openrouter._is_retryable` now retries `json.JSONDecodeError`** (a provider returning malformed
  JSON crashed a DeepSeek run; fixed so the main experiment won't crash).

  provider×size balance / quant-confound check on the actual cells), `analyze_seg9_verdict.py` (→
  `seg9_verdict.md` + `seg9_cells.csv` + `seg9_contrast.csv` + `seg9_opus_ref.md`; `compute_opus_ref`
  derives the Opus comparison contrast off cached seg7_* — NO Opus re-runs), `sample_seg9.py` (→
  `inspect_seg9_kquestion.md`), `plot_seg9.py` (→ `plots/seg9_{b1curve,contrast,position_bk,
  opus_vs_ds_contrast,undirected_decline}.png`). Runner = `run_seg9.sh` (ASSERT_CACHED=1 reproduces at $0).
- **Result files:** `seg9_ds_k1_after.jsonl` (B₁ curve, m=4000, 11-pt grid) + `seg9_ds_k{2,4,8}_{after,
  before}.jsonl` (sweep_positions, sizes {0,100,200} items = {0,200,400} tok, m_inst=6000 for k=4,8 /
  3000 k=2). Reuses cached pilot draws.
- **Gotchas (DeepSeek k-experiment):** (1) **Q1-only is the CLEAN read**, NOT the early pool — add_n4_d10
  is fast-saturating so Q2-Q4 are recency-elevated at n=0; filler erases that → the early-pool reveal-after
  B_k is dragged NEGATIVE (a recency artifact). Lead the regime/contrast with Q1-only; co-report early-pool
  (Opus-matched). (2) Operating points keep n/k ≥ ~50 tok: k=2,4 @ 200 tok (size 100 items), k=8 @ 400 tok
  (size 200 items). (3) The good-cluster provider mix is BALANCED across filler sizes (ModelRun ~66%,
  SiliconFlow ~33% at size 0 AND 200) → the paired boost is unbiased; NOT the Segment-3 size-dependent
  quant confound. (4) k=2 contrast ≈0 / full undirected retention is a REAL cross-model difference (tight
  CIs + sig.+ positive control), not a power artifact. (5) α NOT fit (parallel≈divided; modest boost).
  Opus contrast comparison numbers are COMPUTED by `analyze_seg9_verdict.compute_opus_ref` off the cached
  `seg7_sumprod_*` files → `results/seg9_opus_ref.md` (reproducible; no Opus re-runs). (6) **The headline
  caveat is that cross-MODEL is confounded with cross-LEVEL** (Opus=gradual sumprod, DeepSeek=fast-sat
  add_n4_d10) — no DeepSeek level matches sumprod's flat early band, so model-vs-level can't be separated.
  (7) The cleanest cross-k stat = the **constant-199-tok undirected decline** (+8.2→+4.5→+1.7), holds
  total filler fixed.

## Segment 10 results — DONE (robustness of the evolved headline; Opus only)
- **VERDICT lives in `results/seg10_verdict.md`** (+ `seg10_cells.csv`, `seg10_contrast.csv`). Phase
  write-ups: `writeups/write_up_segment_10_phase_0.md` + `progress_log_segment_10_phase_0.md`. Figures
  `plots/seg10_{v1_framing,v2_decline,v3_dots}.png`. no-CoT `results/seg10_nocot.md`; data inspection
  `results/inspect_seg10.md`.
- **Scripts:** `run_segment10.sh` (the 3-variation run manifest; `ASSERT_CACHED=1 bash run_segment10.sh`
  reproduces at $0). `seg10_lib.py` (analysis lib → `seg7_lib`/`seg8_lib`; `Curve=seg9_lib.DSCurve` for a
  k=1 reference from an arbitrary path; `regime_cell`/`contrast_cell`/`logit_contrast`/`off_ceiling_ok`;
  `OP_ITEMS_COUNTING`/`OP_ITEMS_DOTS`; `fpath(variation,k,cond)` with variation∈{nottold,altk,dots};
  `told_fpath`/`load_told` reuse the cached seg7 cells). `analyze_seg10_verdict.py` (→ verdict.md + CSVs;
  V1/V2/V3 sections + the consolidated HOLD/WEAKEN/BREAK). `analyze_seg10_nocot.py` (→ seg10_nocot.md).
  `plot_seg10.py`. `sample_seg10.py` (→ inspect_seg10.md; reconstructs prompts via draw_instance+
  build_k_messages since rows don't store `messages`).
- **Result files (`results/seg10_*.jsonl`):** `seg10_nottold_sumprod_k{1,2,4,8}_after` + `k{2,4,8}_before`
  (before = $0 cache-hit of the framing-invariant seg7 told before); `seg10_altk_sumprod_k{3,6,16}_{after,
  before}`; `seg10_dots_sumprod_k1_after` + `k{4,8}_{after,before}`. Counting=2 tok/item, dots=1 tok/item.
- **Disclosure follow-up (review-driven): `scripts_segment_10_phase_0/run_disclosure.py`** →
  `results/seg10_disclose_sumprod_k{4,8}_after.jsonl` (framing='disclose' = told MINUS the "— so be ready to
  answer any of them" clause; built by post-processing `build_k_messages`, NOT a harness edit). Result:
  disclose ≈ told ≪ not-told (k=8 Q1 +2.0/+1.2/+26.3) → the random-target **DISCLOSURE** drives the
  Q1-suppression, the encouragement is INCIDENTAL. `seg10_lib.fpath('disclose',k,'reveal_after')`.
- **Gotchas:** (1) **reveal_before is framing-invariant** (names the target in turn1 regardless of
  told/not_told) → the not_told before cells are byte-identical to the seg7 told before cells (cache-hit);
  the told→not_told manipulation bites ONLY on reveal_after. (2) **The directedness contrast under not-told
  SURVIVES on the PRIMARY early-pool read** (+9.1/+10.2 at k=4,8, sig.+) and collapses ONLY on Q1-only
  (because Q1 is the model's DEFAULT undirected target) — an earlier "contrast vanishes" claim was WRONG and
  retracted. Report BOTH reads; the V1 BREAK is carried by the REGIME/near-null leg, NOT the contrast. (3)
  Dots matched on REALIZED TOKENS (200 tok = 200 dots items), not item count. (4) k=16 off-ceiling but
  n/16=12.5 tok < n_lo → divided ref in the dip (np.interp-clamps to B1(24tok)) — decline-extension only.
  (5) V2 robust claim = `B_k ≪ divided` at EVERY k, NOT a clean monotone decline (overlapping CIs). (6)
  Unexplained dots×k interaction (dots≈counting at k=1 but disruptive in k-structure) — Segment-11 flag.
- **Verdict:** V2 (alt-k) + V3 (dots) HOLD under told; **V1 (framing) BREAKS** — the random-target DISCLOSURE
  (not the encouragement) suppresses the model's Q1-default banking; the near-null REGIME leg breaks (REFUTES
  "doesn't bank undirected"), the directedness contrast WEAKENS-but-SURVIVES (early-pool), and "no parallel
  latent thinking" is REINFORCED. Marginal spend ≈ $470; cumulative ≈ $1758.

## Segment 11 results — DONE (mechanism & confound investigation; Opus d6 + sumprod + DeepSeek)
- **VERDICT lives in `results/seg11_verdict.md`** (+ per-tier `results/seg11_{framing,mechanism,default,
  cap,dots,nocot}.md` + CSVs `seg11_{framing,dose,selectivity,default,cap}.csv`). Phase write-ups:
  `writeups/write_up_segment_11_phase_0.md` + `progress_log_segment_11_phase_0.md`. Figures
  `plots/seg11_{framing,dose,default_cap,selectivity}.png`.
- **Scripts:** `run_segment11.sh` + `scripts_segment_11_phase_0/launch_all.sh` (run manifest;
  `ASSERT_CACHED=1 bash run_segment11.sh` reproduces the run_kquestion cells at $0).
  `scripts_segment_11_phase_0/run_disclosure_gen.py` = the GENERALIZED disclose arm (told MINUS the
  encouragement clause, any level/model, sweep_positions; post-processes `build_k_messages`, locked
  harness untouched). `seg11_lib.py` (→ seg7/8/9/10 machinery; `d6_path`/`ds_path` map framing
  not_told→'nottold'; `boost`/`logit_boost`/`contrast`/`per_position_boost`; `D6_OP`/`DS_OP` op sizes).
  `analyze_seg11_{nocot,framing,mechanism,default,cap,dots,verdict}.py` (analysis-only, no API calls).
  `plot_seg11.py`, `sample_seg11.py` (→ `results/inspect_seg11.md`; reconstructs prompts incl. fixed_item
  + disclose).
- **Result files (`results/seg11_*.jsonl`):** `seg11_d6_{nottold,disclose}_k{4,8}_after`;
  `seg11_ds_{nottold_k{4,8},disclose_k8}_after` (DeepSeek, provider-pinned); `seg11_sumprod_{before,
  nottold}_k8_dose` (fine n-grid, sweep_positions); `seg11_sumprod_nottold_fixedpos_k8` (default-target,
  fixed_item positions {0,1,3,7}); `seg11_sumprod_before_fixedpos0_k{1,2,4,8}` (context-cap, fixed_item
  pos0); `seg11_dots_nottold_k8_after` (dots×k probe). Each has a `_meta` line-1.
- **Findings (headline; full detail in write_up_segment_11_phase_0.md — don't trust this summary over it):**
  (1) not-told Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression REPLICATES in DIRECTION cross-family (d6
  told/disclose/not-told Q1 k8 = −2.2/−3.4/+10.6) + cross-model (DeepSeek +3.4/+2.2/+6.2), but magnitude
  ATTENUATES even on logit (DeepSeek ~10× weaker, partial); (2) genuine computation STRENGTHENED-but-bounded
  — directed dose-response rises with filler amount (tracks lone B₁(n)); selectivity rises +6.7→+16.2 at a
  FLAT sumprod baseline (on d6 it rises with a baseline confound, on DeepSeek flat=none); banks predominantly
  Q1; SUPPORTED = genuine/dose-responsive/difficulty-selective/**filler-amount-dependent** (NOT 'filler-time';
  localization unproven), NOT parallel, NOT pure-step attention (graded attention NOT excluded); (3) default
  target POSITIONAL (fixed-item +25.1 Q1 vs −18.0 Q8); (4) context-cap mostly STRUCTURE (lead logit; partly
  headroom pre-consumption; lone +43→+24–30 with ≥1 distractor); (3.5) dots×k framing-independent; (3.6) d6
  carry anomaly = open puzzle.
- **Gotchas:** (1) seg11 file framing tag is **'nottold'** (no underscore) — `seg11_lib._FR_TAG` maps
  `not_told`→'nottold'. (2) **DeepSeek not-told n=0 truncation ~1% is BENIGN** answer-first-then-continue
  (answer is the first token, parsed correctly; boost robust to dropping truncated rows; only 2/369 are
  genuine CoT). (3) d6 is carry-fragile (k=8 Q8 not-told −60pp) → LEAD with Q1 + logit. (4) the dose-
  response/fixed cells are sweep_positions/fixed_item so they cache-hit the seg7/seg9/seg10 size-0/100
  cells; only the new sizes are fresh. (5) max-product is the sumprod difficulty proxy (selectivity).
  Marginal spend ≈ $409; cumulative ≈ $2167.

## Current state
- **PROJECT COMPLETE (Segments 0–12).** **Segment 12 = final synthesis + reproducibility + review (DONE).**
  Consolidated everything into the `write_up.md` **"Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section (evolved
  anti-parallel headline; §5-vs-Segment-10 reconciliation as a *sharpening*; how-to-reproduce guide);
  polished + GPT-vision-checked the headline figures (de-overclaimed captions; two figure fixes —
  `plots/seg11_default_cap.png` two-axis legend, `plots/seg10_v1_framing.png` panel-(c) title); **verified
  `--assert-cached` reproduces the Segment-7/9/10/11 grids + the seg10/11 disclosure arms at $0 (no cache
  miss)**; the analysis/verdict scripts regenerate `seg8_verdict.md` byte-identically (seed-0
  deterministic). Phase write-ups: `writeups/write_up_segment_12_phase_0.md` +
  `progress_log_segment_12_phase_0.md`. No new experiments; the locked grid/harness/scorers/pre-registration
  and the §5 verdict are untouched. Earlier: **Segment 11 = mechanism & confound investigation (DONE;
  cross-family + cross-model REPLICATES; genuine computation STRENGTHENED-not-proven; default positional;
  cap structure).** See the Segment-11 block above. Earlier: **Segment 10 = robustness of the evolved
  headline (DONE; FRAMING-DEPENDENT via V1, k/filler-robust via V2/V3).** See the Segment-10 block above.
  Earlier:
  **Segment 9 = cross-model DeepSeek generalization (DONE; PARTIALLY REPLICATES).** See the Segment-9 block above. Earlier: **Segment 8 = the pre-registered §5 regime +
  directedness VERDICT (DONE);**
  `results/seg8_verdict.md` + `seg8_regime_cells.csv` + `seg8_alpha_collapse.md`; figures
  `plots/seg8_*.png`; scripts `seg8_lib.py` / `analyze_seg8_verdict.py` / `plot_seg8.py`. Verdict =
  PARTIAL/sub-divided (near-null), DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE, "partial directed". Segment 7 result files
  `results/seg7_*.jsonl`, guard table `results/seg7_guards.md` (all PASS), descriptive summary
  `results/main_run_summary.md`. Segment-8 spend ~$0.12 (GPT-vision plot checks only); cumulative ≈ $1214.71. Single authoritative Segment-1 lock file =
  `results/lock_20260604_014350.jsonl` (`canonical.LOCK_FILE`; validators default to it); calibration grid
  = `results/calibrate_20260604_012523.jsonl` (analyze globs the latest); plots = `plots/calibration.png`
  + `plots/dataset_quality.png`; inspection = `results/inspect_canonical.md`. (Older intermediate
  lock/calibrate files were consolidated away; they remain in git history.) Segment-0 headline de-risk
  run = `results/derisk_full_20260603_105934.jsonl`;
  reasoning-bound runs = `results/derisk_reasoning_20260603_112943.jsonl` (k12/k18/AIME) +
  `results/derisk_reasoning2_20260603_233701.jsonl` (k6, matched baseline); finer sweep =
  `results/ngrid_20260603_112506.jsonl`. Key plots: `derisk_full.png`, `selectivity.png`, `ngrid.png`.
- `analyze_nocot_integrity.py` and `plot_selectivity.py` hard-code their input filenames — update if
  re-running with new timestamps. AIME 2025 is demoted (partially memorized).
- Pilot files (`derisk_pilot_*`) used the buggy scratch-space framing for user placement and are
  **superseded** — do not use the GSM-Hard "−28%" from `derisk_pilot` (analysis artifact).
- Note on `plot_selectivity.py`: the input result filenames are hard-coded — update them if you
  re-run the de-risk with new timestamps.
- For later segments: Segment 2 resolved the power gate to **GO for k≤8 on multiple levels** (the
  wide no-degradation plateau is the enabler — operate `n` on the plateau, `n/k` at the threshold);
  see the Segment-2 verdict block above + `write_up.md`. (`add_n15_d8` is NOT disqualified — it just
  has the smallest Δ.)
- **Segment 4 k-question result files** (`results/kq_*.jsonl`, `structcompare_opus.jsonl`): see the
  per-file list in the Segment-4 progress_log entry; consolidated numbers in `results/kquestion_summary.md`,
  inspection in `results/inspect_kquestion_deepseek.md`, plots `plots/kq_*.png`. `analyze_kquestion.py`
  + `plot_kquestion.py` reference these files by name (update if re-run with new names).

## Open model (Segment 3) — how to call DeepSeek-V3-0324 + gotchas
- **Selected open model = DeepSeek-V3-0324** (`deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`). Genuine no-CoT: prefill
  (final `{"role":"assistant","content":"<filler> The answer is"}`) is honored; reasoning OFF via
  `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":False}}`. Verified clean (0.03% text-before-answer, 0% reasoning
  field, 0.01% truncation on the good provider cluster). Fast/cheap/reliable.
- **⚠ PIN THE PROVIDER (most important gotcha).** OpenRouter routes DeepSeek-V3-0324 across providers at
  DIFFERENT quantizations: GOOD cluster {DeepInfra, ModelRun, SiliconFlow} ≈ 54–56% acc, BAD cluster
  {AtlasCloud, Novita, GMICloud} ≈ 22–29% (and the bad ones produce CoT/truncation junk). Unpinned, the
  provider mix is size-dependent and **inflated the boost ~2×** (and inflated truncation). ALWAYS run with
  `run_openrouter.py --only-providers deepinfra modelrun siliconflow` (allowlist a homogeneous-accuracy
  cluster, load-balanced) or a single `--provider DeepInfra`. Headline files are `orboost_GC_deepseek_*`
  (the `orboost_FINAL_*`/`orboost_deepseek_*` files are the SUPERSEDED unpinned/inflated runs).
- **Mid-band for this model (good cluster):** Opus canonical levels are at FLOOR (DeepSeek=0% on
  `add_n15_d8`). Use the generator: primary **`add_n4_d10`** (base ~52%). The boost is NARROW — null at

# Continuation Context (handoff)

Practical "how to run things" + gotchas. Research findings live in `write_up.md` (the canonical final
document — read its **"Final synthesis (READ THIS)"** section first); the chronological record is in
`progress_log.md` (Segments 0–8) plus the phase-specific `progress_log_segment_{9,10,11,12}_phase_0.md`.
**Write-up convention:** Segments 0–8 were **unified-only**; from Segment 9 on the project keeps
**phase-specific** write-ups (`write_up_segment_<phase>.md` + `progress_log_segment_<phase>.md`) **plus**
the unified `write_up.md` (key takeaways + the synthesis). **PROJECT COMPLETE (Segments 0–12).**

## Scripts and their purposes
- **Segment 5 (in-structure curve + confound + design): `instr_lib.py`** (load k=1 in-structure rows,
  paired boost matrix, bootstrap, rising-region) + **`analyze_instructure.py`** (`--curve
  level:filler:path` → `results/instructure_summary.md` + the 3-way gate CSV) + **`plot_instructure.py`**
  (→ `plots/instructure_b1.png` / `_narrowing.png` / `_gate.png`). **`analyze_position.py`** (`--pos
  level:path --plateau-curve level:filler:path` → `results/position_confound.md`) + **`plot_position.py`**
  (→ `plots/position_confound.png`). **`analyze_fixed_validate.py`** (revealed-item-fixed validation →
  `results/fixed_validate_opus.md`). **`analyze_contrast.py`** (→ `results/directedness_contrast.md`,
  the consolidated reveal-before/after table: sumprod/d6/DeepSeek) + **`plot_contrast.py`** (→
  `plots/directedness_contrast.png`).
  Run the in-structure curves/position study/fixed design via `run_kquestion.py` (below). Consolidated
  verdict: `results/segment5_verdict.md`.
- **`kharness.py` (Segment 4)** — the k-question reveal-by-index builder + scorer. `build_k_messages(
  items, *, reveal_index, condition={reveal_after|reveal_before}, framing={told|not_told}, filler_type,
  filler_size, restate=False)` → 4-turn messages (user questions → assistant filler → user reveal-by-
  index → assistant `"The answer to Qj is"` prefill; n=0 = EMPTY filler turn, both APIs accept it).
  `answer_k_problem(...; caller=, parser={opus|open})` runs one prompt; records correct + matched_positions
  (Python-int, precision-safe). `draw_instance(pool,k,inst,salt)` = deterministic i.i.d. k-item draw +
  reveal_index (depends only on salt(=level)/k/inst → paired across conditions/sizes). `python3 kharness.py`
  prints example prompts.
- **`run_kquestion.py` (Segment 4)** — the sweep runner (model-agnostic). `--model --parser {opus|open}
  --levels --ks --condition --framing --filler-type --sizes (incl 0) --n-instances --reveal-mode
  {random|sweep_positions} --restate` + provider controls (`--only-providers ...` for DeepSeek good
  cluster). FRESH_OFFSET=500, POOL_SIZE=2500 (items [500,3000), fixed so draws reproduce). Streams
  `results/kq_*.jsonl` + meta line; `--assert-cached`. Realized filler tokens = input_tokens(size) −
  input_tokens(0) per paired instance.
- **`kanalysis.py` (Segment 4)** — analysis lib (precision-safe, no pandas): `load_rows`, `acc_ci`
  (bootstrap), `paired_boost` (B_k(n) per size vs size-0, same instance), `by_k_n0`, `by_position`,
  `answered_asked` (task 2b), `index_recall_gap` (2c). `analyze_kquestion.py` → `results/kquestion_summary.md`;
  `sample_kquestion.py <files> --out ...` → data-inspection md (reconstructs exact prompts); `plot_kquestion.py`
  → `plots/kq_*.png`.
- **`run_structcompare.py` (Segment 4)** — single-turn (Seg-2 layout) vs multi-turn k=1 boost on the SAME
  items across a difficulty ladder → `results/structcompare_opus.jsonl` (the headline structural-filler
  finding). `scripts/structvsplain_probe.py` / `scripts/adjacency_probe.py` = focused diagnostics
  (structure inflates baseline; reveal-turn separation suppresses carry-fragile d6).
- `llm.py` — cached Opus 4.5 wrapper. `from llm import call_opus`; call `llm.configure(cost_tracker,
  concurrency, assert_cached)` once at program start. Returns `{text, input_tokens, output_tokens,
  stop_reason}`. Reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from env (NO `~/.anthropic_api_key` here).
- **`llm_openrouter.py` (Segment 3)** — cached OpenRouter wrapper (open models). `import
  llm_openrouter as lor`; `lor.configure(cost_tracker, concurrency, assert_cached)`;
  `caller = lor.make_caller("deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324", reasoning_disabled=True)`; pass `caller` to
  `harness.answer_problem(..., caller=caller)`. Returns `{text, input_tokens, output_tokens, stop_reason,
  reasoning, reasoning_content, provider, ...}`. NON-streaming; reads `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` from env/.env.
  Reasoning OFF = `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":False}}` (set by `make_caller`). Cache key = full
  request incl. `extra_body` + sample_idx + uuid. Do NOT route experiment calls through `llm_helper.py`.
- **`or_levels.py` (Segment 3)** — open-model difficulty levels via the generator (`OR_SEED=7777`,
  distinct from canonical seed 12345). `load_level(spec, n, offset)`; `OR_LEVELS` registry +
  `resolve_level(tag)`; `CALIB_GRID` / `BIG_GRID` (difficulty sweeps; `run_or_calibrate.py --big`).
  Selected primary open level: **`add_n4_d10`** (4× 10-digit addition, DeepSeek good-cluster base ~52%).
- **`run_or_calibrate.py`** — open-model n=0 baseline across `CALIB_GRID` (`--model ... --n 300`).
- **`run_openrouter.py`** — paired boost + rising-region runner (`--model --levels --filler-type
  {counting,dots} --n --sizes`). Provider control: `--only-providers ...` (allowlist cluster, USE THIS),
  `--provider X` (single pin), `--ignore-providers ...`. Streams `results/orboost_*.jsonl`. `--assert-cached`.
- **`analyze_or_robust.py`** — logit-scale boost + error-structure (near-miss/give-up + filler fix-rate) +
  provider-stratified accuracy table. `python3 analyze_or_robust.py <orboost_*.jsonl> --plateau-from 100`.
- **`run_or_survey.py`** — multi-model survey (`--models ... --levels add_n4_d8 add_n5_d8 add_n6_d8`).
- **`or_analysis.py`** — robust open-model analysis (separate from `b1lib.py`): `load_rows`,
  `level_matrices`, `boot_curves`, `curve_summary`, `cleanliness`. Handles huge-int parses (no pandas).
- **`analyze_openrouter.py` / `plot_openrouter.py` / `plot_or_survey.py` / `sample_openrouter.py`** —
  summary md/CSV, boost-vs-realized-tokens figure, selection figure, no-CoT data-inspection md.
- `scripts/segment3_smoke.py` — quick prefill/reasoning-off/format smoke probe for candidate models.
- `arithmetic.py` — `generate_dataset(family, params, n, seed)`; deterministic. `python3
  arithmetic.py` runs self-checks.
- `filler.py` — `make_filler(filler_type, size, problem_text=None)`.
- `harness.py` — `build_messages(...)`, `answer_problem(...)`, `parse_answer`, `score`. `python3
  harness.py` runs parse self-tests.
- `tasks.py` — `load_task(spec, n, seed)` for procedural / gsm_hard / gsm8k / count_correct / aime2025.
- `reasoning_tasks.py` — reasoning-bound tasks: `generate_count_correct(...)` (parallelizable
  many-easy-checks generator), `load_aime2025(...)`.
- `canonical.py` — **Segment 1 canonical datasets**. `CANONICAL` = locked levels;
  `load_canonical(tag, n=None)` returns the exact saved items from `data/<tag>.jsonl`;
  `python3 canonical.py` (re)saves all; `python3 canonical.py --check` asserts they regenerate.
- `run_calibrate.py` — difficulty sweep -> no-filler baseline table (finer mid-band grid).
  `python3 run_calibrate.py --n 300` (seed 12345 so 4 Segment-0 baselines cache-hit).
- `run_lock.py` — per candidate level: n=500 baseline + disjoint held-out (items 3000-3499) +
  filler-responsiveness gate (paired). `python3 run_lock.py [--assert-cached]`.
- `analyze_calibration.py` — calibration grid + locked-level table -> `plots/calibration.png` (3-panel).
- `analyze_mul_homogeneity.py` — reproducible mul homogenization demo (raw 27% gap -> 0%).
- `validate_canonical.py` — dataset validation (D1/D2/D3/D7/D8); all PASS. Defaults to `canonical.LOCK_FILE`.
- `sample_canonical.py` — data-inspection markdown -> `results/inspect_canonical.md`.

## Gotchas / learned context
- **Prefill on Opus 4.5 works**, but the prefilled assistant content **must not end with trailing
  whitespace** (API 400). Lead-in is `"The answer is"` (no trailing space).
- **Filler framing matters enormously.** Framing user-prompt filler as "scratch space to work"
  makes the model emit explicit CoT on word problems → breaks no-CoT + corrupts answer extraction.
  Use neutral "ignore this padding" framing (see `harness.py:_USER_FILLER_PREFIX`). Prefill placement
  avoids this entirely and is the chosen default.
- **Opus 4.5 memorizes standard math benchmarks** (GSM8K/MATH/AIME at ceiling, no-CoT). For a
  mid-range positive control use a non-memorizable computation-bound set (GSM-Hard). To measure
  genuine capability, prefer procedural generation.
- **GSM-Hard has ~12% degenerate problems** (negative/nonsensical answers). Paired boost is robust
  to them; filter `truth >= 0` for a cleaner baseline if needed.
- **Answer extraction = FIRST integer** (this is the correct no-CoT measure; do NOT switch to last
  integer — that would count reasoning-derived answers).
- Concurrency 100 worked with no rate-limit issues; runs are fast (15000 calls ≈ 3.5 min uncached,
  seconds when cached). Caching is keyed on full API kwargs + `sample_idx` + a code UUID in `llm.py`.
- `gemini-3.1-pro` is NOT available on this sandbox's OpenRouter (llm_helper `query_all_models`
  fails on the gemini leg); query with `models=["claude","gpt"]`. (So Gemini-vision plot checks
  aren't available; use `query_gpt(..., image_path=...)` for an LLM vision check if needed.)
- **Difficulty knob (Segment 1):** for addition, **#operands at fixed 8 digits is the clean
  monotonic knob**; 6-digit is **non-smooth** (confirmed localized `add_n14_d6`=34% dip vs ~49%
  neighbors — a model quirk; shipped n15_d6 is fine). Multiplication difficulty depends on **both**
  operands (≈ rows×width), not the smaller alone. **Single grid points are noisy at n=300** — trust
  the pooled n=1000 (calib+held-out) for locked levels.
- **Under filler, mul/expr emit ~7% spaced-digit / shows-working outputs** (the format artifact);
  addition stays clean. n=0 baselines are clean for all. `parse_answer_robust` re-scores spaced
  digits if needed downstream.

## Canonical datasets (Segment 1) — how to use them downstream
- Load the **exact** canonical items: `from canonical import load_canonical; items =
  load_canonical("add_n15_d8")` (or `tasks.load_task({"loader":"canonical","tag":"add_n15_d8"}, n=...)`).
  Each item: `{id, question, answer, family, params, task_type:"arithmetic", features}`.
- Locked tags (6 levels, 3 families; each pool = 5000 items): `add_n15_d8` (PRIMARY ~55%),
  `add_n16_d8` (~47%), `add_n12_d8` (~68%, k-headroom), `add_n15_d6` (~52%, +30pp max boost),
  `mul_a4_b4_hom` (2nd family ~47%), `sumprod_t3_d2` (3rd family, all-+ sum-of-products ~40%).
- **Selection-bias guard:** the calibrated baseline used items **[0,500)** and held-out **[3000,3500)**.
  `load_canonical(tag, n)` returns items `[0:n]` = the CALIBRATION sample; for an unbiased FRESH draw
  pass `offset>=500` (e.g. `load_canonical(tag, n=8, offset=500)`).
- All canonical data uses **seed 12345** (matches Segment-0 de-risk + calibration, so n=0 baselines
  cache-hit). Baselines are reported as the **pooled n=1000** (calib+held-out) estimate.
- Filtered levels (`mul_a4_b4_hom`; `sumprod` is unfiltered) use a named `item_filter` recorded in
  metadata; saved item ids are `<tag>_<i>` (not difficulty_tag), so join cached lock outputs by **question**.
- Metadata (line 1 of each `data/*.jsonl`): pinned model config, derived pooled baseline+CI, calib vs
  held-out, filler-gate boost, calibration/held-out item ranges, lock file, per-item feature schema.
- The d8 addition levels share a seed -> **nested prefixes** (smaller-n operands inside larger-n);
  fine for within-level matching, but the three levels are correlated across difficulties.
- **Boost-magnitude caveat:** mul/sumprod emit ~7% spaced-digit outputs UNDER filler -> use
  `harness.parse_answer_robust` (not the conservative first-int) when measuring their boost magnitude
  downstream, else the boost is under-measured. Addition is clean under filler.

## Segment 2 results & the power-gate verdict (where it lives)
- **VERDICT: GO** (for the full planned k∈{2,4,8}). The regimes are separable on **every wide-plateau
  level** because the boost plateau is flat with no degradation to 1200 tok: operate total filler `n`
  on the plateau `[n_sat, n_top]`, keep `n/k ≥ n_lo`, so Δ=B₁(n)−B₁(n/k) ≈ plateau − threshold.
  **GO carried by the m=1000 COUNTING separations:** d6 8.8σ, sumprod 6.7σ, d12 6.1σ (d8 marginal
  3.6σ; mul NO-GO, Δ_fix~0). **Binary parallel-vs-divided** → largest-Δ levels (d6/d12, counting).
  **Headline α-fit + 3-way (parallel/divided/null)** → prioritise a **GRADED-rise** level so the
  divided prediction spans intermediate values: **`add_n15_d6`+DOTS** (m=1000; +27pp plateau, graded
  div-slope ~0.3, k=4 +21/7.9σ, k=8 +23/8.7σ; k=2 marginal 2.4σ — covered by counting-d6) or
  `sumprod_t3_d2`. **Recommend ≥2 levels:** a graded-rise primary (d6+dots or sumprod) + a max-signal
  confirmatory (d6-counting / d12). `add_n15_d10` = promising gradual ADDITION option for later.
  Candidate n-grid {50,100,200,400} tok × k∈{2,4,8}, designed **per-level AND per-filler-type** so each
  k has ≥1 graded cell with n/k≥n_lo. **Caveat:** the divided prediction B₁(n/k) is an ABSTRACTION
  (read from the single-question curve); the gate proves the two references are separable, not where
  real B_k lands. Full numbers: `results/b1curve_summary.md`, `analyze_d6_dots_gate.py` + write_up.md.
- **IMPORTANT (analysis gotcha):** the gate operating `n` must range over the **whole no-degradation
  plateau**, NOT be capped at the saturation point `n_sat` (an earlier draft made that mistake and
  wrongly found d8/d12 fail for k>2 — fixed). `analyze_b1curve.py` reports `n_sat` (saturation onset),
  `n_top` (no-degradation bound), and a `div-slope`/`Delta if n/k +-5tok` placement-sensitivity metric.
- **B₁(n) curve files (pass ALL FOUR to `analyze_b1curve.py`/`plot_b1curve.py`; they merge+de-dup):**
  `b1curve_main.jsonl` (76k; addition m=1000) + `b1curve_dense_d6.jsonl` + `b1curve_dense_d8d12.jsonl`
  (densification) + `b1curve_mulsumprod1k.jsonl` (mul/sumprod topped up to m=1000). d6+dots is separate
  (`b1curve_d6_dots_1k.jsonl`, used by `analyze_d6_dots_gate.py`/`plot_d6_filler_compare.py`). Harder
  probe: `harder_probe_main.jsonl`.
- **Gotchas for Segment 2 code:** (1) pandas `.corr()` is a method — use `df["corr"]` for the
  effective-correctness column. (2) `llm_helper.query_gpt(..., thinking=...)` takes a string level
  ('low'/'medium'/...), NOT a bool; default thinking on may eat the whole max_tokens → pass a level
  and ample max_tokens for vision checks. (3) the realized-token mapping is ≈2·items+1 and
  level-independent (same filler string) — but always report the measured `input_tokens` delta.

## k-question apparatus (Segment 4) — gotchas + per-k baselines
- **Validated 4-turn structure** (see kharness.py): the n=0 baseline uses an **EMPTY** middle assistant
  turn (both Anthropic & OpenRouter accept it) so the structure is identical across filler sizes.
  Reveal is by INDEX only (turn 3); `restate=True` is a PILOT diagnostic, NOT for the real experiment.
- **⚑ ADOPTED `--reveal-style minimal`** (the recommended default for the main experiment). A *verbose*
  reveal turn ("...Do not show any work...") between filler and answer SUPPRESSES the boost; a MINIMAL
  reveal (`"Now answer Q3:"`, no-work instruction moved to turn 1) RECOVERS it to ~single-turn on every
  level/filler (incl. d6+dots) and de-inflates the n=0 baseline. Use `--reveal-style minimal` for
  characterization/main runs (the earlier `full`-reveal files are the diagnostic that motivated it).
  `run_structcompare.py` produces single/full/minimal; `scripts/minimal_reveal_probe.py` is the probe.
- **⚠ Two big facts.** (1) The **distractor questions act as filler** → the n=0 baseline **RISES with k**
  (the position effect averaged over the random reveal; Q1 primacy-disadvantaged), so fast addition
  (d8/d12) **ceilings** at high k. (2) With the FULL reveal turn the boost was suppressed, but with the
  **adopted MINIMAL reveal** it recovers to ~single-turn on EVERY level/filler (incl. d6+dots). So:
  re-measure B₁(n) IN-structure with `--reveal-style minimal`; prefer a level that stays mid-band across
  k AND keeps positions off the ceiling — **`sumprod_t3_d2`** (minimal k=1 +36pp, flat 34% across k,
  graded positions 18–56%) is the lead; `add_n15_d6` (+28 cnt / +25 dots, mid-band) and `add_n15_d10`
  (flat +15) are alternatives; d8/d12 ceiling. (d6+dots is NOT disqualified — that was a full-reveal
  artifact.) `run_structcompare.py` reproduces single/full/minimal.
- **Per-k n=0 baselines (reveal_after, told):** FULL reveal — DeepSeek add_n4_d10 54/62/70/72%; Opus
  add_n12_d8 84/87/92/96% (CEILING); add_n15_d8 61/72/86/90%; add_n15_d6 60/41/51/53%; sumprod 42/42/38/34%.
  MINIMAL reveal (de-inflated) — sumprod 34/38/33/34% (flat), add_n12_d8 74/88/90/95% (still ceilings),
  add_n15_d6 51/56/56/54%, DeepSeek 60/64/68/73%. **Strong primacy disadvantage** (Q1 worst; Opus d12
  k=8 Q1=69% vs positions 2–8 ≈100%; sumprod k=8 graded 18–56%) → balance revealed positions.
- DeepSeek k-prompt outputs use `parser=open` (`:\n<int>` / `: **<int>**` separators are benign &
  parse fine); Opus uses `parser=opus`; sumprod under filler → use the `correct_robust` field.
- Pilot B_k variance for Seg-5 sizing: paired-boost SE ≈ 3.3pp@n=250, 2.0pp@n=600.

## Segment 5 design lock-in (how to run the chosen apparatus + gotchas)
- **FINALIZED DESIGN:** lead level **`sumprod_t3_d2` + counting** (the ONLY off-ceiling level);
  secondary consistency check `add_n15_d6`+counting (carry-fragile). `add_n15_d10`/`add_n12_d8` are OUT
  (they CEILING under k: Q2-Q8 → 90-99% at k=8). **Position-BALANCE + position-RESOLVE** (the aggregate
  is a recency-erasure mixture). **reveal-before/after contrast = primary tool** (run with n=0 control).
  Report **additive AND logit**. **Revealed-item-fixed design implemented but rejected as primary**
  (corr(B_k,B1)≈0 → no variance reduction; kept as exact-control arm).
- **In-structure B₁(n) curve** = `run_kquestion.py --ks 1 --reveal-style minimal --condition reveal_after
  --filler-type counting --sizes 0 12 25 38 50 75 100 150 200 300 400 --n-instances 1000`. Curve files:
  `kq_instr_{sumprod_cnt,d6_cnt,d6_dots,d10_cnt,d12_cnt}.jsonl` (dots grid = items=tok). sumprod peaks
  **+37 @150-200 tok then DECLINES** (operate near the peak); use `correct_robust` for sumprod.
- **Position/confound study** = `--reveal-mode sweep_positions --ks 2 4 8 --sizes 0 100`:
  `kq_pos_{sumprod,d6,d10,d12}.jsonl` (after) + `kq_pos_sumprod_before.jsonl` / `kq_pos_d6_before.jsonl`
  (reveal_before contrast; d6 contrast +32.8pp k=8 confirms directedness generalizes off sumprod).
  Sub-n_lo points `kq_instr_lown.jsonl` (sumprod DIP at ~6 tok → n_lo≈12). Inspection:
  `results/inspect_position.md`. Structure-attribution: `structcompare_opus.jsonl` (single vs minimal
  on MATCHED items → in-structure boost only ~+5pp > single-turn, baseline NOT inflated; cancels in k>1).
- **Revealed-item-fixed** = `--design fixed_item [--positions ...]` (`draw_instance_fixed`: same revealed
  item across k at a fixed slot; `inst` indexes the revealed item). Validation files
  `kq_fixed_{opus,ds}_{after,before}.jsonl`.
- **GATE VERDICT (in-structure, sumprod+counting): GO for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2.** At n=200 tok
  Δ par−div +11.8/+22.3 (k4/k8), Δ div−null +25.3/+14.7 — all three hypotheses resolvable for k=4,8.
  k=2 needs a lower operating n=100. The likely outcome is the **B_k≈0 undirected null** (reveal_after
  ≈0 on clean positions; reveal_before clearly + — contrast +18.9pp k=8 Opus, +8.0 DeepSeek).
- **Gotcha:** the naive position-balanced aggregate B_k is **below null** (−6.8pp) because filler ERASES
  the recency advantage of late positions — NOT the science. Position-resolve + use the contrast.
- **`llm_openrouter._is_retryable` now retries `json.JSONDecodeError`** (a provider returning malformed
  JSON crashed a DeepSeek run; fixed so the main experiment won't crash).

## Segment 8 results — DONE (the pre-registered §5 verdict)
- **THE VERDICT lives in `results/seg8_verdict.md`** (+ `seg8_regime_cells.csv`, `seg8_alpha_collapse.md`).
  Scripts: **`seg8_lib.py`** (the §5 decision-rule machinery: `K1Curve` = bootstrapped+interpolated k=1
  reference on additive+logit with genuine SE_R; `cluster_boot_dist`/`paired_diff_dist`/`ci_se` =
  full-distribution bootstraps for any percentile + SE; `boost_records` retains the revealed-item id for
  the two-way bootstrap; `two_way_se` = Cameron–Gelbach–Miller instance+item SE; `classify_regime`/
  `classify_contrast`/`delta_logit`/`logit_boost_dist`/`logit_contrast_dist`), **`analyze_seg8_verdict.py`**
  (drives it → the 3 result files; verdict.md ends with a §Supplementary block: fixed-item corr(B_k,B₁)≈0,
  the matched-n/k k-gradient, the early-pool definition sweep — the within-scope mechanism diagnostics on the
  +2.5), **`plot_seg8.py`** (→ `plots/seg8_{b1_references,regime_verdict,position_bk,contrast,collapse,
  fraction_retained}.png`). All analysis-only; NO API calls; `--assert-cached` reproduces seg7 at $0. The
  descriptive (Seg-7 `main_run_summary.md`) and verdict (Seg-8) CIs use different bootstrap resample counts,
  so last-digit drift between them is expected (same loaders/scoring/seed-0).
- **HEADLINE:** sumprod k=8 n=200 reveal-after early-pool `B_k` is NEAR-NULL = **PARTIAL/sub-divided +2.5
  [+1.2,+3.7]** on the pooled early-average, **NULL on Q1-only (+1.2) & held-out fresh-only (+0.9)** →
  qualifiers _position-sensitive_ + _not robust to held-out_; ~10σ below divided (SE_R-inclusive). **contrast
  +16.2 DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** (ctrl +18.7); d6 k=8 cross-family **paired-logit contrast +1.50** (additive
  +31.9 is headroom-/recency-inflated). Combined = "partial directed" (directed ≫ undirected; undirected ≪
  divided). α NOT estimated (gate fails). The +2.5 mechanism is UNRESOLVED (corr≈0 vs k-gradient, both
  underpowered) — a Segment-11 attention deep-dive.
- **Gotcha:** `analyze_seg8_verdict.py` takes ~2.5 min (4000-resample bootstraps × many cells × the
  interpolated-reference bootstrap). `plot_seg8.py` uses nb=2000 for speed (~45s). The K1Curve token x-grid

  "doesn't bank undirected"), the directedness contrast WEAKENS-but-SURVIVES (early-pool), and "no parallel

- **Selected open model = DeepSeek-V3-0324** (`deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`). Genuine no-CoT: prefill
  (final `{"role":"assistant","content":"<filler> The answer is"}`) is honored; reasoning OFF via
  `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":False}}`. Verified clean (0.03% text-before-answer, 0% reasoning
  field, 0.01% truncation on the good provider cluster). Fast/cheap/reliable.
- **⚠ PIN THE PROVIDER (most important gotcha).** OpenRouter routes DeepSeek-V3-0324 across providers at
  DIFFERENT quantizations: GOOD cluster {DeepInfra, ModelRun, SiliconFlow} ≈ 54–56% acc, BAD cluster
  {AtlasCloud, Novita, GMICloud} ≈ 22–29% (and the bad ones produce CoT/truncation junk). Unpinned, the
  provider mix is size-dependent and **inflated the boost ~2×** (and inflated truncation). ALWAYS run with
  `run_openrouter.py --only-providers deepinfra modelrun siliconflow` (allowlist a homogeneous-accuracy
  cluster, load-balanced) or a single `--provider DeepInfra`. Headline files are `orboost_GC_deepseek_*`
  (the `orboost_FINAL_*`/`orboost_deepseek_*` files are the SUPERSEDED unpinned/inflated runs).
